7D18 DEFB $81,$81,$81,$81,$81,$81,$81,$81
7D20 DEFB $08,$81,$81,$81,$81,$81,$81,$20
7D28 DEFB $30,$80,$85,$80,$80,$80,$40,$80
7D30 DEFB $40,$81,$08,$C3,$81,$C3,$81,$C3
7D38 DEFB $81,$81,$81,$81,$81,$81,$81,$81
7D40 DEFB $81,$81,$81,$81,$85,$80,$85,$50
7D48 DEFB $85,$80,$82,$80,$81,$08,$81,$81
7D50 DEFB $81,$81,$81,$81,$85,$40,$81,$81
7D58 DEFB $08,$85,$81,$81,$08,$40,$81,$50
7D60 DEFB $83,$83,$82,$83,$83,$83,$83,$81
7D68 DEFB $08,$81,$81,$81,$08,$81,$82,$81
7D70 DEFB $81,$81,$81,$81,$81,$81,$81,$81

7DA8 DEFB $08,$81,$08,$81,$81,$81,$81,$81

7DC0 DEFB $08,$81,$82,$81,$81,$81,$81,$81

7DD0 DEFB $C3,$C3,$C3,$81,$81,$81,$81,$81
7DD8 DEFB $81,$81,$85,$85,$81,$81,$81,$81
7DE0 DEFB $81,$91,$81,$81,$91,$81,$81,$85

7E00 DEFB $81,$81,$81,$81,$81,$08,$81,$81
7E08 DEFB $85,$82,$82,$60,$81,$85,$60,$08
7E10 DEFB $81,$85,$60,$08,$08,$81,$81,$91
7E18 DEFB $91,$81,$91,$91,$81,$81,$81,$81
7E20 DEFB $81,$81,$81,$81,$85,$40,$60,$81
7E28 DEFB $81,$08,$81,$81,$81,$85,$81,$81
7E30 DEFB $81,$81,$81,$81,$81,$70,$08,$81
7E38 DEFB $81,$81,$81,$81,$81,$81,$81,$85

7E48 DEFB $85,$81,$81,$81,$81,$08,$20,$91

7E58 DEFB $81,$91,$08,$81,$81,$81,$81,$81

7E68 DEFB $81,$81,$81,$20,$20,$08,$C3,$C3
7E70 DEFB $C3,$81,$81,$81,$81,$81,$81,$81
7E78 DEFB $81,$81,$40,$85,$85,$40,$40,$40
7E80 DEFB $81,$81,$81,$81,$81,$81,$82,$81

7E90 DEFB $81,$81,$08,$91,$81,$81,$81,$83
7E98 DEFB $83,$83,$83,$81,$81,$83,$81,$81
7EA0 DEFB $08,$83,$83,$81,$83,$83,$81,$83
7EA8 DEFB $83,$81,$81,$83,$81,$81,$81,$81
7EB0 DEFB $85,$40,$40,$81,$81,$81,$81,$40
7EB8 DEFB $85,$85,$81,$81,$40,$81,$08,$82

7EC8 DEFB $81,$81,$83,$83,$83,$83,$85,$83
7ED0 DEFB $81,$83,$83,$83,$91,$83,$81,$83
7ED8 DEFB $83,$91,$91,$91,$91,$81,$81,$81
7EE0 DEFB $81,$81,$81,$81,$81,$81,$83,$83
7EE8 DEFB $10,$81,$83,$83,$83,$83,$08,$81
7EF0 DEFB $81,$91,$81,$81,$10,$81,$91,$91
7EF8 DEFB $81,$81,$81,$81,$81,$81,$0C,$85
7F00 DEFB $70,$81,$81,$81,$81,$81,$81,$81
7F08 DEFB $81,$81,$81,$81,$08,$81,$81,$81
7F10 DEFB $85,$81,$82,$81,$82,$81,$81,$81
7F18 DEFB $60,$08,$81,$81,$81,$81,$08,$81
7F20 DEFB $81,$81,$85,$91,$81,$40,$81,$83
7F28 DEFB $81,$83,$82,$83,$81,$83,$81,$81
7F30 DEFB $83,$81,$83,$81,$40,$81,$81,$82

7F40 DEFB $08,$83,$83,$83,$83,$83,$C3,$83

7F50 DEFB $83,$83,$83,$83,$83,$83,$C3,$83
7F58 DEFB $83,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00
7F60 DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00
7F68 DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00
7F70 DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00
7F78 DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00
7F80 DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00
7F88 DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00
7F90 DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00
7F98 DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00
7FA0 DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00
7FA8 DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00
7FB0 DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00
7FB8 DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00
7FC0 DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00
7FC8 DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00
7FD0 DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00
7FD8 DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00
7FE0 DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00
7FE8 DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00
7FF0 DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00
7FF8 DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00
8000: Game entry
Cold-start, reached from the resident loader.
The loader JP's here once the tape image is fully in RAM.
In: nothing meaningful (IFF1=0, IM 1, SP still loader's).
Out: never returns -- falls through into the init chain and then the main game loop.
Side effects: installs private stack, blacks the border, runs three init subroutines, seeds game-state RAM.
game_entry 8000 JP entry_setup alignment trampoline -- skip 3 pad bytes (8003-8005)
8003 NOP
freeze_ctr 8004 NOP
pad_var_8005 8005 NOP
entry_setup 8006 LD SP,$5FFE install game's private stack just under screen RAM
8009 XOR A A = 0
800A OUT ($FE),A ULA port FE: border=black(0), MIC/beeper off
800C CALL setup_im2 Setup IM2
800F CALL jp_clear_screen init #2 = clear screen + game RAM (jp_clear_screen trampoline)
8012 CALL jp_setup_attrs init #3 = paint playfield attributes (jp_setup_attrs trampoline)
8015 LD A,$01 A = 1
8017 LD ($603F),A first persistent-state write: running_flag = 1
801A: New game
Set up a fresh game/level and run it.
Falls in from game_entry's init chain.
Seeds state vars, builds the level, draws the screen, then enters main_loop.
On death/level-end it loops back to level_retry or game-over handling.
new_game 801A LD A,$80 A=$80 -> $6056 (start flag/counter)
801C LD ($6056),A
801F XOR A
8020 LD ($60D7),A set Control-key toggle flag
8023 LD ($8005),A
8026 LD A,$04 4 attempts/lives -> game_mode
8028 LD ($6017),A set Game mode
802B: Level entry
Used by the routine at poll_controls.
level_retry 802B XOR A
802C LD ($60D8),A set Demo/pause mode flag
802F CALL title_menu Title menu
This entry point is used by the routine at poll_controls.
level_retry_0 8032 CALL init_col_state Init col state
8035 CALL jp_reset_score proc_table slot: build tower / level layout
8038 LD A,$01
803A LD ($6040),A set Lives
This entry point is used by the routine at main_loop.
level_retry_1 803D CALL restore_col_state Restore col state
8040 PUSH AF
8041 CALL jp_init_bonus
8044 POP AF
8045 JR NC,level_retry carry clear -> rebuild (retry)
This entry point is used by the routine at main_loop.
level_retry_2 8047 CALL jp_set_level_timer
804A CALL jp_draw_timer
804D CALL jp_draw_hud
8050 CALL show_level_intro draw the heading/menu text
8053 XOR A
8054 LD ($60C3),A set Player died this frame
8057 DEC A
8058 LD ($6055),A set Level timer prescaler
805B CALL jp_clear_playfield
805E CALL setup_level Setup level
8061 CALL stop_sound Stop sound
8064: Main loop
The in-play frame loop.
Each pass runs main_tick (one frame) then checks end conditions: death_flag (death_flag) set -> life-loss handler ($8084) game_state (game_state) == 2 -> level-complete ($80B2) play_timer (play_timer) == 0 -> time-up ($8081) Otherwise CALL poll_controls (status update) and loop.
main_loop 8064 CALL main_tick run one frame of the game
8067 LD A,($60C3) player died this frame?
806A OR A
806B JR NZ,on_death yes -> life-loss handler
806D LD A,($60D9) level-complete state?
8070 CP $02
8072 JR Z,on_level_done yes -> level-complete handler
8074 LD HL,($6053) play_timer reached 0?
8077 LD A,H
8078 OR L
8079 JR Z,main_loop_0 yes -> time-up
807B CALL poll_controls Poll controls
807E JP main_loop loop
main_loop_0 8081 CALL draw_timeup_screen Draw timeup screen
on_death 8084 LD A,$FF
8086 LD ($6055),A set Level timer prescaler
8089 LD A,($60D7) get Control-key toggle flag
808C OR A
808D JR NZ,main_loop_2
808F LD HL,$6041
8092 LD A,($6040) get Lives
8095 OR A
8096 JR Z,main_loop_1
8098 INC HL
main_loop_1 8099 DEC (HL)
809A PUSH HL
809B CALL jp_draw_hud
809E POP HL
809F LD A,(HL)
80A0 OR A
80A1 JR Z,main_loop_3
main_loop_2 80A3 CALL pack_col_state Pack col state
80A6 JP level_retry_1
main_loop_3 80A9 CALL clear_col_mask Clear col mask
80AC CALL draw_info_screen_a Draw info screen a
80AF JP level_retry_1
on_level_done 80B2 LD A,$FF
80B4 LD ($6055),A set Level timer prescaler
80B7 LD A,($60B9) get Tower rotation position
80BA PUSH AF
80BB LD HL,($60BA)
80BE PUSH HL
80BF LD HL,($6030) get Vertical scroll position
80C2 PUSH HL
80C3 CALL screen_time_bonus Screen time bonus
80C6 CALL jp_clear_playfield
80C9 LD A,$03
80CB LD ($60D9),A set Game state
80CE CALL setup_level Setup level
80D1 POP HL
80D2 LD ($6030),HL set Vertical scroll position
80D5 POP HL
80D6 LD ($60BA),HL
80D9 POP AF
80DA LD ($60B9),A set Tower rotation position
80DD LD A,$04
80DF LD ($60C0),A set Player state-machine state
80E2 LD A,$1D
80E4 LD ($60BF),A set Player state-machine phase/height window
80E7 XOR A
80E8 LD ($60C2),A
main_loop_4 80EB CALL main_tick Main tick
80EE CALL poll_controls Poll controls
80F1 LD A,($60D9) get Game state
80F4 CP $04
80F6 JR NZ,main_loop_4
80F8 XOR A
80F9 LD ($60D9),A set Game state
80FC CALL advance_col_counter Advance col counter
80FF CP $08
8101 JR NZ,main_loop_5
8103 CALL clear_col_mask Clear col mask
8106 CALL draw_info_screen_b Draw info screen b
8109 JP level_retry_1
main_loop_5 810C CALL pack_col_state Pack col state
810F CALL jp_init_bonus
8112 JP level_retry_2
8115: Poll controls
Used by the routine at main_loop.
Per-frame keyboard handling (called from main_loop).
Skipped while input is frozen (freeze_ctr).
- restart key (via $A70F): NC -> jump to level_retry
- several control keys toggle ctrl_flag (ctrl_flag) / pad_var_8005
- PAUSE/DEMO: key $21 enters demo_pause any joystick X exits
- menu select: tests a column of 8 keys the index of the one pressed (0..7) -> pad_var_8005, then restarts setup at $8032.
Uses test_key ($A706) for t
poll_controls 8115 LD A,($8004) A = freeze_ctr
8118 OR A
8119 RET NZ frozen -> do nothing this frame
811A CALL jp_test_break
811D JP NC,level_retry restart condition -> rebuild level
8120 LD A,$27
8122 CALL jp_test_key
8125 JR Z,poll_controls_0
8127 LD A,$08
8129 CALL jp_test_key
812C JR Z,poll_controls_0
812E LD A,$15
8130 CALL jp_test_key
8133 JR Z,poll_controls_0
8135 LD A,$01
8137 LD ($60D7),A set ctrl_flag (ctrl_flag) = 1
poll_controls_0 813A LD A,$20
813C CALL jp_test_key
813F JR Z,poll_controls_1
8141 XOR A
8142 LD ($60D7),A set Control-key toggle flag
8145 LD ($8005),A
poll_controls_1 8148 LD A,($60D8) A = demo_pause
814B OR A
814C JR NZ,poll_controls_2
814E LD A,($6055) get Level timer prescaler
8151 OR A
8152 JR NZ,poll_controls_4
8154 LD A,$21
8156 CALL jp_test_key
8159 JR Z,poll_controls_4
815B LD A,$FF enter demo/pause mode (demo_pause = $FF)
815D LD ($60D8),A set Demo/pause mode flag
8160 LD ($6055),A set Level timer prescaler
8163 JR poll_controls_4
poll_controls_2 8165 CALL proc_table Proc table
8168 LD A,($601C) get Signed input
816B OR A
816C JR Z,poll_controls_4 no X movement -> stay in demo
816E XOR A
816F LD ($60D8),A movement -> exit demo
8172 LD A,($60D7) get Control-key toggle flag
8175 OR A
8176 JP M,poll_controls_3
8179 LD A,$01
poll_controls_3 817B LD ($6055),A set Level timer prescaler
poll_controls_4 817E LD A,($60D7) get Control-key toggle flag
8181 OR A
8182 RET Z
8183 LD A,$27
8185 CALL jp_test_key
8188 RET Z
8189 LD B,$00 --- menu key-column scan: B = option index ---
818B LD A,$24
818D CALL jp_test_key
8190 JR NZ,poll_controls_5
8192 INC B
8193 LD A,$1C
8195 CALL jp_test_key test each option key
8198 JR NZ,poll_controls_5
819A INC B
819B LD A,$14
819D CALL jp_test_key
81A0 JR NZ,poll_controls_5
81A2 INC B
81A3 LD A,$0C
81A5 CALL jp_test_key
81A8 JR NZ,poll_controls_5
81AA INC B
81AB LD A,$04
81AD CALL jp_test_key
81B0 JR NZ,poll_controls_5
81B2 INC B
81B3 LD A,$03
81B5 CALL jp_test_key
81B8 JR NZ,poll_controls_5
81BA INC B
81BB LD A,$0B
81BD CALL jp_test_key
81C0 JR NZ,poll_controls_5
81C2 INC B
81C3 LD A,$13
81C5 CALL jp_test_key
81C8 JR NZ,poll_controls_5
81CA RET none pressed -> return
poll_controls_5 81CB LD A,B a key matched: B = selected option
81CC LD ($8005),A store selection -> pad_var_8005
81CF POP HL
81D0 XOR A
81D1 LD ($60D8),A set Demo/pause mode flag
81D4 JP level_retry_0 restart game setup with the new selection
81D7: Setup level
Used by the routines at level_retry and main_loop.
(Re)build the current level's working state.
Zeroes the work_buffers block (work_buffers.., $0FFF bytes via LDIR), loads the level's column data (load_level_col), resets the tower position (init_tower_pos), re-inits flags (init_b5cf) and the renderers (clear_actor_states, reset_actor_phase).
Clobbers: most.
setup_level 81D7 LD HL,$F000 Clear work_buffers (work_buffers) for tower map
81DA LD DE,$F001
81DD LD BC,$0FFF
81E0 LD (HL),$00
81E2 LDIR
81E4 CALL jp_load_level_col Load level column data into work_buffers
81E7 CALL init_tower_pos Init tower pos
81EA CALL jp_init_b5cf
81ED CALL clear_actor_states Clear actor states
81F0 CALL reset_actor_phase
81F3 LD B,$04 Initialise 4 object slots to empty
81F5 LD HL,$60A9
setup_level_0 81F8 LD (HL),$FF Fill slots with $FF (empty)
81FA INC HL
81FB DJNZ setup_level_0
81FD LD A,($60D9) Check if game_state == 3 (level transition)
8200 CP $03
8202 JR Z,setup_level_1 Skip attribute painting if transitioning
8204 CALL jp_paint_attrs Paint tower colour attributes
8207 LD A,$01
8209 LD ($60C9),A Set $60C9 = 1 (enable column visibility update)
820C LD A,$24 Compute disp_height = 36 - tower_height
820E LD HL,$60B9
8211 SUB (HL)
8212 AND $7F
8214 LD ($6032),A set Display height
8217 CALL jp_col_visible Mark visible tower columns for rendering
821A CALL init_attract_actors Init attract actors
821D LD HL,$60C9
8220 DEC (HL)
8221 XOR A
8222 LD ($60D9),A Set game_state = 0 (normal play)
8225 LD A,$7D
8227 LD ($60A3),A
822A LD A,$01 Set player starting column = 1
822C LD ($60A4),A set Player's current column
822F LD A,($60D7) get Control-key toggle flag
8232 JP setup_level_2
setup_level_1 8235 LD A,$FF Set 6055 = $FF during level transition
setup_level_2 8237 LD ($6055),A Store $FF or $00 into timer prescaler 6055
823A LD A,$02 Freeze input for 2 frames
823C LD ($8004),A
823F RET
8240: Main tick
Used by the routine at main_loop.
One frame of the game.
Called from main_loop.
Order: read input (unless frozen) -> run game-state machine (player move, tower rotation, enemies) -> redraw widgets -> HALT to sync to the 50Hz frame interrupt.
State: game_state (game_state): 3=level transition, 4=finished,
else = normal play.
demo_pause (demo_pause) skips logic.
freeze_ctr ($8004) freezes input for N frames.
Clobbers: most regs.
Returns once per frame.
main_tick 8240 LD HL,$8004 HL -> freeze_ctr (in code padding at $8004)
8243 LD A,(HL) A = freeze counter
8244 OR A
8245 JR Z,main_tick_0 0 = not frozen -> read input normally
8247 DEC (HL) frozen: count down one frame
8248 XOR A
8249 LD ($601D),A zero the three input vars while frozen
824C LD ($601E),A set Signed input
824F LD ($601C),A set Signed input
8252 JR main_tick_1
main_tick_0 8254 CALL proc_table Proc table
main_tick_1 8257 LD A,($60D8) A = demo_pause flag
825A OR A
825B JR NZ,main_tick_3 set -> skip all game logic (attract/demo), just redraw
825D LD A,($60D9) A = game_state
8260 CP $03 state 3 = level transition?
8262 JR NZ,main_tick_2
8264 CALL player_tick Player tick
8267 LD A,($60D9) get Game state
826A CP $04
826C RET Z state 4 = finished -> done this frame
826D JP main_tick_3
main_tick_2 8270 CALL jp_update_objects --- normal play: update one frame ---
8273 CALL update_actor_pos Update actor pos
8276 CALL player_tick Player tick
8279 LD A,($60A4) A = player column before move
827C PUSH AF
827D CALL jp_update_actors move player (proc_table slot)
8280 POP AF
8281 LD B,A
8282 LD A,($60A4) compare new vs old player column
8285 CP B
8286 LD A,$00
8288 CALL NZ,play_sound Play sound
828B CALL update_enemies Update enemies
828E CALL check_collision Check collision
main_tick_3 8291 LD A,$24 --- redraw HUD: A = $24 (36 rows) ---
8293 LD HL,$60B9
8296 SUB (HL) subtract tower_height (tower_height)
8297 AND $7F
8299 LD ($6032),A store display height (disp_height)
829C CALL jp_col_visible draw widgets (proc_table slots)
829F CALL jp_anim_bg
82A2 CALL jp_setup_scroll
82A5 CALL jp_code_ad00
82A8 PUSH AF
82A9 CALL draw_player Draw player
82AC HALT HALT: wait for the 50Hz frame IRQ (Z80
82AD CALL sound_tick Sound tick
82B0 CALL jp_draw_tower
82B3 CALL jp_draw_pending
82B6 CALL draw_actors_sorted Draw actors sorted
82B9 CALL jp_select_sprite
82BC CALL jp_draw_strip
82BF POP AF
82C0 CALL jp_setup_strip
82C3 CALL jp_blit_scroll_wrap
82C6 CALL jp_scroll_attrs
82C9 LD A,($60D9) get Game state
82CC CP $03
82CE JR Z,main_tick_4
82D0 CALL tally_score_to Tally score to
82D3 CALL jp_draw_hud
82D6 CALL jp_tick_timer
main_tick_4 82D9 RET
82DA: Tally score to
Used by the routine at main_tick.
Count $60BC up toward $60BA, awarding $10 (BCD) points per step via add_bcd_score, until the two 16-bit values meet.
Used to animate an end-of-section bonus tally.
Clobbers: A,DE,HL.
tally_score_to 82DA LD HL,($60BC)
82DD LD DE,($60BA)
82E1 OR A
82E2 SBC HL,DE
82E4 RET NC
tally_score_to_0 82E5 LD HL,($60BC)
82E8 LD DE,($60BA)
82EC LD A,L
82ED CP E
82EE JR NZ,tally_score_to_1
82F0 LD A,H
82F1 CP D
82F2 RET Z
tally_score_to_1 82F3 PUSH HL
82F4 LD HL,$0010
82F7 CALL jp_add_score
82FA POP HL
82FB INC HL
82FC LD ($60BC),HL
82FF JR tally_score_to_0
8301: Unused
col_state_words 8301 DEFS $04
8305: Init col state
Used by the routine at level_retry.
Seed BOTH players' saved column-mask words.
HL = (pad_var_8005) [low=0 at level start], then H forced to $FF (all 8 columns present) the same $FF00|sel is written to the P1 (col_state_words) and P2 ($8303) slots.
Called once per level build.
In: pad_var_8005.
Out: (col_state_words),($8303).
Clobbers: A,HL.
init_col_state 8305 LD HL,($8005)
8308 LD H,$FF
830A LD ($8301),HL
830D LD ($8303),HL
8310 RET
8311: Restore col state
Used by the routine at level_retry.
Expand a player's saved column bitmask into the 8-byte col_flags[] array (col_flags) and mirror its counter into 6039.
lives_p1 (lives_p1) selects the slot (0=P1 word col_state_words, else P2 word $8303) -- here lives_p1 doubles as an ACTIVE-PLAYER INDEX, not a life count.
running_flag gates an alternate (re)init path.
Unpacks via RR through col_flags[] returns CY=1 on success.
Out: col_flags[0..7], 6039, CY.
Clobbers: A,B,C,H
restore_col_state 8311 LD A,($603F)
8314 CP $01
8316 LD A,$00
8318 LD HL,$6040
831B JR Z,restore_col_state_0
831D INC A
831E SUB (HL)
restore_col_state_0 831F LD (HL),A
8320 LD HL,$8302
8323 OR A
8324 JR Z,restore_col_state_1
8326 INC HL
8327 INC HL
restore_col_state_1 8328 LD A,(HL)
8329 OR A
832A JR NZ,restore_col_state_3
832C LD A,($603F) get Running flag
832F CP $01
8331 JR Z,restore_col_state_6
8333 LD A,$01
8335 LD HL,$6040
8338 SUB (HL)
8339 LD (HL),A
833A LD HL,$8302
833D OR A
833E JR Z,restore_col_state_2
8340 INC HL
8341 INC HL
restore_col_state_2 8342 LD A,(HL)
8343 OR A
8344 JR Z,restore_col_state_6
restore_col_state_3 8346 DEC HL
8347 LD A,(HL)
8348 LD ($6039),A set Level number 0
834B INC HL
834C LD C,(HL)
834D LD HL,$60CB
8350 LD B,$08
restore_col_state_4 8352 XOR A
8353 RR C
8355 JR NC,restore_col_state_5
8357 INC A
restore_col_state_5 8358 LD (HL),A
8359 INC HL
835A DJNZ restore_col_state_4
835C SCF
835D RET
restore_col_state_6 835E OR A
835F RET
8360: Pack col state
Used by the routine at main_loop.
Inverse of restore_col_state.
Writes the live counter (6039) into the selected player's low byte, then packs the 8 col_flags[] LSBs (col_flags..$60D2) back into the high mask byte.
lives_p1 selects the P1/P2 slot.
Clobbers: A,B,C,HL.
pack_col_state 8360 LD HL,$8301
8363 LD A,($6040) get Lives
8366 OR A
8367 JR Z,pack_col_state_0
8369 INC HL
836A INC HL
pack_col_state_0 836B LD A,($6039) get Level number 0
836E LD (HL),A
836F INC HL
8370 PUSH HL
8371 LD B,$08
8373 XOR A
8374 LD HL,$60CB
pack_col_state_1 8377 LD C,(HL)
8378 RR C
837A RR A
837C INC HL
837D DJNZ pack_col_state_1
837F POP HL
8380 LD (HL),A
8381 RET
8382: Clear col mask
Used by the routine at main_loop.
Zero the selected player's column mask byte (high byte of col_state_words/$8303).
lives_p1 selects the slot.
clear_col_mask 8382 LD HL,$8302
8385 LD A,($6040) get Lives
8388 OR A
8389 JR Z,clear_col_mask_0
838B INC HL
838C INC HL
clear_col_mask_0 838D LD (HL),$00
838F RET
8390: Advance col counter
Used by the routine at main_loop.
Mark current column done: clear col_flags[6039], then bump the selected player's saved counter (low byte) and mirror it back to 6039.
advance_col_counter 8390 LD A,($6039)
8393 LD E,A
8394 LD D,$00
8396 LD HL,$60CB
8399 ADD HL,DE
839A LD (HL),$00
839C LD HL,$8301
839F LD A,($6040) get Lives
83A2 OR A
83A3 JR Z,advance_col_counter_0
83A5 INC HL
83A6 INC HL
advance_col_counter_0 83A7 INC (HL)
83A8 LD A,(HL)
83A9 LD ($6039),A set Level number 0
83AC RET
83AD: Setup IM2
Used by the routine at game_entry.
Install the Z80 mode-2 interrupt vector.
Builds a 257-byte page of identical bytes at $BB00..runtime_tables so that, whatever the data-bus byte is at IRQ time, the Z80 reads the 16-bit vector im2_dispatch from table[$FF]/table[$100].
At im2_dispatch it plants 'JP irq_handler' -- the actual interrupt service routine.
In: nothing.
Out: IM 2 active, IFF1=1 (interrupts enabled).
Clobbers: A, B, HL, I.
The porter must emulate this
setup_im2 83AD DI no IRQs while we build the vector table
83AE LD HL,$BB00 HL -> vector-table page base $BB00
83B1 LD A,H A = $BB = high byte of the table page
83B2 LD I,A I = $BB: IM2 reads vectors from page I (Z80-only)
83B4 DEC A A = $BA = the byte we flood the table with
83B5 LD B,$00 B = 0 -> DJNZ loops 256 times (Z80 DJNZ: dec B, jump nz)
setup_im2_0 83B7 LD (HL),A table[i] = $BA
83B8 INC HL next table slot
83B9 DJNZ setup_im2_0 fill all 256 bytes of $BB00..$BBFF
83BB LD (HL),A +1 byte at runtime_tables = $BA: covers the table[$100] off-by-one
83BC LD H,A H = $BA
83BD LD L,A L = $BA -> HL = im2_dispatch (the vector the IRQ will fetch)
83BE LD (HL),$C3 plant C3 = JP opcode at im2_dispatch
83C0 INC L
83C1 LD (HL),$CA low byte of JP target -> $CA
83C3 INC L
83C4 LD (HL),$83 high byte -> $83, so vector = 'JP irq_handler' = irq_handler
83C6 IM 2 switch CPU to interrupt mode 2 (Z80-only)
83C8 EI interrupts on
83C9: IM2 noop return
Used by the routine at irq_handler.
im2_noop_ret 83C9 RET return to game_entry
83CA: IRQ handler
Used by the routine at im2_dispatch.
IM 2 interrupt service routine (50 Hz vblank).
Reached via the planted vector at im2_dispatch -> JP here.
It is a deliberate NEAR-NO-OP: 'CALL im2_noop_ret' targets the bare RET at the end of setup_im2, so the call does nothing.
The point is to REPLACE the 48K ROM's own interrupt routine (which scans the keyboard and bumps the FRAMES sysvar) with a handler that has no side effects, while still letting interrupts fire.
Verified: no code anywhere
irq_handler 83CA CALL im2_noop_ret CALL im2_noop_ret = the RET in setup_im2: a no-op (neutralized ISR)
83CD EI re-enable interrupts
83CE RETI RETI: return from maskable interrupt (Z80-only
83D0: Init tower pos
Used by the routine at setup_level.
Reset the tower/scroll position for a new level: tower_height=$04, $60BA/$60BC (current & target column)=8,
scroll_pos = 8-$28, and clear the high bytes $60BB/$60BD.
Clobbers: A.
init_tower_pos 83D0 LD A,$04
83D2 LD ($60B9),A set Tower rotation position
83D5 LD A,$08
83D7 LD ($60BA),A
83DA LD ($60BC),A
83DD SUB $28
83DF LD ($6030),A set Vertical scroll position
83E2 LD A,$00
83E4 LD ($60BB),A
83E7 LD ($60BD),A
83EA SBC A,$00
83EC AND $03
83EE LD ($6031),A
83F1 XOR A
83F2 LD ($6037),A set Unknown byte
83F5 LD ($60BF),A set Player state-machine phase/height window
83F8 LD ($60C3),A set Player died this frame
83FB LD ($6020),A set Debounce latch
83FE LD ($60C5),A
8401 LD A,$00
8403 LD ($60C0),A set Player state-machine state
8406 LD A,$80
8408 LD ($60BE),A
840B LD A,$01
840D LD ($60C2),A
8410 RET
8411: Rotate tower
Used by the routines at rotate_if_idle, pstate_play, pstate_2, pstate_1 and pstate_4.
Rotate the tower by signed B: tower_height = (tower_height + B) AND $7F (128-column wrap), saving the old value in 6033.
Tests the new position for a wall/collision (check_rotate_collision) on collision (NC) it reverts tower_height.
In: B=delta.
Out: CY=moved.
Clobbers: A,B,HL.
rotate_tower 8411 LD B,A
8412 LD A,($60B9) get Tower rotation position
8415 LD ($6033),A set Previous tower_height
8418 ADD A,B
8419 AND $7F
841B LD ($60B9),A set Tower rotation position
841E LD HL,($60BA)
8421 CALL check_rotate_collision Check rotate collision
8424 JR NC,rotate_tower_0
8426 LD A,($6033) get Previous tower_height
8429 LD ($60B9),A set Tower rotation position
842C SCF
842D RET
rotate_tower_0 842E OR A
842F RET
8430: Step scroll
Used by the routines at pstate_2, pstate_1 and 879B.
Advance the column/scroll position $60BA by signed A (sign-extended via B), folding H into the 4-quadrant range (AND $03) saves the previous position in 6034.
Clobbers: A,B,HL.
step_scroll 8430 LD HL,($60BA)
8433 LD ($6034),HL set Previous scroll_pos
8436 LD B,$00
8438 OR A
8439 JP P,step_scroll_0
843C DEC B
step_scroll_0 843D ADD A,L
843E LD L,A
843F LD A,B
8440 ADC A,H
8441 JP M,step_scroll_2
8444 AND $03
8446 LD H,A
8447 LD ($60BA),HL
844A LD A,($60B9) get Tower rotation position
844D CALL check_rotate_collision Check rotate collision
8450 JR NC,step_scroll_1
8452 LD HL,($6034) get Previous scroll_pos
8455 LD ($60BA),HL
8458 SCF
8459 RET
step_scroll_1 845A OR A
845B RET
step_scroll_2 845C POP HL
845D JP death_handler
8460: Rotate if idle
Used by the routines at pstate_play and pstate_5.
If check_step_cell returns a pending step (CY) and there is no vertical input (input_y==0), rotate the tower by it (tail-calls rotate_tower).
Clobbers: A,B.
rotate_if_idle 8460 CALL check_step_cell
8463 RET NC
8464 LD B,A
8465 LD A,($601D) get Signed input
8468 OR A
8469 RET NZ
846A LD A,B
846B JP rotate_tower
846E: Data block at 846E
player_state_table 846E DEFB $B9,$84,$8D,$86,$DB,$85,$63,$85
8476 DEFB $36,$87,$54,$88,$BD,$88,$71,$8A
847E DEFB $0E,$8B,$1D,$A1
8482: Player tick
Used by the routine at main_tick.
Per-frame player state machine, run every frame from main_tick (both in normal play AND on the game_state==3 level-transition path TASK.md called this 'the level-transition path' -- it is really the general player update, the transition is just one caller).
Preamble debounces directional input: input_latch (input_latch) records whether input_x is active so a press fires once a held input is consumed (input_x cleared).
Tail reads player_state and dispatches through a 2-byte JP-address table at player_state_table (index = state*2) to the pstate_N handler below.
player_tick 8482 LD HL,$6020
8485 LD A,(HL)
8486 OR A
8487 JR NZ,player_tick_0
8489 LD A,($601C) get Signed input
848C OR A
848D JR Z,player_tick_2
848F LD (HL),A
8490 JR player_tick_2
player_tick_0 8492 LD A,($601C) get Signed input
8495 OR A
8496 JR NZ,player_tick_1
8498 LD (HL),A
8499 JR player_tick_2
player_tick_1 849B XOR A
849C LD ($601C),A set Signed input (debounce: force input_x=0 this frame)
player_tick_2 849F LD A,($60C0) get Player state-machine state
84A2 ADD A,A index player_state*2 into jump table player_state_table and dispatch (JP (HL))
84A3 LD E,A
84A4 LD D,$00
84A6 LD HL,$846E
84A9 ADD HL,DE
84AA LD E,(HL)
84AB INC HL
84AC LD D,(HL)
84AD EX DE,HL
84AE JP (HL)
84AF: Pstate reset
Used by the routines at pstate_3, pstate_2, pstate_1, 879B, pstate_4_land_tail, pstate_5 and pstate_6.
Common "return to normal/play" exit: player_state=0, pstate_phase=0.
Reached by tail-jump from most other pstate_N handlers when their animation/action completes.
pstate_reset 84AF LD A,$00
84B1 LD ($60C0),A set Player state-machine state
84B4 XOR A
84B5 LD ($60BF),A set Player state-machine phase/height window
84B8 RET
84B9: Pstate play
State 0: standing/walking, the default state.
Reads the cell under the player (READ_PLAYER_CELL) and dispatches on its type (0=normal falls through, 1->pstate_play_cell1, 2->pstate_play_cell2, else->pstate_play_cell_other).
Normal cell: ROTATE_IF_IDLE, then vertical input picks a climb direction (tests $60BE facing sign) and enters pstate_3 (climb) at $8559.
No vertical input: horizontal input nonzero -> JP code_8656 (enter pstate_1, climb a column) else ROTATE_TOWER with vertical input, carry (blocked) -> JP code_8673 (alt pstate_1 entry).
No input at all: CHECK_CONVEYOR / TEST_CELL_SPECIAL under fire button -> may set game_state=1 and JP pstate4_entry (enter pstate_4, jump/fall) else just advances the idle-walk animation phase (0..7, sound 2 every 4th step).
pstate_play 84B9 CALL consume_cell
84BC CALL read_player_cell Read player cell
84BF OR A
84C0 JR Z,pstate_play_0
84C2 CP $01
84C4 JP Z,pstate_play_cell1
84C7 CP $02
84C9 JP Z,pstate_play_cell2
84CC JP pstate_play_cell_other
pstate_play_0 84CF CALL rotate_if_idle Rotate if idle
84D2 LD A,($601D) get Signed input
84D5 OR A
84D6 JR Z,pstate_play_3
84D8 JP M,pstate_play_1
84DB LD A,($60BE)
84DE OR A
84DF JP P,pstate_play_8
84E2 JR pstate_play_2
pstate_play_1 84E4 LD A,($60BE)
84E7 OR A
84E8 JP M,pstate_play_8
pstate_play_2 84EB LD A,($601C) get Signed input
84EE OR A
84EF JP NZ,code_8656
84F2 LD A,($601D) get Signed input
84F5 CALL rotate_tower Rotate tower
84F8 JP C,code_8673
84FB LD A,($60BF) get Player state-machine phase/height window
84FE INC A
84FF AND $07
8501 LD ($60BF),A set Player state-machine phase/height window
8504 LD HL,$60BE
8507 ADD A,(HL)
8508 ADD A,$00
850A LD ($60C1),A
850D LD A,($60BF) get Player state-machine phase/height window
8510 AND $03
8512 RET NZ
8513 LD A,$02
8515 JP play_sound
pstate_play_3 8518 LD A,($601C) get Signed input
851B OR A
851C JP NZ,pstate_4_land_tail
851F LD A,($601E) get Signed input
8522 OR A
8523 JR Z,pstate_play_7
8525 CALL check_conveyor Check conveyor
8528 JR NC,pstate_play_5
852A OR A
852B JR Z,pstate_play_4
852D LD HL,$601E
8530 CP (HL)
8531 JR NZ,pstate_play_5
pstate_play_4 8533 LD A,($601E) get Signed input
8536 JP pstate5_exit
pstate_play_5 8539 LD A,($601E) get Signed input
853C CP $01
853E JR NZ,pstate_play_7
8540 CALL test_cell_special Test cell special
8543 JR NC,pstate_play_7
8545 OR A
8546 JR Z,pstate_play_6
8548 LD A,$01
854A LD ($60D9),A set Game state
pstate_play_6 854D JP pstate4_entry
pstate_play_7 8550 LD A,($60BE)
8553 OR $0C
8555 LD ($60C1),A
8558 RET
pstate_play_8 8559 LD A,$03
855B LD ($60C0),A set Player state-machine state
855E XOR A
855F LD ($60BF),A set Player state-machine phase/height window
8562 RET
8563: Pstate 3
State 3: vertical climb animation (entered from pstate_play at $8559).
Picks a sprite frame from table 86F2 indexed by phase (mirrored via +7 when $60BE facing is negative), increments phase each call.
At phase==7 the climb cycle ends: flips the facing bit ($60BE ^= $80) and returns to pstate_reset (state 0).
pstate_3 8563 LD A,($60BE)
8566 OR A
8567 LD A,($60BF) get Player state-machine phase/height window
856A JP P,pstate_3_0
856D ADD A,$07
pstate_3_0 856F LD E,A
8570 LD D,$00
8572 LD HL,$86F2
8575 ADD HL,DE
8576 LD A,(HL)
8577 LD ($60C1),A
857A LD HL,$60BF
857D INC (HL)
857E LD A,(HL)
857F CP $07
8581 RET NZ
8582 LD A,($60BE)
8585 XOR $80
8587 LD ($60BE),A
858A JP pstate_reset
858D: Unused
858D DEFS $03
8590: Pstate play cell1
Used by the routines at pstate_play and code_8a71.
pstate_play continuation for cell type 1 (also re-entered from code_8a71's wall-bump bounce-back path).
pstate_play_cell1 8590 LD A,$00
8592 LD B,$00
8594 JR pstate_play_cell_other_0
8596: Routine at 8596
Used by the routine at pstate_1.
pstate_play_cell1b 8596 LD A,$03
8598 LD ($858D),A
859B LD A,$00
859D LD B,$00
859F JR pstate_play_cell_other_1
85A1: Pstate play cell2
Used by the routine at pstate_play.
pstate_play continuation for cell type 2.
pstate_play_cell2 85A1 LD A,$0F
85A3 LD B,$FF
85A5 LD HL,$60BE
85A8 BIT 7,(HL)
85AA JR NZ,pstate_play_cell_other_0
85AC LD B,$01
85AE JR pstate_play_cell_other_0
85B0: Pstate play cell other
Used by the routine at pstate_play.
pstate_play continuation for any other cell type (not 0/1/2).
pstate_play_cell_other 85B0 LD A,$0E
85B2 LD B,$01
85B4 LD HL,$60BE
85B7 BIT 7,(HL)
85B9 JR NZ,pstate_play_cell_other_0
85BB LD B,$FF
This entry point is used by the routines at pstate_play_cell1 and pstate_play_cell2.
pstate_play_cell_other_0 85BD LD HL,$858D
85C0 LD (HL),$01
This entry point is used by the routine at pstate_play_cell1b.
pstate_play_cell_other_1 85C2 LD HL,$60BE
85C5 ADD A,(HL)
85C6 LD ($60C1),A
85C9 LD A,B
85CA LD ($858E),A
85CD LD A,$02
85CF LD ($60C0),A set Player state-machine state
85D2 XOR A
85D3 LD ($60BF),A set Player state-machine phase/height window
85D6 DEC A
85D7 LD ($858F),A
85DA RET
85DB: Pstate 2
State 2: top-of-climb slide-in, entered from pstate_1 when a column climb finishes normally (phase reaches its $8642 limit).
Nudges tower rotation (ROTATE_TOWER) and scroll position (STEP_SCROLL), plays a sound, then hands off (see C-lines) back to normal play.
pstate_2 85DB LD A,($60BF)
85DE OR A
85DF JR NZ,pstate_2_2
85E1 LD HL,$858F
85E4 INC (HL)
85E5 LD A,($858E)
85E8 CALL rotate_tower Rotate tower
85EB JR NC,pstate_2_0
85ED XOR A
85EE LD ($858E),A
pstate_2_0 85F1 LD A,($858D)
85F4 NEG
85F6 CALL step_scroll Step scroll
85F9 JR NC,pstate_2_3
85FB LD HL,$858D
85FE DEC (HL)
85FF JR NZ,pstate_2_0
8601 LD A,($858E)
8604 OR A
8605 JR Z,pstate_2_1
8607 LD A,($858F)
860A CP $02
860C JR C,pstate_2_3
pstate_2_1 860E LD HL,$60BF
8611 INC (HL)
pstate_2_2 8612 LD A,$02
8614 LD HL,$60BF
8617 SUB (HL)
8618 LD HL,$60BE
861B ADD A,(HL)
861C ADD A,$0C
861E LD ($60C1),A
8621 LD HL,$60BF
8624 INC (HL)
8625 LD A,(HL)
8626 CP $03
8628 RET NZ
8629 LD A,$02
862B CALL play_sound Play sound
862E JP pstate_reset
pstate_2_3 8631 LD A,($858D)
8634 INC A
8635 CP $04
8637 JR C,pstate_2_4
8639 LD A,$04
pstate_2_4 863B LD ($858D),A
863E RET
863F: Data block at 863F
23-byte signed-offset data table read by pstate_1 for scroll-delta/frame lookups.
Byte tbl_863f itself doubles as a SELF-MODIFIED scratch cell: code_8656/code_8673 store the climb's vertical-input direction there for pstate_1 to feed into ROTATE_TOWER.
tbl_863f 863F DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$03,$02,$02,$01
8647 DEFB $01,$00,$00,$FF,$FF,$FE,$FE,$FD
864F DEFB $02,$02,$01,$00,$FF,$FE,$FE
8656: Enter column climb (via horizontal input)
Used by the routine at pstate_play.
Climb-column entry #1 (from pstate_play when horizontal input is pressed on a normal cell).
Sets player_state=1 (pstate_1), stashes vertical input into tbl_863f[0] (self-mod, read back by pstate_1), resets phase=0, sets self-mod climb params $8641=0/$8642=$0C, plays sound 4 (climb start).
code_8656 8656 LD A,$01
8658 LD ($60C0),A set Player state-machine state
865B LD A,($601D) get Signed input
865E LD ($863F),A
8661 XOR A
8662 LD ($60BF),A set Player state-machine phase/height window
8665 LD ($8641),A
8668 LD A,$0C
866A LD ($8642),A
866D LD A,$04
866F CALL play_sound Play sound
8672 RET
8673: Enter column climb (via blocked rotate)
Used by the routine at pstate_play.
Climb-column entry #2: alternate entry into pstate_1, taken from pstate_play when ROTATE_TOWER reports a collision (carry) while vertical input is held.
Same shape as code_8656 but with different climb params ($8641=$80, $8642=$07) and no sound call.
code_8673 8673 LD A,$01
8675 LD ($60C0),A set Player state-machine state
8678 LD A,($601D) get Signed input
867B LD ($863F),A
867E XOR A
867F LD ($60BF),A set Player state-machine phase/height window
8682 LD A,$80
8684 LD ($8641),A
8687 LD A,$07
8689 LD ($8642),A
868C RET
868D: Pstate 1
State 1: climbing a tower column (entered via code_8656/code_8673).
Sets a sprite frame from phase&7 combined with facing, ROTATE_TOWERs using the direction stashed in tbl_863f, then looks up a scroll delta (table $8643, mirrored $864F when climbing the other way) and applies it via STEP_SCROLL.
On a special zero-crossing case it aborts the climb (sound 2, back to pstate_reset). Otherwise advances phase; once phase reaches the $8642 limit it does a final STEP_SCROLL($FF) -- no-carry falls into the pstate_5 setup path (pstate_play_cell1b), carry sets phase=1 and player_state=2 (pstate_2, slide-in).
pstate_1 868D LD A,($60BF)
8690 AND $07
8692 LD HL,$60BE
8695 OR (HL)
8696 ADD A,$00
8698 LD ($60C1),A
869B LD A,($863F)
869E CALL rotate_tower Rotate tower
86A1 LD A,($60BF) get Player state-machine phase/height window
86A4 LD E,A
86A5 LD D,$00
86A7 LD HL,$8643
86AA LD A,($8641)
86AD OR A
86AE JP P,pstate_1_0
86B1 LD HL,$864F
pstate_1_0 86B4 ADD HL,DE
86B5 LD A,(HL)
pstate_1_1 86B6 LD ($8640),A
86B9 CALL step_scroll Step scroll
86BC JR NC,pstate_1_3
86BE LD A,($8640)
86C1 OR A
86C2 JP M,pstate_1_2
86C5 DEC A
86C6 JR NZ,pstate_1_1
86C8 JR pstate_1_3
pstate_1_2 86CA INC A
86CB JR NZ,pstate_1_1
86CD LD A,$02
86CF CALL play_sound Play sound
86D2 JP pstate_reset
pstate_1_3 86D5 LD HL,$60BF
86D8 INC (HL)
86D9 LD A,(HL)
86DA LD HL,$8642
86DD CP (HL)
86DE RET C
86DF LD A,$FF
86E1 CALL step_scroll Step scroll
86E4 JP NC,pstate_play_cell1b
86E7 LD A,$01
86E9 LD ($60BF),A set Player state-machine phase/height window
86EC LD A,$02
86EE LD ($60C0),A set Player state-machine state
86F1 RET
86F2: Data block at 86F2
Sprite-frame table for pstate_3 (climb animation), indexed by phase.
86F2 DEFB $08,$09,$0A,$0B,$8A,$89,$88,$88
86FA DEFB $89,$8A,$0B,$0A,$09,$08,$90,$91
8702 DEFB $92,$13,$10,$11,$12,$13,$0B,$8A
870A DEFB $89,$88,$0B,$0A,$09,$08,$94,$14
8712 DEFB $15,$95,$96,$16,$97,$17,$98,$18
871A DEFB $19,$99,$1A,$1B,$1A,$00,$1C,$1D
8722 DEFB $1E,$00
8724: Enter pstate 4 (jump/fall)
Used by the routine at pstate_play.
Entry into pstate_4 (jump/fall), taken from pstate_play when fire is pressed over a conveyor/special cell.
Sets player_state=4, phase=0, clears self-mod flag $8723 (used later inside pstate_4's landing logic), plays sound 3 (jump start).
pstate4_entry 8724 LD A,$04
8726 LD ($60C0),A set Player state-machine state
8729 XOR A
872A LD ($60BF),A set Player state-machine phase/height window
872D LD ($8723),A
8730 LD A,$03
8732 CALL play_sound Play sound
8735 RET
8736: Pstate 4
State 4: jump/fall arc (entered from pstate4_entry or from pstate_play's ROTATE_TOWER-blocked path).
Early phase (0..6): plays a landing sound at gated points, picks a sprite frame from tables $8700/$8704 (mirrored by facing), nudges tower rotation toward the center column (tower_height&7 vs 4) via ROTATE_TOWER, advances phase.
Phase 7..$0C: picks frame from table $8709, advances phase. Falls through to 879B for the rest of the arc.
pstate_4 8736 LD A,($60BF) A = pstate_phase (landing-sound gate)
8739 CP $07 phase in [7,$22]? sound-gate window test
873B JR C,pstate_4_0
873D CP $23
873F JR NC,pstate_4_0
8741 SUB $07
8743 AND $03
8745 LD A,$02
8747 CALL Z,play_sound Play sound
pstate_4_0 874A LD A,($60BF) get Player state-machine phase/height window
874D CP $04 phase>=4? skip walk-frame select
874F JR NC,pstate_4_4
8751 LD E,A select sprite frame table $8700/$8704 (mirrored by facing)
8752 LD D,$00
8754 LD HL,$8704
8757 LD A,($60BE)
875A OR A
875B JP P,pstate_4_1
875E LD HL,$8700
pstate_4_1 8761 ADD HL,DE
8762 LD A,(HL)
8763 LD ($60C1),A
8766 LD A,($60B9) get Tower rotation position
8769 AND $07 rotate tower toward center column (compare &7 vs 4)
876B CP $04
876D JR Z,pstate_4_3
876F LD A,$01
8771 JR C,pstate_4_2
8773 LD A,$FF
pstate_4_2 8775 CALL rotate_tower Rotate tower
pstate_4_3 8778 LD HL,$60BF
877B INC (HL)
877C RET
pstate_4_4 877D CP $07 phase<7: force phase=7 (skip window)
877F JR NC,pstate_4_5
8781 LD A,$07
8783 LD ($60BF),A set Player state-machine phase/height window
pstate_4_5 8786 CP $0D phase>=$0D? end of this section
8788 JR NC,$879B
878A LD E,A phase 7..$0C: frame from table $8709, phase++
878B LD D,$00
878D LD HL,$8709
8790 ADD HL,DE
8791 LD A,(HL)
8792 LD ($60C1),A
8795 LD HL,$60BF
8798 INC (HL)
8799 RET
879A: Unused
879A DEFS $01
879B: Pstate 4 land
Used by the routine at pstate_4.
Continuation of pstate_4 for phase>=$0D (descent/landing).
At phase==$1D: clears $60C2, and if game_state!=0 sets game_state=2 (signals landing to main_tick). Also rotates tower position by +-2 per facing, toggling a self-mod alternator so this only fires every other call.
Phase $1D..$22: frame from table $86F9. Phase>=$23: if game_state==3, JP level_shift_entry (external -- special level-transition landing, not covered here). Else STEP_SCROLL($FC) and, at phase<$24, latches a landing facing direction into $60BE from vertical input.
Phase $24..$27: frame from table $86E4/$86E8 (mirrored). Phase>=$28: jump sequence complete, JP pstate_reset.
879B CP $1D phase < $1D? skip landing-signal block
879D JR NC,$87CF
879F XOR A clear $60C2
87A0 LD ($60C2),A
87A3 LD A,($60D9) get Game state
87A6 OR A
87A7 JR Z,$87AF
87A9 LD A,$02
87AB LD ($60D9),A set Game state
87AE RET
87AF LD A,($60BE) rotate +-2 per facing (self-mod alternator gated)
87B2 OR A
87B3 LD A,$02
87B5 JP M,$87BA
87B8 LD A,$FE
87BA LD HL,$60B9
87BD ADD A,(HL)
87BE AND $7F
87C0 LD ($60B9),A set Tower rotation position
87C3 LD HL,$879A alternator toggled -> fire every other call
87C6 INC (HL)
87C7 BIT 0,(HL)
87C9 RET NZ
87CA LD HL,$60BF not yet: phase++, done
87CD INC (HL)
87CE RET
87CF CP $23 phase < $23? use table $86F9 frame
87D1 JR NC,$87E8
87D3 LD E,A frame from $86F9[phase], phase++
87D4 LD D,$00
87D6 LD HL,$86F9
87D9 ADD HL,DE
87DA LD A,(HL)
87DB LD ($60C1),A
87DE LD A,$01
87E0 LD ($60C2),A
87E3 LD HL,$60BF
87E6 INC (HL)
87E7 RET
87E8 LD B,A phase >= $23: check game_state==3
87E9 LD A,($60D9) get Game state
87EC CP $03
87EE JP Z,level_shift_entry
87F1 LD A,B
87F2 CP $24
87F4 JR NC,$8812
87F6 LD A,$FC
87F8 CALL step_scroll Step scroll
87FB RET NC
87FC LD B,$00
87FE LD A,($601D) get Signed input
8801 OR A
8802 JR Z,$880D
8804 JP M,$8809
8807 LD B,$80
8809 LD A,B
880A LD ($60BE),A
880D LD HL,$60BF
8810 INC (HL)
8811 RET
8812 CP $28 phase < $28? frame table $86E4/E8
8814 JP NC,pstate_reset
8817 LD E,A
8818 LD D,$00
881A LD HL,$86E4
881D LD A,($60BE)
8820 OR A
8821 JP M,$8827
8824 LD HL,$86E8
8827 ADD HL,DE
8828 LD A,(HL)
8829 LD ($60C1),A
882C LD HL,$60BF
882F INC (HL)
8830 RET
8831: Pstate 4 land tail
Used by the routine at pstate_play.
Tail of pstate_4's landing phase-$24..$27 frame-select branch: sets player_state=2 or similar hand-off, phase, plays a sound.
pstate_4_land_tail 8831 LD IX,$605F
8835 LD A,(IX+$2A)
8838 CP $FF
883A JP NZ,pstate_reset
883D LD A,$05
883F LD ($60C0),A set Player state-machine state
8842 XOR A
8843 LD ($60BF),A set Player state-machine phase/height window
8846 LD A,($60BE)
8849 OR $00
884B LD ($60C1),A
884E LD A,$08
8850 CALL play_sound Play sound
8853 RET
8854: Pstate 5
State 5: ledge-grab / climb-onto (entered from pstate_1 at pstate_play_cell1b when a column climb ends without reaching the top-of-climb case).
Calls ROTATE_IF_IDLE each frame. At phase==3 the grab completes -> JP pstate_reset. Otherwise picks a sprite frame from table $871C indexed by phase OR'd with facing bits ($60BE), and at phase==1 calls update_player_obj to sync the actor's screen position. Increments phase each call.
pstate_5 8854 CALL rotate_if_idle
8857 LD A,($60BF) get Player state-machine phase/height window
885A CP $03
885C JP Z,pstate_reset
885F LD E,A
8860 LD D,$00
8862 LD HL,$871C
8865 ADD HL,DE
8866 LD A,(HL)
8867 LD HL,$60BE
886A OR (HL)
886B LD ($60C1),A
886E LD A,E
886F CP $01
8871 JR NZ,pstate_5_0
8873 CALL update_player_obj Update player obj
pstate_5_0 8876 LD HL,$60BF
8879 INC (HL)
887A RET
887B: Update player obj
Used by the routine at pstate_5.
Copy the live tower position ($60BA) into the player object (actor_obj) column fields (+6/+12), biased by tower_height and facing ($60BE bit7).
Clobbers: A,HL,IX.
update_player_obj 887B LD IX,$605F
887F LD HL,($60BA)
8882 LD (IX+$06),L
8885 LD (IX+$0C),H
8888 LD A,($60B9) get Tower rotation position
888B DEC A
888C LD HL,$60BE
888F BIT 7,(HL)
8891 JR Z,update_player_obj_0
8893 ADD A,$02
update_player_obj_0 8895 AND $7F
8897 LD (IX+$00),A
889A LD (IX+$2A),$00
889E LD A,$01
88A0 BIT 7,(HL)
88A2 JR NZ,update_player_obj_1
88A4 LD A,$FF
update_player_obj_1 88A6 LD (IX+$36),A
88A9 RET
88AA: Unused
88AA DEFS $01
88AB: Exit pstate 5 (ledge grab)
Used by the routine at pstate_play.
Exit path from pstate_5's ledge-grab completion: checks CHECK_CONVEYOR under the player and either hands off into pstate_6 (state=6, on a conveyor) or resets phase/state back to normal play.
pstate5_exit 88AB LD ($88AA),A
88AE XOR A
88AF LD ($60BF),A set Player state-machine phase/height window
88B2 LD A,$06
88B4 LD ($60C0),A set Player state-machine state
88B7 CALL check_conveyor Check conveyor
88BA JP jp_free_object
88BD: Pstate 6
State 6: riding a conveyor / column-transition (entered from pstate5_exit).
Phase==0: nudges tower rotation (tower_height) toward the center column (compare &7 vs 4), same trick as pstate_4.
Phase==1: sets flag 6038=$FE, calls CHECK_CONVEYOR and combines its result with a self-mod "descending" byte at 88AA to build a movement mask, then applies a signed 16-bit step to the actor's column position ($60BA). Every 4th sub-step plays sound $0A and re-checks the conveyor.
Examines the cell flags to either block movement (early RET) or complete it: on completion ORs $81 into the cell flags, calls $A766 (object/effect hook, not analysed here), sets 6038=$FF, returns to pstate_reset.
pstate_6 88BD LD A,($60BF) phase!=0? skip center-rotate step
88C0 OR A
88C1 JR NZ,pstate_6_3
88C3 LD A,($60B9) get Tower rotation position
88C6 AND $07
88C8 CP $04
88CA JR Z,pstate_6_1
88CC LD A,$01
88CE JR C,pstate_6_0
88D0 LD A,$FF
pstate_6_0 88D2 LD HL,$60B9
88D5 ADD A,(HL)
88D6 AND $7F
88D8 LD (HL),A
88D9 RET
pstate_6_1 88DA LD HL,$60BF phase==0 tail: phase++, flag_6038=$FE
88DD INC (HL)
88DE LD A,$FE
88E0 LD ($6038),A set Flag?? $00 $FE $FF
88E3 CALL check_conveyor Check conveyor
88E6 LD A,(HL) combine conveyor result with self-mod 88AA descending flag
88E7 AND $0C
88E9 LD IX,$88AA
88ED BIT 7,(IX+$00)
88F1 JR NZ,pstate_6_2
88F3 OR $80
pstate_6_2 88F5 LD (HL),A
pstate_6_3 88F6 LD A,($88AA) A = descending flag; B = sign extend
88F9 LD B,$00
88FB OR A
88FC JP P,pstate_6_4
88FF DEC B
pstate_6_4 8900 LD HL,($60BA) apply signed step to actor column (16-bit)
8903 ADD A,L
8904 LD L,A
8905 LD A,B
8906 ADC A,H
8907 AND $03
8909 LD H,A
890A LD ($60BA),HL
890D LD A,L
890E AND $03
8910 RET NZ
8911 LD A,$0A
8913 CALL play_sound Play sound
8916 CALL check_conveyor Check conveyor
8919 LD A,($88AA) re-check conveyor result sign
891C OR A
891D JP P,pstate_6_6
8920 LD A,(HL) facing-negative branch: test cell flags, block or pass
8921 AND $0C
8923 CP $04
8925 JR Z,pstate_6_8
8927 CP $0C
8929 JR NZ,pstate_6_5
892B LD B,A
892C LD A,($601E) get Signed input
892F OR A
8930 LD A,B
8931 JP P,pstate_6_8
pstate_6_5 8934 LD (HL),A
8935 RET
pstate_6_6 8936 LD A,(HL) facing-positive branch: mirrored test
8937 AND $0C
8939 CP $08
893B JR Z,pstate_6_8
893D CP $0C
893F JR NZ,pstate_6_7
8941 LD B,A
8942 LD A,($601E) get Signed input
8945 DEC A
8946 LD A,B
8947 JR NZ,pstate_6_8
pstate_6_7 8949 OR $80
894B LD (HL),A
894C RET
pstate_6_8 894D OR $81 complete: mark cell done, notify, reset flag, return
894F LD (HL),A
8950 CALL jp_alloc_slot
8953 LD A,$FF
8955 LD ($6038),A set Flag?? $00 $FE $FF
8958 JP pstate_reset
895B: Collision test
Used by the routine at check_collision.
Player-vs-world collision probe, called from check_collision.
Builds a synthetic object record for the player at $6060 (angular pos=tower_height, column=$60BA+1/$60BB carry AND 3) so the shared actor-vs-actor code in check_collisions can be reused, then calls it.
If check_collisions reports a hit (CY), converts its returned IY pointer into IX and returns CY=1 (hit an enemy/moving actor).
If no actor hit, falls back to a static "wall marker" scan: walks the 4 object_table entries, considering only those with state (+48)==7 (used here as a static-obstacle marker, distinct from player_state's own state 7), and tests whether the player's row (16-bit, from $60BA/$60BB) falls within roughly [entry_row-9, entry_row+16] of the entry's position (IX+6/IX+12). First match sets CY=1 with IX pointing at that entry else NC.
Out: CY=hit, IX=colliding object (actor or wall-marker entry).
Clobbers: A,BC,DE,HL,IX.
collision_test 895B LD IX,$6060 IX -> synthetic player record ($6060)
895F LD A,($60B9) get Tower rotation position
8962 LD (IX+$00),A store angular pos + column (16-bit) into it
8965 LD A,($60BA)
8968 ADD A,$01
896A LD (IX+$06),A
896D LD A,($60BB)
8970 ADC A,$00
8972 AND $03
8974 LD (IX+$0C),A
8977 CALL check_collisions Check collisions
897A JR C,collision_test_3 actor hit -> convert IY to IX (below)
897C LD B,$04 fallback: B=4, walk object_table for wall markers
897E LD IX,$605B
collision_test_0 8982 LD A,(IX+$30) only consider state==7 (wall marker) entries
8985 CP $07
8987 JR Z,collision_test_2
8989 INC IX
898B DJNZ collision_test_0
collision_test_1 898D OR A
898E RET
collision_test_2 898F LD A,(IX+$00) row window test vs entry's position: too far?
8992 CP $35
8994 JR C,collision_test_1
8996 CP $44
8998 JR NC,collision_test_1
899A LD A,(IX+$06) row+7 <= entry's position? (upper bound)
899D ADD A,$07
899F LD L,A
89A0 LD A,(IX+$0C)
89A3 ADC A,$00
89A5 LD H,A
89A6 LD DE,($60BA)
89AA OR A
89AB SBC HL,DE
89AD JR C,collision_test_1
89AF LD HL,$0009 entry's position+9 >= row? (lower bound)
89B2 ADD HL,DE
89B3 LD E,(IX+$06)
89B6 LD D,(IX+$0C)
89B9 OR A
89BA SBC HL,DE
89BC JR C,collision_test_1
89BE SCF
89BF RET
collision_test_3 89C0 PUSH HL convert actor hit: HL(from check_collisions)->IX, set CY
89C1 POP IX
89C3 SCF
89C4 RET
89C5: Check collision
Used by the routine at main_tick.
Test the player against the tower/hazards and apply the result.
Dispatches on player_state (player_state):
7 = invulnerable/idle -> no test (RET)
4 = check a height window (pstate_phase in $0A..$1E)
8 = RET else test flag $60C5.
Calls collision_test (carry = hit).
On a hit it patches the value at hit_response (SELF-MODIFYING: $14/$0F/$20 select the bump/death response used downstream) and branches into the hit handler.
check_collision 89C5 LD A,($60C0) A = player_state (player_state)
89C8 CP $07
89CA RET Z state 7: no collision this frame
89CB CP $04
89CD JR NZ,check_collision_0
89CF LD A,($60BF) A = pstate_phase (height-in-column)
89D2 CP $0A
89D4 JR C,check_collision_1
89D6 CP $1F
89D8 JR NC,check_collision_1
89DA RET
check_collision_0 89DB CP $08
89DD RET Z
89DE LD A,($60C5)
89E1 OR A
89E2 JR NZ,check_collision_6
check_collision_1 89E4 CALL collision_test Collision test
89E7 JR C,check_collision_2 hit -> hit handler
89E9 LD A,($60C0) get Player state-machine state
89EC CP $06
89EE RET NZ
89EF LD A,($60B9) get Tower rotation position
89F2 LD HL,($60BA)
89F5 CALL check_rotate_collision Check rotate collision
89F8 RET NC
89F9 LD A,$14
89FB LD ($8A49),A patch response code at hit_response = $14 (SELF-MOD)
89FE JP check_collision_4
check_collision_2 8A01 LD A,(IX+$30) A = colliding object state (+48)
8A04 LD B,$0F
8A06 CP $07
8A08 JR Z,check_collision_3
8A0A LD B,$14
check_collision_3 8A0C LD A,B
8A0D LD ($8A49),A patch response code ($0F enemy / $14 wall)
8A10 LD A,($60C0) get Player state-machine state
8A13 CP $06
8A15 JR NZ,check_collision_5
check_collision_4 8A17 LD A,($60BA)
8A1A AND $03
8A1C JR Z,check_collision_7
8A1E LD HL,$60C5
8A21 INC (HL)
8A22 RET
check_collision_5 8A23 JP coll_apply
check_collision_6 8A26 LD A,($60C0) get Player state-machine state
8A29 CP $06
8A2B JR NZ,check_collision_9
8A2D LD A,($60BA)
8A30 AND $03
8A32 RET NZ
check_collision_7 8A33 CALL check_conveyor Check conveyor
8A36 CALL jp_alloc_slot
8A39 JR NC,check_collision_8
8A3B LD (HL),$8D
check_collision_8 8A3D LD A,$FF
8A3F LD ($6038),A set Flag?? $00 $FE $FF
check_collision_9 8A42 XOR A
8A43 LD ($60C5),A
8A46 JP coll_apply
8A49: Unused
SELF-MODIFIED scratch byte, not code: check_collision writes a response code here ($14 wall / $0F enemy) on a hit, and code_a871 reads it at $8A74 to pick the bump-vs-death branch.
hit_response 8A49 DEFS $01
8A4A: Apply a hit (player invulnerable, sound)
Used by the routine at check_collision.
Common "player got hit" handler, reached from check_collision's hit path.
Sets player_state=7 (invulnerable/frozen, matches check_collision's "state 7 = no test" case), phase=0, game_state=0, calls $A72D with A=2 (life/damage counter update), plays sound 5 (hit sound).
coll_apply 8A4A LD A,$07
8A4C LD ($60C0),A set Player state-machine state
8A4F XOR A
8A50 LD ($60BF),A set Player state-machine phase/height window
8A53 LD ($60D9),A set Game state
8A56 LD A,$02
8A58 CALL jp_bonus_sub
8A5B LD A,$05
8A5D CALL play_sound Play sound
8A60 RET
8A61: Data block at 8A61
16-byte signed-offset table used by code_8a71 to size the knockback step, indexed by (response_code>>1)&3.
tbl_8a61 8A61 DEFB $03,$02,$01,$01,$01,$00,$00,$FF
8A69 DEFB $FE,$FE,$FD,$FD,$FD,$FD,$FD,$FC
8A71: Bump/knockback handler
Bump/knockback handler, dispatched from check_collision's hit path via the response code at hit_response (hit_response).
If the current animation phase equals the stored response code: "wall bump" branch ($8AB5) -- sets a sprite frame, subtracts 4 from the actor's column position ($60BA), and if that didn't go negative, re-checks via CHECK_ROTATE_COLLISION; no collision bounces back into pstate_play_cell1 (pstate_play_cell1), otherwise (or on negative position) falls into the death handler at $8AD7.
If phase differs from the response code: "step" branch -- picks a sprite frame from table $871F (mirrored by facing), clamps the response code to $0F, looks up a signed delta from tbl_8a61 and applies it to the actor's column position; going negative also falls into $8AD7 (death), otherwise the position is masked/stored and phase increments.
code_8a71 8A71 LD A,($60BF) A=phase; compare vs stored response code (hit_response)
8A74 LD HL,$8A49
8A77 CP (HL)
8A78 JR Z,code_8a71_2
8A7A LD C,A step branch: index sprite frame table $871F by (code>>1)&3
8A7B SRL A
8A7D AND $03
8A7F LD E,A
8A80 LD D,$00
8A82 LD HL,$871F
8A85 ADD HL,DE
8A86 LD A,($60BE)
8A89 OR (HL)
8A8A LD ($60C1),A
8A8D LD D,$00 clamp response code to $0F for delta lookup
8A8F LD A,C
8A90 CP $0F
8A92 JR C,code_8a71_0
8A94 LD C,$0F
code_8a71_0 8A96 LD B,$00 B=0; lookup signed delta from tbl_8a61
8A98 LD HL,$8A61
8A9B ADD HL,BC
8A9C LD A,(HL)
8A9D OR A
8A9E JP P,code_8a71_1
8AA1 DEC B
code_8a71_1 8AA2 LD HL,$60BA apply delta to actor column (16-bit)
8AA5 ADD A,(HL)
8AA6 LD (HL),A
8AA7 LD A,B
8AA8 INC HL
8AA9 ADC A,(HL)
8AAA JP M,death_handler went negative -> death_handler
8AAD AND $03
8AAF LD (HL),A
8AB0 LD HL,$60BF
8AB3 INC (HL)
8AB4 RET
code_8a71_2 8AB5 LD A,($60BE) bump branch: sprite frame from facing
8AB8 OR $00
8ABA LD ($60C1),A
8ABD LD HL,($60BA) subtract 4 from actor column (wall bump)
8AC0 LD DE,$0004
8AC3 OR A
8AC4 SBC HL,DE
8AC6 LD ($60BA),HL
8AC9 BIT 7,H negative -> death_handler
8ACB JR NZ,death_handler
8ACD LD A,($60B9) get Tower rotation position
8AD0 CALL check_rotate_collision Check rotate collision
8AD3 RET C
8AD4 JP pstate_play_cell1
Death processing: sets player_state=8 (pstate_8), phase=0, clears actor position ($60BA/$60BB), resets each of the 4 object_table actors' state field (+48: 7->0, else->4) and clears their +42 field, plays sound 6, falls straight into pstate_8.
death_handler 8AD7 LD A,$08 death: state=8
8AD9 LD ($60C0),A set Player state-machine state
8ADC XOR A
8ADD LD ($60BF),A set Player state-machine phase/height window
8AE0 LD ($60BA),A clear actor position ($60BA/$60BB)
8AE3 LD ($60BB),A
8AE6 LD IX,$605B IX -> object_table, B=4
8AEA LD B,$04
code_8a71_3 8AEC LD A,(IX+$30) reset each actor's state (7->0, else->4), clear phase
8AEF CP $00
8AF1 JR Z,code_8a71_6
8AF3 CP $07
8AF5 JR NZ,code_8a71_4
8AF7 LD A,$00
8AF9 JP code_8a71_5
code_8a71_4 8AFC LD A,$04
code_8a71_5 8AFE LD (IX+$30),A
8B01 LD (IX+$2A),$00
code_8a71_6 8B05 INC IX next actor, loop
8B07 DJNZ code_8a71_3
8B09 LD A,$06
8B0B CALL play_sound Play sound
8B0E: Pstate 8
State 8: death fall-off animation, entered from death_handler ($8AD7).
While phase<$18, sets sprite frame ($60C1) from phase>>2 and increments phase each call. Once phase>=$18, clears $60C2 and keeps counting.
At phase==$38 sets death_flag (death_flag)=1 -- this is the signal main_loop polls to trigger the life-loss handler.
pstate_8 8B0E LD HL,$60BF A = pstate_phase
8B11 LD A,(HL)
8B12 CP $18 phase<$18: frame=phase>>2, phase++
8B14 JR NC,pstate_8_0
8B16 SRL A
8B18 SRL A
8B1A LD ($60C1),A
8B1D INC (HL)
8B1E RET
pstate_8_0 8B1F XOR A phase>=$18: clear $60C2, check phase==$38
8B20 LD ($60C2),A
8B23 LD A,(HL)
8B24 CP $38
8B26 JR Z,pstate_8_1
8B28 INC (HL)
8B29 RET
pstate_8_1 8B2A LD A,$01
8B2C LD ($60C3),A set Player died this frame
8B2F RET
8B30: Unused
8B30 DEFS $20
8B50: Sprite setup
Used by the routine at draw_player.
Compute on-screen positions for the 4 actor sprites (objects 0..3) ahead of drawing.
Derives a buffer base from tower_height into 600A, fills two coordinate tables at 8B30/$8B40 (X and Y per sprite, via col_screen_y/col_screen_x), then per object copies sprite id (IX+18 -> IX+24) and stores the screen column (IX+30) from the angular position (IX+0).
Self-mod: 8B30/$8B40 are coordinate scratch buffers IN code.
sprite_setup 8B50 LD HL,$60B9 HL -> tower_height
8B53 LD A,$04
8B55 SUB (HL) 4 - height, low 3 bits -> sub-row
8B56 AND $07
8B58 LD L,$00
8B5A SRL A
8B5C RR L
8B5E LD H,A
8B5F LD DE,$6600 DE = $6600 sprite work buffer base
8B62 ADD HL,DE
8B63 LD ($600A),HL store buffer ptr -> 600A
8B66 LD HL,$8B30 fill X-coord table 8B30 (4 sprites, step 8)
8B69 LD A,$04
sprite_setup_0 8B6B PUSH AF
8B6C CALL col_screen_y Col screen y
8B6F ADD A,$1E
8B71 LD (HL),A
8B72 INC HL
8B73 POP AF
8B74 ADD A,$08
8B76 CP $84
8B78 JR NZ,sprite_setup_0
8B7A LD HL,$8B40 fill Y-coord table $8B40 (4 sprites)
8B7D LD A,$04
sprite_setup_1 8B7F PUSH AF
8B80 CALL col_screen_x Col screen x
8B83 LD (HL),A
8B84 INC HL
8B85 POP AF
8B86 ADD A,$08
8B88 CP $84
8B8A JR NZ,sprite_setup_1
8B8C LD IX,$605B IX -> object_table
8B90 LD B,$04
sprite_setup_2 8B92 PUSH BC
8B93 LD A,(IX+$12) back up sprite id (IX+18 -> IX+24)
8B96 LD (IX+$18),A
8B99 LD A,(IX+$30)
8B9C CP $00 state 0 (inactive) -> skip
8B9E JR Z,sprite_setup_4
8BA0 CP $07
8BA2 LD A,$40 state 7 -> fixed screen col $40
8BA4 JR Z,sprite_setup_3
8BA6 LD A,(IX+$00)
8BA9 CALL col_screen_x Col screen x
sprite_setup_3 8BAC LD (IX+$1E),A store sprite screen column (IX+30)
8BAF LD L,(IX+$06)
8BB2 LD H,(IX+$0C)
8BB5 LD DE,($6057)
8BB9 OR A
8BBA PUSH HL
8BBB SBC HL,DE
8BBD POP HL
8BBE JR C,sprite_setup_6
8BC0 LD DE,($6059)
8BC4 OR A
8BC5 SBC HL,DE
8BC7 JR NC,sprite_setup_4
8BC9 LD A,(IX+$30)
8BCC CP $07
8BCE JR Z,sprite_setup_5
8BD0 CALL calc_sprite_shift Calc sprite shift
8BD3 JR NC,sprite_setup_5
sprite_setup_4 8BD5 LD (IX+$18),$FF
sprite_setup_5 8BD9 POP BC
8BDA INC IX
8BDC DJNZ sprite_setup_2
8BDE RET
sprite_setup_6 8BDF LD A,(IX+$30)
8BE2 CP $07
8BE4 JR Z,sprite_setup_4
8BE6 LD (IX+$30),$00
8BEA JR sprite_setup_4
8BEC: Col screen y
Used by the routines at sprite_setup and calc_sprite_shift.
Map an actor's angular column to its screen Y / vertical-projection value.
In: A = angular position (0..127).
Offsets by the tower rotation: rel = (A + $20 - tower_height) & $7F, then returns col_y_table[col_y_table + rel].
Out: A = projected value.
Preserves HL (PUSH/POP).
col_screen_y 8BEC PUSH HL
8BED ADD A,$20 bias by +$20 (quarter turn)
8BEF LD HL,$60B9
8BF2 SUB (HL) subtract current tower rotation
8BF3 AND $7F wrap to 0..127 (around the tower)
8BF5 LD E,A
8BF6 LD D,$00
8BF8 LD HL,$6380 HL -> col_y_table (col_y_table)
8BFB ADD HL,DE
8BFC LD A,(HL) A = table[rel] = projected Y/value
8BFD POP HL
8BFE RET
8BFF: Col screen x
Used by the routine at sprite_setup.
Map an actor's angular column to a screen X column, folding the cylinder so only the front face shows.
In: A = angular position (0..127).
rel = (A + $20 - tower_height) & $7F, then fold: rel < $20 -> rel + $20 (left quadrant) $20..$5F -> $60 - rel (front, mirrored) rel >= $60 -> rel - $60 (right quadrant) Out: A = screen column.
Preserves HL.
col_screen_x 8BFF PUSH HL
8C00 ADD A,$20 bias +$20
8C02 LD HL,$60B9
8C05 SUB (HL) subtract tower rotation
8C06 AND $7F wrap to 0..127
8C08 CP $20 left quadrant?
8C0A JR NC,col_screen_x_0
8C0C ADD A,$20 -> rel + $20
8C0E JR col_screen_x_2
col_screen_x_0 8C10 CP $60 back face?
8C12 JR C,col_screen_x_1
8C14 SUB $60 -> rel - $60 (right quadrant)
8C16 JR col_screen_x_2
col_screen_x_1 8C18 LD H,A
8C19 LD A,$60
8C1B SUB H front: mirror $60 - rel
col_screen_x_2 8C1C POP HL
8C1D RET
8C1E: Sprite shift masks
1bpp AND/shift mask data for drawing the tower-column sprites at sub-byte X offsets (patterns 3F/0F/03/00 FF...).
Base referenced from $8D35/$8F5E.
Interleaved with a few SELF-MODIFIED 1-byte slots ($8C76/$8C77/$8C78/$8C81/$8C82/$8C83/ $8C85) that calc_sprite_shift patches with computed shift counts.
Spans sprite_shift_masks..$8CC1 code resumes at calc_sprite_shift.
sprite_shift_masks 8C1E DEFB $FF,$FF,$FC,$FF,$FF,$F0,$FF,$FF
8C26 DEFB $C0,$FF,$FF,$00,$FF,$FC,$00,$FF
8C2E DEFB $F0,$00,$FF,$C0,$00,$FF,$00,$00
8C36 DEFB $FC,$00,$00,$F0,$00,$00,$C0,$00
8C3E DEFB $00,$3F,$FF,$FF,$0F,$FF,$FF,$03
8C46 DEFB $FF,$FF,$00,$FF,$FF,$00,$3F,$FF
8C4E DEFB $00,$0F,$FF,$00,$03,$FF,$00,$00
8C56 DEFB $FF,$00,$00,$3F,$00,$00,$0F,$00
8C5E DEFB $00,$03,$C0,$03,$FF,$F0,$00,$FF
8C66 DEFB $FC,$00,$3F,$FF,$00,$0F,$FF,$C0
8C6E DEFB $03,$C0,$00,$03,$00,$00,$00,$00
8C76 DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$08,$02,$06,$04
8C7E DEFB $04,$06,$02,$00,$00,$00,$00,$FF
8C86 DEFB $FF,$FF,$03,$03,$03,$07,$07,$07
8C8E DEFB $07,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00
8C96 DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00
8C9E DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00
8CA6 DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00
8CAE DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00
8CB6 DEFB $00,$00,$12,$12,$12,$0E,$0E,$0E
8CBE DEFB $0E,$FF,$FF,$FF
8CC2: Calc sprite shift
Used by the routine at sprite_setup.
For the actor at IX, compute its screen row (via col_screen_y) and the horizontal sub-byte shift, and patch the shift counts into the self-mod slots above ($8C76/77/ 81).
Reads the column buffer (600A) to skip empty/edge columns ($0F/$0E sentinels).
Called from sprite_setup ($8BD0).
calc_sprite_shift 8CC2 LD A,(IX+$00) A = actor angular pos (IX+0)
8CC5 CALL col_screen_y Col screen y
8CC8 ADD A,$1E
8CCA ADD A,$02
8CCC AND $FC
8CCE LD ($8C81),A patch self-mod slot $8C81 (row)
8CD1 LD HL,($600A) HL -> column buffer (600A)
8CD4 ADD HL,DE
8CD5 LD A,(HL)
8CD6 CP $0F $0F = empty column -> skip (RET)
8CD8 RET Z
8CD9 CP $0E
8CDB CCF
8CDC RET Z
8CDD LD B,A
8CDE LD A,($60B9) A = tower_height (rotation)
8CE1 SUB $1D
8CE3 AND $7F
8CE5 SRL A /8 -> byte-column shift
8CE7 SRL A
8CE9 SRL A
8CEB LD C,A
8CEC ADD A,B
8CED AND $0F
8CEF LD ($8C77),A patch shift count slot $8C77
8CF2 LD B,A
8CF3 SRL A
8CF5 SRL A
8CF7 SRL A
8CF9 SRL A
8CFB ADD A,C
8CFC AND $0F
8CFE LD ($8C76),A patch shift count slot $8C76
8D01 LD HL,$FFFF
8D04 LD ($600C),HL
8D07 LD ($600E),HL
8D0A LD ($6010),HL
8D0D LD ($6012),HL
8D10 LD ($6013),HL
8D13 XOR A
8D14 LD ($8C78),A
8D17 LD A,(IX+$00)
8D1A CALL col_screen_y Col screen y
8D1D BIT 6,E
8D1F LD ($8C83),A
8D22 JR Z,calc_sprite_shift_0
8D24 ADD A,$1E
8D26 LD E,A
8D27 LD D,$00
8D29 LD HL,$8C85
8D2C ADD HL,DE
8D2D LD A,(HL)
8D2E CP $FF
8D30 JR Z,calc_sprite_shift_0
8D32 ADD A,A
8D33 ADD A,(HL)
8D34 LD E,A
8D35 LD HL,$8C1E
8D38 ADD HL,DE
8D39 LD A,(HL)
8D3A INC HL
8D3B LD ($600C),A
8D3E LD ($600F),A
8D41 LD ($6012),A
8D44 LD A,(HL)
8D45 INC HL
8D46 LD ($600D),A
8D49 LD ($6010),A
8D4C LD ($6013),A
8D4F LD A,(HL)
8D50 LD ($600E),A
8D53 LD ($6011),A
8D56 LD ($6014),A
8D59 LD HL,$8C78
8D5C INC (HL)
calc_sprite_shift_0 8D5D LD A,(IX+$06)
8D60 AND $03
8D62 LD ($8C82),A
8D65 LD L,(IX+$06)
8D68 LD A,(IX+$0C)
8D6B RRA
8D6C RR L
8D6E RRA
8D6F RR L
8D71 LD A,($8C77)
8D74 LD B,A
8D75 LD A,($8C76)
calc_sprite_shift_1 8D78 PUSH AF
8D79 PUSH BC
8D7A PUSH HL
8D7B CALL calc_sprite_col Calc sprite col
8D7E POP HL
8D7F POP BC
8D80 POP AF
8D81 INC A
8D82 AND $0F
8D84 CP B
8D85 JR NZ,calc_sprite_shift_1
8D87 LD A,($8C78)
8D8A OR A
8D8B RET Z
8D8C PUSH IX
8D8E POP DE
8D8F LD A,($8C83)
8D92 ADD A,$3C
8D94 AND $03
8D96 ADD A,(IX+$12)
8D99 PUSH AF
8D9A LD A,E
8D9B SUB $5B
8D9D ADD A,$7C
8D9F LD (IX+$18),A
8DA2 SUB $7C
8DA4 ADD A,A
8DA5 ADD A,A
8DA6 ADD A,A
8DA7 ADD A,A
8DA8 ADD A,A
8DA9 LD L,A
8DAA ADD A,A
8DAB ADD A,L
8DAC LD L,A
8DAD LD H,$00
8DAF JR NC,calc_sprite_shift_2
8DB1 INC H
calc_sprite_shift_2 8DB2 LD DE,$BC80
8DB5 ADD HL,DE
8DB6 POP AF
8DB7 PUSH HL
8DB8 CALL jp_get_sprite_def
8DBB POP DE
8DBC LD A,C
8DBD CP $02
8DBF JP NZ,patch_expand_masks
8DC2 LD A,($8C82)
8DC5 ADD A,A
8DC6 ADD A,$79
8DC8 LD C,A
8DC9 LD A,$8C
8DCB ADC A,$00
8DCD LD B,A
8DCE LD A,(BC)
8DCF LD ($8DF2),A
8DD2 INC BC
8DD3 LD A,(BC)
8DD4 LD ($8E24),A
8DD7 LD A,($8C82)
8DDA OR A
8DDB JR Z,calc_sprite_shift_3
8DDD LD A,($600C)
8DE0 LD ($8E2D),A
8DE3 CPL
8DE4 LD ($8E27),A
8DE7 LD A,($600D)
8DEA LD ($8E39),A
8DED CPL
8DEE LD ($8E33),A
8DF1 LD B,$00
8DF3 CALL expand_sprite_a Expand sprite a
calc_sprite_shift_3 8DF6 LD A,($600F)
8DF9 LD ($8E2D),A
8DFC CPL
8DFD LD ($8E27),A
8E00 LD A,($6010)
8E03 LD ($8E39),A
8E06 CPL
8E07 LD ($8E33),A
8E0A LD B,$08
8E0C CALL expand_sprite_a Expand sprite a
8E0F LD A,($6012)
8E12 LD ($8E2D),A
8E15 CPL
8E16 LD ($8E27),A
8E19 LD A,($6013)
8E1C LD ($8E39),A
8E1F CPL
8E20 LD ($8E33),A
8E23 LD B,$00
8E25: Expand sprite a
Used by the routine at calc_sprite_shift.
Unrolled copy of a sprite/mask definition from (HL) to (DE), terminated by $FF.
The alternating OR $00 / AND $00 immediates are SELF-MODIFIED (by calc_sprite_shift) to apply the per-column shift mask.
Clobbers: A,DE,HL.
expand_sprite_a 8E25 LD A,(HL) A = source byte (HL)
8E26 OR $00 self-mod OR mask (set by calc_sprite_shift)
8E28 LD (DE),A store merged byte, advance dest
8E29 INC HL
8E2A INC DE
8E2B LD A,(HL) A = next source byte
8E2C AND $00 self-mod AND mask (set by calc_sprite_shift)
8E2E LD (DE),A
8E2F INC HL
8E30 INC DE
8E31 LD A,(HL)
8E32 OR $00
8E34 LD (DE),A
8E35 INC HL
8E36 INC DE
8E37 LD A,(HL)
8E38 AND $00
8E3A LD (DE),A
8E3B INC HL
8E3C INC DE
8E3D LD A,$FF A = $FF (terminator byte)
8E3F LD (DE),A store terminator, advance dest
8E40 INC DE
8E41 INC A A += 1 (second terminator byte)
8E42 LD (DE),A
8E43 INC DE
8E44 DJNZ expand_sprite_a loop 4 columns
8E46 OR A
8E47 RET
Entry point used by calc_sprite_shift, not by expand_sprite_a's own DJNZ loop.
Reads the self-mod shift-count slot ($8C82) to index a 2-byte pair from table $8C79 (shift*2), storing it into the loop-count operands of the first/third strip's "LD B,nn" at $8E81/$8EC7 (operand bytes $8E82/$8EC8) -- CORRECTION: these are NOT routine pointers or mask operands, they are the row counts for the two edge strips. Confirmed by emulation: for every shift value 0-3, (first-strip-count + third-strip-count) == 8, and combined with the always-8-row middle strip, TOTAL decoded height is a shift-INVARIANT 16 rows. When the shift-count slot is 0, the first strip is skipped entirely (JR Z,$8E86) and its row count (0) is moot.
Then, per 8-pixel strip, copies 3 source mask bytes ($600C/$600D/$600E or $600F/$6010/$6011 or $6012/$6013/$6014, selected by the same shift-count test) straight into expand_sprite_b's real OR-immediate operands ($8ECB/$8ED7/$8EE3), and their bitwise complement (CPL) into the paired AND-immediate operands ($8ED1/$8EDD/$8EE9) -- so expand_sprite_b ORs in the new sprite bits and ANDs out the old ones at the right sub-byte shift, then calls expand_sprite_b.
See gfx_c1f6 (gfx_c1f6) for the full sprite-bitmap decode writeup (this self-modifying pipeline is why the bitmap can't be rendered as a raw UDG array image).
Clobbers: A,BC.
patch_expand_masks 8E48 LD A,($8C82) A = shift-count slot ($8C82)
8E4B ADD A,A index a 2-byte routine-pointer pair
8E4C ADD A,$79
8E4E LD C,A
8E4F LD A,$8C
8E51 ADC A,$00
8E53 LD B,A
8E54 LD A,(BC) fetch first pointer byte
8E55 LD ($8E82),A self-mod store: expand_sprite_b OR operand
8E58 INC BC
8E59 LD A,(BC) fetch second pointer byte
8E5A LD ($8EC8),A self-mod store: expand_sprite_b AND operand
8E5D LD A,($8C82) re-test shift-count slot
8E60 OR A
8E61 JR Z,expand_sprite_a_0 zero? -> skip first mask triple
8E63 LD A,($600C) A = mask byte 1 ($600C)
8E66 LD ($8ED1),A self-mod store: OR operand
8E69 CPL complement for AND operand
8E6A LD ($8ECB),A self-mod store: AND operand
8E6D LD A,($600D)
8E70 LD ($8EDD),A
8E73 CPL
8E74 LD ($8ED7),A
8E77 LD A,($600E)
8E7A LD ($8EE9),A
8E7D CPL
8E7E LD ($8EE3),A
8E81 LD B,$00 B = 0 (loop count for this strip)
8E83 CALL expand_sprite_b Expand sprite b
expand_sprite_a_0 8E86 LD A,($600F)
8E89 LD ($8ED1),A
8E8C CPL
8E8D LD ($8ECB),A
8E90 LD A,($6010)
8E93 LD ($8EDD),A
8E96 CPL
8E97 LD ($8ED7),A
8E9A LD A,($6011)
8E9D LD ($8EE9),A
8EA0 CPL
8EA1 LD ($8EE3),A
8EA4 LD B,$08 B = $08 (loop count for this strip)
8EA6 CALL expand_sprite_b Expand sprite b
8EA9 LD A,($6012)
8EAC LD ($8ED1),A
8EAF CPL
8EB0 LD ($8ECB),A
8EB3 LD A,($6013)
8EB6 LD ($8EDD),A
8EB9 CPL
8EBA LD ($8ED7),A
8EBD LD A,($6014)
8EC0 LD ($8EE9),A
8EC3 CPL
8EC4 LD ($8EE3),A
8EC7 LD B,$00 B = 0 (loop count for final strip)
8EC9: Expand sprite b
Used by the routine at expand_sprite_a.
Same unrolled-copy shape as expand_sprite_a, but its OR/AND immediate operands are self-modified by patch_expand_masks ($8E48) rather than calc_sprite_shift directly -- used for the shift-boundary case where 3 source mask bytes (not 2) are needed per strip.
Clobbers: A,DE,HL.
expand_sprite_b 8EC9 LD A,(HL) A = source byte (HL)
8ECA OR $00 self-mod OR mask (set by patch_expand_masks)
8ECC LD (DE),A store merged byte, advance dest
8ECD INC HL
8ECE INC DE
8ECF LD A,(HL)
8ED0 AND $00
8ED2 LD (DE),A
8ED3 INC HL
8ED4 INC DE
8ED5 LD A,(HL)
8ED6 OR $00
8ED8 LD (DE),A
8ED9 INC HL
8EDA INC DE
8EDB LD A,(HL)
8EDC AND $00
8EDE LD (DE),A
8EDF INC HL
8EE0 INC DE
8EE1 LD A,(HL)
8EE2 OR $00
8EE4 LD (DE),A
8EE5 INC HL
8EE6 INC DE
8EE7 LD A,(HL)
8EE8 AND $00
8EEA LD (DE),A
8EEB INC HL
8EEC INC DE
8EED DJNZ expand_sprite_b loop 3 mask bytes
8EEF OR A restore flags, RET
8EF0 RET
8EF1: Calc sprite col
Used by the routine at calc_sprite_shift.
Resolve a sprite column: index table 8B30 by A, compare the result against the actor's clip field (IX+30), and patch the shift slot $8C81.
RET early when within bounds.
Clobbers: A,DE,HL.
calc_sprite_col 8EF1 LD E,A
8EF2 ADD A,$F0
8EF4 LD H,A
8EF5 LD D,$00
8EF7 PUSH HL
8EF8 LD HL,$8B30
8EFB ADD HL,DE
8EFC LD A,(HL)
8EFD LD DE,$0010
8F00 ADD HL,DE
8F01 LD E,A
8F02 LD A,(HL)
8F03 CP (IX+$1E)
8F06 JR NC,calc_sprite_col_0
8F08 POP HL
8F09 RET
calc_sprite_col_0 8F0A LD A,E
8F0B LD HL,$8C81
8F0E SUB (HL)
8F0F ADD A,$0B
8F11 POP HL
8F12 RET M
8F13 CP $16
8F15 RET NC
8F16 LD E,A
8F17 PUSH DE
8F18 LD BC,$6012
8F1B CALL draw_tower_cell Draw tower cell
8F1E INC L
8F1F POP DE
8F20 PUSH DE
8F21 LD BC,$600F
8F24 CALL draw_tower_cell Draw tower cell
8F27 INC L
8F28 POP DE
8F29 LD A,($8C82)
8F2C OR A
8F2D RET Z
8F2E LD BC,$600C
8F31: Draw tower cell
Used by the routine at calc_sprite_col.
Decode one tower map cell byte at (HL) -- full bitfield format documented in docs/Level-Format.md (bit7=occupied, bits0-1=type, bits4-5=subtype).
Bit7 clear (unoccupied) or type==3 (invalid combo here) -> RET, nothing drawn. Otherwise type bit0 selects one of three offset tables (bit0==0 -> tbl_cell_offsets_c $8FA1 bit0==1 and bits2-3==0 -> tbl_cell_offsets_a tbl_cell_offsets_a bit0==1 and bits2-3!=0 -> tbl_cell_offsets_b $8F8B).
The chosen table is indexed by DE (caller-supplied sub-position, not part of the cell byte) negative entries ($FF) mean nothing to draw at that sub-position.
A non-negative entry is *3 and indexes sprite_shift_masks (sprite_shift_masks) for 3 AND-mask bytes, ANDed into 3 destination screen bytes at (BC) to punch out the feature's silhouette. Bumps a self-mod counter at $8C78 on every successful draw.
Clobbers: A,HL,...
draw_tower_cell 8F31 PUSH HL
8F32 LD A,(HL) A = cell byte; bit7 clear (unoccupied) -> RET
8F33 OR A
8F34 JP P,draw_tower_cell_3
8F37 LD H,A H = cell byte; isolate type bits0-1
8F38 AND $03
8F3A CP $03 type==3 (invalid/no-render)? -> RET
8F3C JR Z,draw_tower_cell_3
8F3E SRL A test type bit0
8F40 JR NC,draw_tower_cell_1
8F42 LD A,H reload cell byte, isolate bits2-3
8F43 AND $0C
8F45 JR Z,draw_tower_cell_0 bits2-3==0 -> table_a
8F47 LD HL,$8F8B else -> table_b ($8F8B)
8F4A JR draw_tower_cell_2
draw_tower_cell_0 8F4C LD HL,$8F75 table_a (tbl_cell_offsets_a)
8F4F JR draw_tower_cell_2
draw_tower_cell_1 8F51 LD HL,$8FA1 bit0==0 -> table_c ($8FA1)
draw_tower_cell_2 8F54 ADD HL,DE HL += DE (sub-position index)
8F55 LD A,(HL) A = table[DE]; negative -> RET (nothing here)
8F56 OR A
8F57 JP M,draw_tower_cell_3
8F5A LD E,A index = A*3 into sprite_shift_masks
8F5B ADD A,A
8F5C ADD A,E
8F5D LD E,A
8F5E LD HL,$8C1E HL -> sprite_shift_masks[index]
8F61 ADD HL,DE
8F62 LD A,(BC) AND-mask 3 destination bytes at (BC)
8F63 AND (HL)
8F64 LD (BC),A
8F65 INC BC
8F66 INC HL
8F67 LD A,(BC)
8F68 AND (HL)
8F69 LD (BC),A
8F6A INC BC
8F6B INC HL
8F6C LD A,(BC)
8F6D AND (HL)
8F6E LD (BC),A
8F6F LD HL,$8C78 bump feature-drawn counter ($8C78)
8F72 INC (HL)
draw_tower_cell_3 8F73 POP HL
8F74 RET
8F75: Data block at 8F75
First of three sibling offset tables (tbl_cell_offsets_a/$8F8B/$8FA1), selected by draw_tower_cell on the cell descriptor's bits 2-3, then indexed by DE (low descriptor bits) to get an offset into sprite_shift_masks (sprite_shift_masks).
tbl_cell_offsets_a 8F75 DEFB $0B,$0C,$0D,$0E,$0F,$10,$11,$12
8F7D DEFB $13,$14,$15,$1C,$0A,$09,$08,$07
8F85 DEFB $06,$05,$04,$03,$02,$01,$FF,$0B
8F8D DEFB $0C,$0D,$0E,$0F,$10,$11,$12,$13
8F95 DEFB $14,$1B,$09,$08,$07,$06,$05,$04
8F9D DEFB $03,$02,$01,$00,$FF,$FF,$FF,$0B
8FA5 DEFB $0C,$0D,$0E,$0F,$10,$16,$17,$18
8FAD DEFB $19,$1A,$05,$04,$03,$02,$01,$00
8FB5 DEFB $FF,$FF
8FB7: Clear actor states
Used by the routine at setup_level.
Zero the state byte (+48) of all four object_table actors, deactivating every enemy.
Clobbers: A,B,IX.
clear_actor_states 8FB7 LD IX,$605B
8FBB LD A,$00
8FBD LD B,$04
clear_actor_states_0 8FBF LD (IX+$30),A
8FC2 INC IX
8FC4 DJNZ clear_actor_states_0
8FC6 RET
8FC7: Draw player
Used by the routine at main_tick.
Compute the player sprite's on-screen address from the vertical scroll (scroll_pos) and draw it.
Handles the tower wrap: the playfield scrolls vertically mod the tower height, so the screen row is (scroll_pos +/- offset) kept in range.
Stores the two derived screen pointers ($6057, $6059) then calls the blit helpers (sprite_setup, sprite_depth_sort).
In: scroll_pos = scroll position.
Out: player drawn.
Clobbers A,HL.
draw_player 8FC7 LD HL,($6030) HL = scroll position (scroll_pos)
8FCA LD A,L
8FCB SUB $08 - 8 (sprite top offset)
8FCD LD L,A
8FCE LD A,H
8FCF SBC A,$00
8FD1 AND $03 keep within the 3 screen-thirds (mod via AND 3)
8FD3 LD H,A
8FD4 CP $03 past the wrap point ($3D0)?
8FD6 JR NZ,draw_player_0
8FD8 LD A,L
8FD9 CP $D0
8FDB JR C,draw_player_0
8FDD LD HL,$0000 -> clamp to top (0)
draw_player_0 8FE0 LD ($6057),HL store upper screen pointer ($6057)
8FE3 LD HL,($6030) get Vertical scroll position
8FE6 LD A,L
8FE7 ADD A,$4D + $4D (sprite body offset)
8FE9 LD L,A
8FEA LD A,H
8FEB ADC A,$00
8FED AND $03
8FEF LD H,A
8FF0 LD ($6059),HL store lower screen pointer ($6059)
8FF3 CALL sprite_setup Sprite setup
8FF6 CALL sprite_depth_sort Sprite depth sort
8FF9 RET
8FFA: Draw actors sorted
Used by the routine at main_tick.
Draw the 4 actors back-to-front: for depth key C=3..0, find the object whose field (+36) matches and draw it via draw_actor.
Clobbers: A,BC,IX.
draw_actors_sorted 8FFA LD C,$03
draw_actors_sorted_0 8FFC LD IX,$605B
9000 LD B,$04
9002 LD A,C
draw_actors_sorted_1 9003 CP (IX+$24)
9006 JR Z,draw_actors_sorted_3
9008 INC IX
900A DJNZ draw_actors_sorted_1
draw_actors_sorted_2 900C DEC C
900D JP P,draw_actors_sorted_0
9010 JR draw_actors_sorted_4
draw_actors_sorted_3 9012 PUSH BC
9013 CALL jp_draw_actor
9016 POP BC
9017 JR draw_actors_sorted_2
draw_actors_sorted_4 9019 LD IX,$605F
901D LD A,(IX+$2A)
9020 CP $FF
9022 RET Z
9023 LD A,(IX+$12)
9026 ADD A,$4A
9028 LD (IX+$18),A
902B CALL jp_draw_actor
902E LD A,($60A6)
9031 LD ($60A5),A
9034 RET
9035: Sprite depth sort
Used by the routine at draw_player.
Decide the back-to-front draw order of the 4 actor sprites so nearer ones overdraw farther ones around the tower.
Does all 6 pairwise comparisons of the sprites' screen columns (IX+30..IX+33), packing the results into 6 bits of B, XORs $3F, and uses that as an index into data_908e -- a precomputed table mapping each comparison outcome to a draw permutation.
The 4 order indices are unpacked (2 bits each) into IX+36..IX+39.
TRI
sprite_depth_sort 9035 LD IX,$605B IX -> object_table
9039 LD B,$00
903B LD A,(IX+$1E) compare sprite0 col vs sprite1..3
903E CP (IX+$1F)
9041 RL B shift compare result into key (RL B)
9043 CP (IX+$20)
9046 RL B
9048 CP (IX+$21)
904B RL B
904D LD A,(IX+$1F)
9050 CP (IX+$20)
9053 RL B
9055 CP (IX+$21)
9058 RL B
905A LD A,(IX+$20)
905D CP (IX+$21)
9060 RL B
9062 LD A,B A = packed 6-bit comparison key
9063 XOR $3F invert (XOR $3F) to index orientation
9065 LD C,A
9066 LD B,$00
9068 LD HL,$908E HL -> data_908e[key]
906B ADD HL,BC
906C LD B,(HL) B = packed draw order (4 x 2-bit indices)
906D LD A,B
906E AND $03
9070 LD (IX+$27),A unpack order[0] -> IX+39
9073 LD A,B
9074 RRA
9075 RRA
9076 LD B,A
9077 AND $03
9079 LD (IX+$26),A unpack order[1] -> IX+38
907C LD A,B
907D RRA
907E RRA
907F LD B,A
9080 AND $03
9082 LD (IX+$25),A unpack order[2] -> IX+37
9085 LD A,B
9086 RRA
9087 RRA
9088 AND $03
908A LD (IX+$24),A unpack order[3] -> IX+36
908D RET return: draw order in IX+36..+39
908E: DATA_908E
64-entry packed draw-order permutation table for sprite_depth_sort.
Index = 6-bit pairwise-comparison key of the 4 sprites' columns (XOR $3F).
Each byte packs 4 two-bit object
indices = the back-to-front draw order.
Bounded by update_enemies.
data_908e 908E DEFB $E4,$E1,$00,$D2,$D8,$00,$C9,$C6
9096 DEFB $00,$00,$00,$93,$00,$00,$00,$87
909E DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$9C,$00,$8D,$00
90A6 DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$4E,$4B
90AE DEFB $B4,$B1,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00
90B6 DEFB $00,$72,$00,$63,$00,$00,$00,$00
90BE DEFB $78,$00,$00,$00,$6C,$00,$00,$00
90C6 DEFB $39,$36,$00,$27,$2D,$00,$1E,$1B
90CE DEFB $02,$02,$01,$01,$00,$00,$FF,$FF
90D6 DEFB $FE,$FE,$FC,$00
90DA: Update enemies
Used by the routine at main_tick.
Step the 4 enemy actors (objects 0..3 of object_table).
For each: dispatch on its state (+48):
0 = inactive (skip), 7 = patrol/home toward player_col,
4/6 = -> enemy_move_dispatch (full movement state machine), 5 = phase countdown (+42) -- expires (bit7 of +42 set) into state 2.
else (any other state, e.g. 3/8/9) falls straight into enemy_move_dispatch too, since only 0/7/5 are filtered out here.
State 7 homing: moves angular pos (+0) by speed (+54), then compares against player_col and the tower quadrant marks $3C/$78 to pick a chase direction (+2 / -2), or deactivates.
In: nothing.
Loops B=4 objects.
Clobbers most regs + IX.
update_enemies 90DA LD IX,$605B IX -> object_table (enemy 0)
90DE LD B,$04 B = 4 enemies
This entry point is used by the routine at enemy_next.
update_enemies_0 90E0 PUSH BC
90E1 LD A,(IX+$30) A = this enemy's state (+48)
90E4 CP $00
90E6 JP Z,enemy_next 0 = inactive -> next enemy
90E9 CP $07
90EB JR NZ,update_enemies_6 state 7 = active/homing?
90ED LD (IX+$12),$4E set sprite id (+18) = $4E
90F1 LD A,(IX+$00)
90F4 ADD A,(IX+$36) angular pos (+0) += speed (+54)
90F7 LD (IX+$00),A
90FA LD HL,$60A4 HL -> player_col
90FD BIT 7,(HL) bit7 = which side of the tower the player is on
90FF JR NZ,update_enemies_2
9101 CP $3C at quadrant mark $3C?
9103 JR Z,update_enemies_1
9105 CP $78
9107 JR NZ,update_enemies_5
9109 JR update_enemies_4
update_enemies_1 910B LD (IX+$36),$02 chase: speed = +2 (toward player)
910F JR update_enemies_5
update_enemies_2 9111 CP $3C
9113 JR Z,update_enemies_3
9115 OR A
9116 JR NZ,update_enemies_5
9118 JR update_enemies_4
update_enemies_3 911A LD (IX+$36),$FE chase: speed = -2 ($FE)
911E JR update_enemies_5
update_enemies_4 9120 LD (IX+$30),$00 off-screen/limit -> deactivate (state 0)
9124 LD A,$7D flag $60A3 = $7D (respawn/notify)
9126 LD ($60A3),A
update_enemies_5 9129 JP enemy_next
update_enemies_6 912C CP $04 state 4?
912E JP Z,enemy_move_dispatch
9131 CP $06
9133 JP Z,enemy_move_dispatch
9136 CP $05 state 5 = phase countdown
9138 JP NZ,update_enemies_8
913B DEC (IX+$2A) decrement phase (+42)
913E BIT 7,(IX+$2A) phase underflowed (bit7 set) -> state=2
9142 JR Z,update_enemies_7
9144 LD (IX+$30),$02
update_enemies_7 9148 JP enemy_next
update_enemies_8 914B CALL check_actor_collision_a Check actor collision
914E JR NC,enemy_move_dispatch
9150 LD (IX+$30),$04 blocked -> force state=4, phase=0
9154 LD (IX+$2A),$00
9158: Enemy state dispatch
Used by the routine at update_enemies.
Entry into the enemy's full movement/behaviour state machine, reached from update_enemies for states 4, 6, and the default case.
state==4 -> enemy_idle_check (idle/animate-in-place check). state==6 -> tests phase (+42): phase==6 means a pending transform is queued, jumps to enemy_transform (enemy_transform) else falls into the shared idle-animation tail (enemy_idle_anim, enemy_idle_anim) with a mirrored phase.
Any other state falls through to enemy_move_dispatch2 (enemy_move_dispatch2).
enemy_move_dispatch 9158 LD A,(IX+$30) A = state (+48)
915B CP $04
915D JR Z,enemy_idle_check state 4 -> enemy_idle_check
915F CP $06 state 6?
