9161 JR NZ,enemy_move_dispatch2
9163 LD A,(IX+$2A) A = phase (+42)
9166 CP $06 phase==6 -> pending transform
9168 JR Z,enemy_transform
916A NEG
916C ADD A,$05
916E JR enemy_idle_anim
9170: Enemy transforms into queued next state
Used by the routine at enemy_move_dispatch.
Applies a queued state transform: copies the enemy's own +42/+3C fields into its live state (+48) and phase (+42), sets sprite id=$52.
This is how an enemy morphs into a different behaviour after a countdown (fields +42/+3C are pre-loaded elsewhere as the "next state/phase" to become).
enemy_transform 9170 LD A,(IX+$42)
9173 LD (IX+$30),A
9176 LD A,(IX+$3C)
9179 LD (IX+$2A),A
917C LD (IX+$12),$52
9180 JP enemy_next
9183: Enemy idle: deactivate after full cycle
Used by the routine at enemy_move_dispatch.
state==4 path: if phase (+42) has reached 6, deactivates the enemy (state=0) else falls into enemy_idle_anim to keep cycling its idle sprite.
enemy_idle_check 9183 LD A,(IX+$2A)
9186 CP $06
9188 JR NZ,enemy_idle_anim
918A LD (IX+$30),$00
918E JP enemy_next
9191: Enemy idle animation cycle
Used by the routines at enemy_move_dispatch and enemy_idle_check.
Shared idle-animation tail for state 4 and the non-transform branch of state 6: clamps phase to 0..2 (wraps at 3), computes sprite id = phase*4+$5A, increments phase (+42), done.
enemy_idle_anim 9191 CP $03 clamp phase to 0..2
9193 JR C,enemy_idle_anim_0
9195 SUB $03
enemy_idle_anim_0 9197 ADD A,A sprite id = phase*4 + $5A
9198 ADD A,A
9199 ADD A,$5A
919B LD (IX+$12),A
919E INC (IX+$2A) phase++
91A1 JP enemy_next
91A4: Enemy movement: pick vertical/horizontal/attack path
Used by the routine at enemy_move_dispatch.
Second dispatch tier, reached for any state other than 0/4/5/6/7.
state==3 -> enemy_post_move (enemy_post_move, shared attack/death dispatch). state==8 -> enemy_move_vertical (enemy_move_vertical). else -> enemy_move_horizontal (enemy_move_horizontal, the default case covering states like 1/2/9 during normal movement).
enemy_move_dispatch2 91A4 CP $03 state==3?
91A6 JP Z,enemy_post_move -> enemy_post_move
91A9 CP $08 state==8?
91AB JR NZ,enemy_move_horizontal
91AD: Enemy moves vertically
state 8: moves the enemy's 16-bit vertical position (+6/+12) by its signed speed (+54), backing up the old position in 6034 first.
Tests the new position via CHECK_ACTOR_COLLISION_A (check_actor_collision_a) and, if clear, CHECK_COLLISIONS against the player/other actors on-screen wraps checked via bit7 of the high byte (JP NZ,enemy_bounds_reset = out-of-bounds reset).
On a block, restores the previous position and reverses the speed sign (bounce).
Always ends by setting sprite id from the level number (6039&3)*4+$66 (level-tinted variant), then next enemy -- does not fall into enemy_post_move.
enemy_move_vertical 91AD LD L,(IX+$06) backup old position, add speed (16-bit)
91B0 LD H,(IX+$0C)
91B3 LD ($6034),HL
91B6 LD E,(IX+$36)
91B9 BIT 7,E
91BB LD D,$00
91BD JR Z,enemy_move_vertical_0
91BF DEC D
enemy_move_vertical_0 91C0 ADD HL,DE wrap-check: out of range -> enemy_bounds_reset (bounds reset)
91C1 BIT 7,H
91C3 JP NZ,enemy_bounds_reset
91C6 LD (IX+$06),L
91C9 LD A,H
91CA AND $03
91CC LD (IX+$0C),A
91CF CALL check_actor_collision_a Check actor collision
91D2 JR C,enemy_move_vertical_1
91D4 CALL check_collisions Check collisions
91D7 JR NC,enemy_move_vertical_2
enemy_move_vertical_1 91D9 LD HL,($6034) blocked: restore old position
91DC LD (IX+$06),L
91DF LD (IX+$0C),H
91E2 XOR A reverse speed sign
91E3 SUB (IX+$36)
91E6 LD (IX+$36),A
This entry point is used by the routine at enemy_post_move.
enemy_move_vertical_2 91E9 LD A,($6039)
91EC AND $03
91EE ADD A,A
91EF ADD A,A
91F0 ADD A,$66
91F2 LD (IX+$12),A
91F5 JP enemy_next
91F8: Enemy moves horizontally (around tower)
Used by the routine at enemy_move_dispatch2.
Default movement case (any state not 3/4/5/6/7/8): moves the enemy's angular position (+0) by its signed speed (+54), mod 128, backing up the old value in 6033 first.
Same collision test/bounce pattern as enemy_move_vertical (CHECK_ACTOR_COLLISION_A then CHECK_COLLISIONS on clear on a block, restores old position and reverses speed).
Falls through into enemy_post_move (enemy_post_move), unlike enemy_move_vertical.
enemy_move_horizontal 91F8 LD A,(IX+$00) backup old angular pos
91FB LD ($6033),A
91FE ADD A,(IX+$36) add speed, wrap mod 128
9201 AND $7F
9203 LD (IX+$00),A
9206 CALL check_actor_collision_a Check actor collision
9209 JR C,enemy_move_horizontal_0
920B CALL check_collisions Check collisions
920E JR NC,enemy_post_move
enemy_move_horizontal_0 9210 LD A,($6033) blocked: restore old position, reverse speed
9213 LD (IX+$00),A
9216 XOR A
9217 SUB (IX+$36)
921A LD (IX+$36),A
921D: Enemy post-move: hazard/attack dispatch
Used by the routines at enemy_move_dispatch2 and enemy_move_horizontal.
Shared post-movement dispatch: state==2 -> enemy_hazard_react (enemy_hazard_react). state==9 -> $91E9 (just refresh the level-tinted sprite, no movement -- an inert/spawned variant).
Otherwise this is the attack-pattern driver: indexes a per-enemy step table at $90CE by phase (+42). A zero entry means no attack step due yet -> enemy_attack_cooldown (enemy_attack_cooldown, phase++ up to $0A). A nonzero entry is a signed step consumed by enemy_attack_step (enemy_attack_step) to chain a dash/charge move.
enemy_post_move 921D LD A,(IX+$30) A = state (+48)
9220 CP $02 state==2 -> enemy_hazard_react
9222 JP Z,enemy_hazard_react
9225 CP $09 state==9 -> refresh level-tinted sprite only
9227 JP Z,enemy_move_vertical_2
922A LD E,(IX+$2A) phase -> index into attack-step table $90CE
922D LD D,$00
922F LD HL,$90CE
9232 ADD HL,DE
9233 LD A,(HL)
9234 OR A
9235 JP Z,enemy_attack_cooldown entry==0 -> no attack step due, cooldown
9238: Enemy attack dash step
One step of an attack dash/charge sequence, driven by the $90CE table entry for the current phase.
Applies the (sign-extended) step as a 16-bit delta to the enemy's vertical position (+6/+12), backing up the old value in 6034. Wrap past the tower bounds (bit7 of H) resets the enemy via enemy_bounds_reset.
Tests the new position (CHECK_ACTOR_COLLISION_A then CHECK_COLLISIONS); on a block, restores the old position and falls to enemy_hazard_react (enemy_attack_cooldown) instead of continuing the dash.
On success, re-examines the just-consumed table entry: $FF or $01 (a mid-dash unit step) loops back to consume the next table entry (re-enters at enemy_attack_step) any other value (the dash's terminating marker) branches to either finish the attack (state==3 -> set state=2, phase=0) or fall to enemy_attack_trigger to decide whether to trigger a fresh attack.
enemy_attack_step 9238 LD ($90D9),A
923B LD E,A
923C ADD A,A
923D SBC A,A
923E LD D,A
923F LD L,(IX+$06)
9242 LD H,(IX+$0C)
9245 LD ($6034),HL backup old position (16-bit)
9248 ADD HL,DE
9249 BIT 7,H
924B JP NZ,enemy_bounds_reset wrap -> enemy_bounds_reset (bounds reset)
924E LD (IX+$06),L
9251 LD A,H
9252 AND $03
9254 LD (IX+$0C),A
9257 CALL check_actor_collision_a Check actor collision
925A JR C,enemy_attack_step_0
925C CALL check_collisions Check collisions
925F JP NC,enemy_attack_cooldown
enemy_attack_step_0 9262 LD HL,($6034) blocked: restore old position
9265 LD (IX+$06),L
9268 LD (IX+$0C),H
926B LD A,($90D9)
926E OR A
926F JP M,enemy_attack_step_1
9272 DEC A
9273 JR NZ,enemy_attack_step
9275 JP enemy_attack_cooldown
enemy_attack_step_1 9278 INC A terminating step: A==1?
9279 JR NZ,enemy_attack_step
927B LD A,(IX+$30)
927E CP $03 state==3 -> finish attack (state=2, phase=0)
9280 JR NZ,enemy_attack_trigger
9282 LD (IX+$30),$02
9286 LD (IX+$2A),$00
928A JP enemy_next
928D: Enemy checks whether to launch an attack
Used by the routine at enemy_attack_step.
End of a completed (or not-yet-started) attack cycle: only fires when the enemy is currently stationary (speed +54 == 0).
Requires $60C2 nonzero, player_state==0 (normal play), and the player's tower position to exactly match this enemy's (+6/+12) -- i.e. same column.
If so, computes the angular direction D (+1/-1) from tower_height to the enemy and, if the angular distance is under $20 (close enough), stores D into speed (+54) -- launching the attack dash that enemy_attack_step will then drive frame by frame.
Always resets phase (+42)=0 and plays sound 1 (attack cue) before falling to the next enemy.
enemy_attack_trigger 928D LD A,(IX+$36) A = speed (+54)
9290 OR A
9291 JR NZ,enemy_attack_reset
9293 LD A,($60C2) require $60C2 and player_state==0
9296 OR A
9297 JR Z,enemy_attack_reset
9299 LD A,($60C0)
929C CP $00
929E JR NZ,enemy_attack_reset
92A0 LD A,($60BA) require player column matches (first axis)
92A3 CP (IX+$06)
92A6 JR NZ,enemy_attack_reset
92A8 LD A,($60BB) require player column matches (second axis)
92AB CP (IX+$0C)
92AE JR NZ,enemy_attack_reset
92B0 LD D,$FF
92B2 LD A,(IX+$00) compute chase direction from tower_height delta
92B5 LD HL,$60B9
92B8 SUB (HL)
92B9 AND $7F
92BB CP $40
92BD JR C,enemy_attack_trigger_0
92BF OR $80
enemy_attack_trigger_0 92C1 CP $80
92C3 JR C,enemy_attack_trigger_1
92C5 NEG
92C7 LD D,$01
enemy_attack_trigger_1 92C9 CP $20 too far (>=$20) -> skip trigger
92CB JR NC,enemy_attack_reset
92CD LD (IX+$36),D
92D0: Enemy attack: reset phase, play cue
Used by the routine at enemy_attack_trigger.
Common tail for enemy_attack_trigger: phase=0, play sound 1 (attack cue), next enemy.
enemy_attack_reset 92D0 LD (IX+$2A),$00
92D4 LD A,$01
92D6 CALL play_sound Play sound
92D9 JP enemy_next
92DC: Enemy attack cooldown countdown
Used by the routines at enemy_post_move and enemy_attack_step.
No attack step due this frame: increments phase (+42), clamped to a max of $0A, then next enemy.
enemy_attack_cooldown 92DC LD A,(IX+$2A)
92DF INC A
92E0 CP $0A
92E2 JR C,enemy_attack_cooldown_0
92E4 LD A,$0A
enemy_attack_cooldown_0 92E6 LD (IX+$2A),A
92E9 JP enemy_next
92EC: Enemy reacts to hazard cell
Used by the routine at enemy_post_move.
state 2 (also reached when enemy_attack_step is blocked): sets sprite id=$56, reads the tower-map cell under the enemy via read_actor_cell.
Cell type 0 -> nothing, next enemy. Type 1 -> enters state 3 with phase=$0A (a timed sub-state, presumably "stuck/retreating"). Any other type -> reverses speed (+54) sign and continues.
enemy_hazard_react 92EC LD A,$56 sprite id = $56
92EE LD (IX+$12),A
92F1 CALL read_actor_cell Read actor cell
92F4 OR A
92F5 JP Z,enemy_next cell type 0 -> nothing, next enemy
92F8 CP $01 cell type == 1?
92FA JR Z,enemy_hazard_react_0 -> type-1 handler ($9306)
92FC XOR A else: reverse speed sign
92FD SUB (IX+$36)
9300 LD (IX+$36),A
9303 JP enemy_next
enemy_hazard_react_0 9306 LD (IX+$30),$03 type 1: state=3, phase=$0A
930A LD (IX+$2A),$0A
930E JP enemy_next
9311: Enemy out-of-bounds reset
Used by the routines at enemy_move_vertical and enemy_attack_step.
Safety reset when a position update wraps past the tower's valid range (bit7 of the computed high byte): zeroes the enemy's position (+6/+12) and phase (+42), forces state=4 (idle/animate).
enemy_bounds_reset 9311 XOR A
9312 LD (IX+$06),A
9315 LD (IX+$0C),A
9318 LD (IX+$2A),A
931B LD (IX+$30),$04
931F: Next enemy
Used by the routines at update_enemies, enemy_transform, enemy_idle_check, enemy_idle_anim, enemy_move_vertical, enemy_attack_step, enemy_attack_reset, enemy_attack_cooldown and enemy_hazard_react.
Common per-enemy loop tail: advance IX to the next object_table slot, DJNZ back to update_enemies' outer loop, RET when all 4 are done.
enemy_next 931F INC IX
9321 POP BC
9322 DEC B
9323 JP NZ,update_enemies_0
9326 RET
9327: Unused
9327 DEFS $09
9330: Check rotate collision
Used by the routines at rotate_tower, step_scroll, check_collision and code_8a71.
Player-move collision probe.
Loads the self-mod probe params (column $9329=A, direction step $932A=$FD, span $932B/$932C, plus $9432/$9437) then runs the shared cell probe at $9395.
In: A=column.
Out: CY/flags from the probe.
check_rotate_collision 9330 LD ($9329),A
9333 LD A,$FD
9335 LD ($932A),A
9338 LD A,$02
933A LD ($932B),A
933D XOR A
933E LD ($932C),A
9341 LD ($9432),A
9344 LD A,$09
9346 LD ($9437),A
9349 JP run_cell_probe

9498 SRL H

949B POP HL
949C RET
949D: Read player cell
Used by the routine at pstate_play.
Compute and read the tower-map cell at the player's position (from $60BA via pos_to_col and tower_height), returning the cell contents for the pstate_play logic.

read_player_cell 949D LD HL,($60BA)

94A3 DEC A
94A4 LD L,A
94A5 LD A,($60B9) get Tower rotation position
94A8 ADD A,$7A

94AE SRL A

94B3 ADD A,$F0
94B5 LD H,A
94B6 LD C,$00
94B8 LD A,(HL)

94BA JP P,read_player_cell_0

94C3 LD C,$02
read_player_cell_0 94C5 POP AF
94C6 INC A
94C7 AND $0F
94C9 ADD A,$F0
94CB LD H,A
94CC LD A,(HL)
94CD OR A
94CE JP P,read_player_cell_1
94D1 AND $03
94D3 CP $03
94D5 JR Z,read_player_cell_1
94D7 INC C
read_player_cell_1 94D8 LD A,($60B9) get Tower rotation position
94DB AND $07
94DD ADD A,A

94DF OR C

9516: Player cell addr
Used by the routines at test_cell_special, check_conveyor, consume_cell and check_step_cell.
Return HL = address in the work_buffers tower map of the player's current cell (column from $60BA, row from tower_height>>3, page $F0+).
Clobbers: A,HL.
player_cell_addr 9516 LD HL,($60BA)
9519 CALL pos_to_col Pos to col
951C LD L,A
951D LD A,($60B9) get Tower rotation position
9520 SRL A
9522 SRL A
9524 SRL A
9526 ADD A,$F0
9528 LD H,A
9529 RET
952A: Test cell special
Used by the routine at pstate_play.
Read the cell under the player return CY=1 with A=its $40 flag when it is a 'special' cell (type bits == 3), else NC.
Full tower-map cell bitfield format: docs/Level-Format.md.
Clobbers: A,B,HL.
test_cell_special 952A CALL player_cell_addr
952D LD A,(HL)
952E OR A
952F JP P,test_cell_special_0
9532 LD B,A
9533 AND $03
9535 CP $03
9537 JR NZ,test_cell_special_0
9539 LD A,B
953A AND $40
953C SCF
953D RET
test_cell_special_0 953E OR A
953F RET
9540: Check conveyor
Used by the routines at pstate_play, pstate5_exit, pstate_6 and check_collision.
Read the cell left of the player if it is a conveyor/lift tile ($85 -> +1, $89 -> -1, $8D -> 0) return that direction in A with CY set, else NC.
Clobbers: A,HL.
check_conveyor 9540 CALL player_cell_addr
9543 DEC L
9544 LD A,(HL)
9545 CP $85
9547 JR Z,check_conveyor_0
9549 CP $89
954B JR Z,check_conveyor_1
954D SUB $8D
954F JR Z,check_conveyor_2
9551 OR A
9552 RET
check_conveyor_0 9553 LD A,$01
9555 SCF
9556 RET
check_conveyor_1 9557 LD A,$FF
check_conveyor_2 9559 SCF
955A RET
955B: Consume cell
Used by the routine at pstate_play.
If the cell left of the player is a collectable (type bits==1, subtype $10) remove it from the map (LD (HL),$00).
Clobbers: A,B,HL.
consume_cell 955B CALL player_cell_addr
955E DEC L
955F LD A,(HL)
9560 OR A
9561 JP P,consume_cell_0
9564 LD B,A
9565 AND $03
9567 CP $01
9569 JR NZ,consume_cell_0
956B LD A,B
956C AND $30
956E CP $10
9570 JR NZ,consume_cell_0
9572 LD (HL),$00
9574 LD A,$09
9576 CALL play_sound Play sound
consume_cell_0 9579 RET
957A: Check step cell
Used by the routine at rotate_if_idle.
Probe the cell left of the player for a step/ ledge (type bits==1, subtype $20/$30) and return its movement code (used by rotate_if_idle to auto-step).
Clobbers: A,B,HL.
check_step_cell 957A CALL player_cell_addr
957D DEC L
957E LD A,(HL)
957F OR A
9580 JP P,check_step_cell_1
9583 LD B,A
9584 AND $03
9586 CP $01
9588 JR NZ,check_step_cell_1
958A LD A,B
958B AND $30
958D CP $20
958F JR Z,check_step_cell_0
9591 CP $30
9593 JR NZ,check_step_cell_1
9595 LD A,$01
9597 SCF
9598 RET
check_step_cell_0 9599 LD A,$FF
959B SCF
959C RET
check_step_cell_1 959D OR A
959E RET
959F: Data block at 959F
959F DEFB $1C,$3E,$3E,$7F,$7F,$7F,$7F,$7F
95A7 DEFB $7F,$7F,$7F,$7F,$3E,$3E,$1C,$00
95AF DEFB $00,$1C,$1C
95B2 DEFM ">>>>>>>"
95B9: Data block at 95B9
95B9 DEFB $1C,$1C,$00,$00
95BD: Check collisions
Used by the routines at collision_test, enemy_move_vertical, enemy_move_horizontal, enemy_attack_step and update_actor_pos.
Test actor IX against the four object_table entries (IY walks object_table..) for an overlap, runs with interrupts off.
Per entry, skips to the next one ($9648: INC IY, next loop pass) when: IY==IX (self), or state (+48) is 0/4/6/7 (inactive/certain non-collidable states).
Otherwise does a 2-stage proximity test: angular distance |IX+0 - IY+0| (mod 128) must be small (<4, or >$7C i.e. wrapped small-negative) -- else skip; then vertical distance from the 16-bit position (IX+6/+12 vs IY+6/+12) must be within a small window (same page and delta<8, or one-page-back wrap with delta>=$F9) -- else skip.
On proximity, resolves a per-shape collision bitmask: if the target is object index 5 (the synthetic player record, see collision_test) it's an unconditional hit (SCF); if index 4, tests a bit in table $95AE; otherwise table 959F -- indexed by the angular delta, bit tested via RRA loop.
On a hit, exits via $9650 with CY=1 and HL=IY (colliding object's base pointer, since PUSH IY/POP HL runs before the final POP IY restores the caller's IY). No hit after all 4 entries: CY=0.
Skips comparing the actor with itself.
Clobbers: A,DE,HL,IY (IX preserved).
check_collisions 95BD DI DI; IY -> object_table[0]; B=4 outer loop
95BE PUSH IY
95C0 LD A,$04
95C2 LD IY,$605B
check_collisions_0 95C6 EX AF,AF' skip if IY==IX (self)
95C7 PUSH IX
95C9 PUSH IY
95CB POP DE
95CC POP HL
95CD LD A,H
95CE CP D
95CF JR NZ,check_collisions_1
95D1 LD A,L
95D2 CP E
95D3 JP Z,check_collisions_8
check_collisions_1 95D6 LD A,(IY+$30) A = target's state (+48)
95D9 CP $07 skip if state is 0/4/6/7 (non-collidable)
95DB JP Z,check_collisions_8
95DE CP $00
95E0 JP Z,check_collisions_8
95E3 CP $04
95E5 JP Z,check_collisions_8
95E8 CP $06
95EA JP Z,check_collisions_8
95ED LD A,(IX+$00) angular distance |IX+0 - IY+0| within window?
95F0 SUB (IY+$00)
95F3 AND $7F
95F5 CP $04
95F7 JR C,check_collisions_2
95F9 CP $7C
95FB JP C,check_collisions_8
check_collisions_2 95FE ADD A,$03
9600 AND $07
9602 LD B,A
9603 LD A,(IX+$06) vertical delta low byte (+6)
9606 SUB (IY+$06)
9609 LD L,A
960A LD A,(IX+$0C) vertical delta high byte (+12) &3
960D SBC A,(IY+$0C)
9610 AND $03
9612 JR NZ,check_collisions_3 same page: within 8 rows?
9614 LD A,L
9615 CP $08
9617 JR C,check_collisions_4
9619 JR check_collisions_8
check_collisions_3 961B CP $03 one-page-back wrap: within -7 rows?
961D JR NZ,check_collisions_8
961F LD A,L
9620 CP $F9
9622 JR C,check_collisions_8
check_collisions_4 9624 ADD A,$07 build delta+3 &7 -> table index E
9626 LD E,A
9627 LD D,$00
9629 PUSH IX object index = IX low - $5B
962B POP HL
962C LD A,L
962D SUB $5B index==5 (synthetic player)? unconditional hit
962F CP $05
9631 JR Z,check_collisions_7
9633 LD HL,$959F else pick shape table 959F/$95AE by index==4
9636 CP $04
9638 JR NZ,check_collisions_5
963A LD HL,$95AE
check_collisions_5 963D ADD HL,DE test bit E in shape table byte
963E LD A,(HL)
963F INC B
check_collisions_6 9640 RRA
9641 DJNZ check_collisions_6
9643 JR NC,check_collisions_8
check_collisions_7 9645 SCF
9646 JR check_collisions_9
check_collisions_8 9648 INC IY next object: INC IY, loop B
964A EX AF,AF'
964B DEC A
964C JP NZ,check_collisions_0
964F OR A
check_collisions_9 9650 PUSH IY exit: HL=IY (colliding object), EI, RET
9652 POP HL
9653 POP IY
9655 EI
9656 RET
9657: Read actor cell
Used by the routine at enemy_hazard_react.
Compute the tower-map cell address for actor IX (column IX+0>>3, position IX+6/IX+12) and read the cell there the actor counterpart of player_cell_addr.
Beyond the address/read, also classifies the result: tests the cell's type bits, then re-reads the cell one row above or below (direction chosen by the facing/movement bit at IX+54) to detect an edge. Returns A=0 (blocked/special), 1 (open) or 2 (edge in the movement direction) -- consumed by enemy_hazard_react's 0/1/else dispatch.
Clobbers: A,HL.
read_actor_cell 9657 LD A,(IX+$00) A = actor's angular column (IX+0)
965A RRA
965B RRA
965C RRA
965D AND $0F isolate row nibble
965F ADD A,$F0 bias to $F0.. row page
9661 LD H,A
9662 LD L,(IX+$06) L = actor's vertical pos low (IX+6)
9665 LD A,(IX+$0C)
9668 RRA
9669 RR L
966B RRA
966C RR L
966E DEC L L -= 1 (row above)
966F LD A,(HL) A = cell at (HL)
9670 OR A
9671 JP P,read_actor_cell_1 sign bit set? -> skip to $9685
9674 AND $03 mask cell type bits
9676 CP $01 type == 1?
9678 JR NZ,read_actor_cell_0
967A LD A,(HL)
967B AND $0C test sub-bits $0C
967D JR Z,read_actor_cell_6
967F JR read_actor_cell_5
read_actor_cell_0 9681 CP $03
9683 JR NZ,read_actor_cell_6
read_actor_cell_1 9685 LD A,(IX+$00) A = actor's angular column again
9688 AND $07 mask to row-in-column (0..7)
968A CP $02 row < 2? -> $9694 (near top)
968C JR C,read_actor_cell_2
968E CP $07 row < 7? -> $96CC (open, no edge)
9690 JR C,read_actor_cell_4
9692 JR read_actor_cell_3
read_actor_cell_2 9694 LD A,H A = row-page byte (H)
9695 SUB $F0
9697 DEC A row -= 1 (check row above)
9698 AND $0F
969A ADD A,$F0
969C LD H,A
969D LD A,(HL) A = cell above
969E OR A
969F JP P,read_actor_cell_4
96A2 AND $03
96A4 CP $03
96A6 JR Z,read_actor_cell_4
96A8 BIT 7,(IX+$36) test facing/direction bit (IX+54)
96AC JR Z,read_actor_cell_5
96AE JR read_actor_cell_6
read_actor_cell_3 96B0 LD A,H A = row-page byte (H)
96B1 SUB $F0
96B3 INC A row += 1 (check row below)
96B4 AND $0F
96B6 ADD A,$F0
96B8 LD H,A
96B9 LD A,(HL) A = cell below
96BA OR A
96BB JP P,read_actor_cell_4
96BE AND $03
96C0 CP $03
96C2 JR Z,read_actor_cell_4
96C4 BIT 7,(IX+$36) test facing/direction bit (IX+54)
96C8 JR NZ,read_actor_cell_5
96CA JR read_actor_cell_6
read_actor_cell_4 96CC LD A,$01 return 1 (open/no obstruction)
96CE RET
read_actor_cell_5 96CF LD A,$02 return 2 (edge in movement direction)
96D1 RET
read_actor_cell_6 96D2 XOR A return 0 (blocked/special)
96D3 RET
96D4: Routine at 96D4
Used by the routine at setup_level.
reset_actor_phase 96D4 LD IX,$605F
96D8 LD (IX+$2A),$FF
96DC RET
96DD: Data block at 96DD
move_curve 96DD DEFB $00,$01,$00,$01,$00,$01,$00,$00
96E5 DEFB $01,$00,$00,$00
96E9: Update actor pos
Used by the routine at main_tick.
Advance one actor's position around/along the tower for this frame.
IX = actor_obj (actor_obj).
Fields used: (IX+0) angular column around the tower (accumulated mod 128) (IX+6) vertical position, low (sub-pixel) (IX+12) vertical position, high (0..3) (IX+18) per-frame scratch, cleared on entry (IX+42) animation/move phase 0..$0C ($FF = inactive/done) (IX+48) actor state (2,4,5,8,9 -- see transitions below) (IX+54)
update_actor_pos 96E9 LD IX,$605F IX -> actor struct base (actor_obj)
96ED LD (IX+$12),$00 clear per-frame scratch field (+18)
96F1 LD A,(IX+$2A) A = phase (+42)
96F4 CP $FF $FF = inactive -> nothing to do
96F6 RET Z
96F7 CP $0C phase $0C = end of move -> mark done ($FF)
96F9 JR NZ,update_actor_pos_1
update_actor_pos_0 96FB LD (IX+$2A),$FF
96FF RET
update_actor_pos_1 9700 LD E,A E = phase (table index)
9701 INC (IX+$2A) advance phase
9704 LD A,(IX+$36) A = speed(+54) * 2
9707 ADD A,A
9708 ADD A,(IX+$00) + angular pos (+0)
970B AND $7F wrap to 0..127 (around the tower)
970D LD (IX+$00),A store new angular column (+0)
9710 LD D,$00
9712 LD HL,$96DD HL -> move_curve[phase]
9715 ADD HL,DE
9716 LD A,(IX+$06) add curve delta to vertical-low (+6)
9719 ADD A,(HL)
971A LD (IX+$06),A
971D LD A,(IX+$0C) carry into vertical-high (+12), keep 0..3
9720 ADC A,$00
9722 AND $03
9724 LD (IX+$0C),A
9727 CALL check_actor_collision_b collision test (carry set = blocked)
972A JR C,update_actor_pos_0 blocked -> mark move done
972C CALL check_collisions Check collisions
972F RET NC
9730 PUSH IX
9732 PUSH HL
9733 POP IX
9735 LD A,(IX+$30) branch on actor state (+48)
9738 CP $05
973A JR Z,update_actor_pos_3
973C CP $08
973E JR Z,update_actor_pos_3
9740 CP $09
9742 JR Z,update_actor_pos_3
9744 CP $02
9746 JR NZ,update_actor_pos_2
9748 LD (IX+$2A),$4B state 2: arm phase=$4B, state=5
974C LD (IX+$30),$05
9750 JR update_actor_pos_3
update_actor_pos_2 9752 LD (IX+$30),$04 default: state=4, phase=0
9756 LD (IX+$2A),$00
975A LD HL,$0100
975D CALL jp_add_score
9760 LD A,$05
9762 CALL jp_field_604f_add
9765 LD A,$09
9767 CALL play_sound Play sound
update_actor_pos_3 976A POP IX
976C JP update_actor_pos_0
976F: TEXT_STRINGS
Menu / level-name / status text, 976F..$9952.
Each entry is [colour-control byte <$10] + ASCII + 00.
Rendered by print_string / print_centered.
976F DEFM $0E,">> GAME ON <<",$00
977F DEFM $0D,"PLAYER ONE",$00
978B DEFM $0D,"PLAYER TWO",$00
9797 DEFM $07,"ENTERING THE",$00
97A6 DEFM $0F,"TOWER OF EYES",$00
97B5 DEFM $0F,"REALM OF ROBOTS",$00
97C6 DEFM $0F,"TRAP OF TRICKS",$00
97D6 DEFM $0F,"SLIPPERY SLIDE",$00
97E6 DEFM $0F,"BROKEN PATH",$00
97F3 DEFM $0F,"SWIMMERS DELIGHT",$00
9805 DEFM $0F,"NASTY ONE",$00
9810 DEFM $0F,"EDGE OF DOOM",$00
981E DEFM $0E,">> GAME OVER <<",$00
982F DEFM $0D,"NO LIVES LEFT",$00
983E DEFM $0F,"TOWER COMPLETED",$00
9850 DEFM $07,"BONUS POINTS",$00
985E DEFM $0E,"TIME LEFT 10*999",$00
9870 DEFM $0D,"TECHNIQUE 10*999",$00
9882 DEFM $0C,"EXTRAS 10*999",$00
9894 DEFM $0F,"CONGRATULATIONS",$00
98A5 DEFM $07,"YOU HAVE",$00
98AF DEFM $07,"COMPLETED",$00
98BA DEFM $07,"YOUR MISSION",$00
98C8 DEFM $06,"WRITTEN BY",$00
98D4 DEFM $07,"J M PHILLIPS",$00
98E2 DEFM $0F,"@1987 HEWSON",$00
98F0 DEFM $0D,"1 PLAYER",$00
98FA DEFM $0C,"2 PLAYER",$00
9904 DEFM $0D,"SOUND ON",$00
990E DEFM $0C,"NO SOUND",$00
9918 DEFM $07,"TIME LIMIT UP",$00
9927 DEFM $07,"HIGH SCORERS",$00
9935 DEFM $0B,"PLEASE ENTER",$00
9943 DEFM $0B,"YOUR INITIALS",$00
9953: Data block at 9953
tbl_level_name 9953 DEFW $97A6,$97B5,$97C6,$97D6
995B DEFW $97E6,$97F3,$9805,$9810
9963: Show level intro
Used by the routine at level_retry.
show_level_intro 9963 CALL title_anim_tick Play title animation
9966 LD A,$01 Start music ID 1
9968 CALL start_music Start music
996B LD A,$07 Print ">> GAME ON <<" at row $07
996D LD HL,$976F
9970 CALL print_string_at Print string at

9975 CALL set_print_row Set print row
9978 CALL print_player_label Print PLAYER label

997D LD HL,$9797
9980 CALL print_string_at Print string at

9986 ADD A,A Double for word index into level name table
9987 LD E,A
9988 LD D,$00
998A LD HL,$9953 Point to level name pointer table (tbl_level_name)
998D ADD HL,DE Add level number index
998E LD E,(HL) Fetch low byte of level name address
998F INC HL
9990 LD D,(HL) Fetch high byte
9991 EX DE,HL Swap to HL
9992 LD A,$0E Print level name at row $0E
9994 CALL print_string_at Print string at
show_level_intro_0 9997 CALL music_tick Music tick
999A JR NC,show_level_intro_0
999C LD A,$19 Jump to wait_frames (wait_frames) with A=$19
999E JP wait_frames_0

Used by the routine at main_loop.
Render an animated info/message screen: tick the title animation, (re)start jingle 2, and print the screen's text lines (strings at 981E..) via print_string_at, running the music sequencer.
Clobbers: most.
draw_info_screen_a 99A1 CALL title_anim_tick
99A4 LD A,$02
99A6 CALL start_music Start music
99A9 LD A,$0A
99AB LD HL,$981E
99AE CALL print_string_at Print string at
99B1 LD A,$0E
99B3 CALL set_print_row Set print row
99B6 CALL print_player_label Print PLAYER label
99B9 LD A,$12
99BB LD HL,$982F
99BE CALL print_string_at Print string at
draw_info_screen_a_0 99C1 CALL music_tick Music tick
99C4 JR NC,draw_info_screen_a_0
99C6 LD A,$19
99C8 CALL wait_frames Wait frames
99CB JP enter_hiscore_name

Used by the routine at main_loop.

99D3 CALL start_music Start music
99D6 LD A,$0C

99FB LD A,$0B

9A23: Screen time bonus
Used by the routine at main_loop.
End-of-level bonus screen: animate the remaining play_timer counting down (update_timer_digits + blips) with wait_frames pacing, converting leftover time into score.
screen_time_bonus 9A23 CALL title_anim_tick
9A26 CALL update_timer_digits
9A29 LD A,$19
9A2B CALL wait_frames Wait frames
9A2E LD A,($6053) get Level countdown timer
9A31 AND $F0
9A33 LD ($6053),A set Level countdown timer

9A40 SUB $10
9A42 DAA

9A46 LD A,($6054)
9A49 SBC A,$00
9A4B DAA
9A4C LD ($6054),A

9A52 CALL jp_draw_timer
9A55 LD HL,$0014
9A58 CALL jp_add_score
9A5B CALL jp_draw_hud
9A5E JP screen_time_bonus_0

9B87: Data block at 9B87
menu_page 9B87 DEFB $00,$BC,$01
9B8A: Title menu
Used by the routine at level_retry.
Title screen, option menu and attract/demo loop.
(TASK.md guessed 'init level data' here -- WRONG verified to be the front-end.) Blits the 24-row NEBULUS logo bitmap from rt_be00 ($BE00) into the screen, paints attribute rows, and prints the option lines (1/2 PLAYER etc.) via draw_menu_text.
Runs proc_table as an attract demo until input, then polls the keyboard ($FE) and Kempston ($1F) for select/start.
Entry: ticks the background animation once, starts music 0, resets the page/timeout/active flags (menu_page=0 main menu page, $9B88=$C0 attract timeout, $9B89=0 not-yet-active), falls into title_menu_draw.
menu_page
title_menu 9B8A CALL title_anim_tick tick background animation once
9B8D LD A,$00 A = tune 0
9B8F CALL start_music Start music
9B92 XOR A clear menu_page (menu_page=0, main menu)
9B93 LD ($9B87),A
9B96 LD A,$C0 attract_timer = $C0
9B98 LD ($9B88),A
9B9B XOR A menu_active = 0
9B9C LD ($9B89),A
9B9F JP title_menu_draw
9BA2: Title menu redraw anim
Used by the routine at title_menu_draw.
Re-ticks the background animation (falls straight into title_menu_draw) -- the re-entry point used when the attract timer flips between the menu page and the hiscore page.
title_menu_redraw_anim 9BA2 CALL jp_clear_playfield
9BA5: Title menu draw
Used by the routine at title_menu.
Draws the current page: if menu_page (menu_page)!=0, shows the hiscore table (draw_hiscore_screen + draw_menu_text for the CONTROLS/SOUND lines) and skips straight to the input poll ($9C23).
Otherwise draws the main menu: blits the 24-row NEBULUS logo from $BE00 in 3 vertical thirds (A=0/8/16), each blitting 16 columns x 7 source bytes/column via jp_get_screen_addr paints 3 header attribute rows ($58C8/$58E8/$5908, colours $47/$46/$45) via fill_attr_row prints the 3 option lines ("1 PLAYER" etc, strings at 98C8/98D4/98E2) at rows $0C/$0E/$10 then draw_menu_text for the CONTROLS/SOUND state lines.
title_menu_draw 9BA5 LD A,($9B87)
9BA8 OR A
9BA9 JR Z,title_menu_draw_0
9BAB CALL draw_hiscore_screen Draw hiscore screen
9BAE CALL draw_menu_text Draw menu text
9BB1 JP title_menu_draw_3
title_menu_draw_0 9BB4 LD DE,$BE00 DE -> logo source ($BE00)
9BB7 LD A,$00 A = 0 (vertical third counter)
title_menu_draw_1 9BB9 PUSH AF
9BBA ADD A,$30 row/page base = $30+A
9BBC LD H,A
9BBD LD L,$08
9BBF CALL jp_get_screen_addr jp_get_screen_addr
9BC2 LD B,$10 B = 16 (columns this third)
title_menu_draw_2 9BC4 PUSH HL
9BC5 LD A,(DE)
9BC6 LD (HL),A
9BC7 INC E
9BC8 INC H
9BC9 LD A,(DE)
9BCA LD (HL),A
9BCB INC E
9BCC INC H
9BCD LD A,(DE)
9BCE LD (HL),A
9BCF INC E
9BD0 INC H
9BD1 LD A,(DE)
9BD2 LD (HL),A
9BD3 INC E
9BD4 INC H
9BD5 LD A,(DE)
9BD6 LD (HL),A
9BD7 INC E
9BD8 INC H
9BD9 LD A,(DE)
9BDA LD (HL),A
9BDB INC E
9BDC INC H
9BDD LD A,(DE)
9BDE LD (HL),A
9BDF INC E
9BE0 INC H
9BE1 LD A,(DE)
9BE2 LD (HL),A
9BE3 INC DE
9BE4 POP HL
9BE5 INC L
9BE6 DJNZ title_menu_draw_2 loop 16 columns
9BE8 POP AF
9BE9 ADD A,$08 A += 8 (next vertical third)
9BEB CP $18 done all 3 thirds?
9BED JP NZ,title_menu_draw_1
9BF0 LD HL,$58C8
9BF3 LD (HL),$47 paint header attr row 1 ($47)
9BF5 CALL fill_attr_row Fill attr row
9BF8 LD HL,$58E8
9BFB LD (HL),$46 paint header attr row 2 ($46)
9BFD CALL fill_attr_row Fill attr row
9C00 LD HL,$5908
9C03 LD (HL),$45 paint header attr row 3 ($45)
9C05 CALL fill_attr_row Fill attr row
9C08 LD A,$0C
9C0A LD HL,$98C8 HL -> "1 PLAYER"-ish option string
9C0D CALL print_string_at Print string at
9C10 LD A,$0E
9C12 LD HL,$98D4
9C15 CALL print_string_at Print string at
9C18 LD A,$10
9C1A LD HL,$98E2
9C1D CALL print_string_at Print string at
9C20 CALL draw_menu_text Draw menu text
title_menu_draw_3 9C23 LD A,($9B89) menu_active flag
9C26 OR A
9C27 JR NZ,title_menu_draw_5
title_menu_draw_4 9C29 CALL proc_table Proc table
9C2C LD A,($601C) get Signed input
9C2F OR A
9C30 JR NZ,title_menu_draw_4
title_menu_draw_5 9C32 LD A,$01 menu_active = 1
9C34 LD ($9B89),A
9C37 CALL music_tick Music tick
9C3A XOR A
9C3B IN A,($FE) A = keyboard row $FE, inverted, masked
9C3D CPL
9C3E AND $1F
9C40 JR NZ,title_menu_draw_6
9C42 IN A,($1F) nothing on keyboard -> check Kempston
9C44 AND $1F Kempston: any direction or fire?
9C46 CP $1F
9C48 JR Z,title_menu_draw_11
9C4A AND $10
9C4C JR Z,title_menu_draw_11
title_menu_draw_6 9C4E LD A,$00 A = 0 (idle option index)
title_menu_draw_7 9C50 LD ($6017),A set Game mode
9C53 CALL proc_table Proc table
9C56 LD A,($601C) get Signed input
9C59 OR A horizontal input? -> release-wait ($9D07)
9C5A JP NZ,menu_wait_release
9C5D LD A,($6017) get Game mode
9C60 INC A advance/wrap option index 0..4
9C61 CP $05
9C63 JR NZ,title_menu_draw_7
9C65 LD A,$24 A = key $24 ('1')
9C67 CALL jp_test_key test_key
9C6A JR Z,title_menu_draw_8 pressed? -> 1-player start
9C6C LD A,$01 A = 1 (running_flag: 1 player)
9C6E LD ($603F),A set Running flag
9C71 JP title_menu_draw restart into game (title_menu_draw draw path with running_flag set)
title_menu_draw_8 9C74 LD A,$1C A = key $1C ('2')
9C76 CALL jp_test_key test_key
9C79 JR Z,title_menu_draw_9 pressed? -> 2-player start
9C7B LD A,$02 A = 2 (running_flag: 2 player)
9C7D LD ($603F),A set Running flag
9C80 JP title_menu_draw
title_menu_draw_9 9C83 LD A,$14 A = key $14 (controls toggle)
9C85 CALL jp_test_key test_key
9C88 JR Z,title_menu_draw_10
9C8A LD HL,$6056 HL -> $6056 (sound-enable/control flag)
9C8D BIT 7,(HL) test bit 7
9C8F JR NZ,title_menu_draw_10
9C91 LD (HL),$80 set bit 7 (toggle on)
9C93 JP title_menu_draw
title_menu_draw_10 9C96 LD A,$0C A = key $0C (controls toggle off)
9C98 CALL jp_test_key test_key
9C9B JR Z,title_menu_draw_11
9C9D LD HL,$6056 HL -> $6056
9CA0 BIT 7,(HL) test bit 7
9CA2 JR Z,title_menu_draw_11
9CA4 LD (HL),$00 clear bit 7 (toggle off)
9CA6 JP title_menu_draw
title_menu_draw_11 9CA9 LD HL,$9B88 HL -> attract_timer
9CAC DEC (HL) decrement
9CAD JR NZ,title_menu_draw_5 not yet expired -> keep polling
9CAF LD A,$01 A = 1
9CB1 LD HL,$9B87
9CB4 SUB (HL) flip menu_page (1-current)
9CB5 LD (HL),A
9CB6 LD A,$C0 reload attract_timer = $C0
9CB8 LD ($9B88),A
9CBB JP title_menu_redraw_anim redraw the other page
9CBE: Fill attr row
Used by the routine at title_menu_draw.
Flood-fill 16 bytes starting at HL with the byte already stored at (HL) (DE=HL+1, LDIR propagates it forward) -- paints one attribute row a single flat colour.
In: HL=start address (byte already set), fills 16 bytes total.
fill_attr_row 9CBE LD E,L DE = HL+1
9CBF LD D,H
9CC0 INC DE
9CC1 LD BC,$000F BC = 15 (remaining bytes)
9CC4 LDIR LDIR: propagate the seed byte forward
9CC6 RET
9CC7: Draw menu text
Used by the routine at title_menu_draw.
Prints the two state-dependent menu lines: CONTROLS (row $15, text depends on running_flag==1 vs other -- 1P vs 2P control scheme) and SOUND (row $17, text depends on $6056 bit7 -- on/off).
draw_menu_text 9CC7 LD A,($603F) A = running_flag
9CCA CP $01 ==1 (single player)?
9CCC LD A,$15
9CCE LD HL,$98F0
9CD1 JR Z,draw_menu_text_0
9CD3 LD HL,$98FA
draw_menu_text_0 9CD6 CALL print_string_at Print string at
9CD9 LD A,($6056) A = sound-enable flag ($6056)
9CDC OR A
9CDD LD A,$17
9CDF LD HL,$990E HL -> "on" text
9CE2 JR Z,draw_menu_text_1
9CE4 LD HL,$9904 HL -> "off" text (if flag clear)
draw_menu_text_1 9CE7 CALL print_string_at Print string at
9CEA RET
9CEB: Title anim tick
Used by the routines at show_level_intro, draw_info_screen_a, draw_timeup_screen, draw_info_screen_b, screen_time_bonus, title_menu and enter_hiscore_name.
One-instruction wrapper around jp_anim_bg ($A778) -- ticks the title background animation.
title_anim_tick 9CEB CALL jp_clear_playfield
9CEE RET
9CEF: Unused
9CEF DEFS $01
9CF0: Wait frames
Used by the routines at draw_info_screen_a, draw_timeup_screen, draw_info_screen_b and screen_time_bonus.
Pause for A frames (HALT-synced), running the attract animation (proc_table) meanwhile returns early if input (input_x) arrives.
9CEF = frame counter.
Clobbers: A,HL.
wait_frames 9CF0 HALT
This entry point is used by the routine at show_level_intro.
wait_frames_0 9CF1 LD ($9CEF),A
9CF4 CALL menu_wait_release
wait_frames_1 9CF7 HALT
9CF8 LD HL,$9CEF
9CFB DEC (HL)
9CFC JR Z,wait_frames_2
9CFE CALL proc_table Proc table
9D01 LD A,($601C) get Signed input
9D04 OR A
9D05 JR Z,wait_frames_1
This entry point is used by the routine at title_menu_draw.
menu_wait_release 9D07 CALL proc_table Proc table
9D0A LD A,($601C) get Signed input
9D0D OR A
9D0E JR NZ,menu_wait_release
wait_frames_2 9D10 RET
9D11: Print PLAYER label
Used by the routines at show_level_intro, draw_info_screen_a, draw_timeup_screen and draw_info_screen_b.
Prints "PLAYER 1" or "PLAYER 2" (strings at 977F/978B) depending on lives_p1 (lives_p1).
print_player_label 9D11 LD A,($6040) A = lives_p1 (active player index)
9D14 LD HL,$977F
9D17 OR A ==0? player 1
9D18 JR Z,print_player_label_0
9D1A LD HL,$978B else HL -> "PLAYER 2" string
print_player_label_0 9D1D CALL jp_print_centered jp_print_centered
9D20 RET
9D21: Print string at
Used by the routines at show_level_intro, draw_info_screen_a, draw_timeup_screen, draw_info_screen_b, update_timer_digits, title_menu_draw, draw_menu_text, draw_hiscore_screen and enter_hiscore_name.
Set the print row (A) then print the string at HL, centered. Thin wrapper combining set_print_row + jp_print_centered.
print_string_at 9D21 CALL set_print_row set_print_row
9D24 JP jp_print_centered jp_print_centered (tail call)
9D27: Set print row
Used by the routines at show_level_intro, draw_info_screen_a, draw_timeup_screen, draw_info_screen_b and print_string_at.
Set print_cursor's row to A and column to 0.
set_print_row 9D27 LD ($60C6),A store row into print_cursor high byte
9D2A XOR A A = 0
9D2B LD ($60C7),A store column = 0
9D2E RET
9D2F: Data block at 9D2F
Default hiscore table, 4 entries x 7 bytes (4 BCD score bytes + 3-char ASCII name). All 4 ship with the same "JMP" initials (programmer's easter egg) and descending round scores (hiscore_entry_1 highest). Live-updated in place by check_hiscore (check_hiscore) as real scores qualify -- not reloaded from tape, so scores reset each time the game is loaded.
hiscore_entry_1 9D2F DEFB $40,$26,$04,$00
9D33 DEFM "JMP"
9D36: Data block at 9D36
hiscore_entry_2 9D36 DEFB $00,$50,$00,$00
9D3A DEFM "JMP"
9D3D: Data block at 9D3D
hiscore_entry_3 9D3D DEFB $00,$30,$00,$00
9D41 DEFM "JMP"
9D44: Data block at 9D44
hiscore_entry_4 9D44 DEFB $00,$10,$00,$00
9D48 DEFM "JMP"
9D4B: Unused
9D4B DEFS $01
9D4C: Draw hiscore screen
Used by the routines at title_menu_draw and enter_hiscore_name.
Render the high-score table (the alternate title page): header string 9927 plus each score entry printed via print_score_entry.
Clobbers: most.
draw_hiscore_screen 9D4C LD A,$06 A = row $06 (header)
9D4E LD HL,$9927 HL -> header string (9927)
9D51 CALL print_string_at Print string at
9D54 LD HL,$9D2F HL -> hiscore_entry_1
9D57 LD C,$47 C = colour $47
9D59 LD A,$0A A = row $0A
9D5B CALL print_score_entry Print score entry
9D5E LD HL,$9D36 HL -> hiscore_entry_2
9D61 LD C,$46 C = colour $46
9D63 LD A,$0C A = row $0C
9D65 CALL print_score_entry Print score entry
9D68 LD HL,$9D3D HL -> hiscore_entry_3
9D6B LD C,$45 C = colour $45
9D6D LD A,$0E A = row $0E
9D6F CALL print_score_entry Print score entry
9D72 LD HL,$9D44 HL -> hiscore_entry_4
9D75 LD C,$44 C = colour $44
9D77 LD A,$10 A = row $10
9D79 CALL print_score_entry Print score entry
9D7C RET
9D7D: Unused
9D7D DEFS $03
9D80: Enter HiScore name
Used by the routines at draw_info_screen_a and draw_info_screen_b.
"Type your initials" screen, entered from the time-up/bonus screens (draw_info_screen_a/draw_info_screen_b) after a level. Checks check_hiscore first RET NC immediately if the active player's score didn't qualify.
On a qualifying score: seeds the new table row's 3-char name with "A " (IX+4/5/6), stashes the row pointer at $9D7E and the letter-position counter (4/5/6, one per char) at 9D7D=4, then redraws the hiscore screen with prompt text and enters an input loop.
Per letter: waits for horizontal input release, then a fire/movement poll. Vertical/horizontal movement (input_x, added to the current letter) cycles the letter A-Z (wrapping $40/$5B boundaries) fire or continued input confirms the letter and advances the position counter, defaulting the next letter to the same value as the one just confirmed. After all 3 letters (counter reaches 7), pauses ~25 frames and returns.
Clobbers: most.
enter_hiscore_name 9D80 CALL check_hiscore check_hiscore
9D83 RET NC not a qualifying score -> RET
9D84 LD (IX+$04),$41 default letter 1 = 'A'
9D88 LD (IX+$05),$20 default letter 2 = ' '
9D8C LD (IX+$06),$20 default letter 3 = ' '
9D90 LD ($9D7E),IX stash row pointer ($9D7E)
9D94 LD A,$04 A = 4 (first letter-position value)
9D96 LD ($9D7D),A stash letter-position counter (9D7D)
9D99 CALL title_anim_tick Title anim tick
enter_hiscore_name_0 9D9C CALL draw_hiscore_screen Draw hiscore screen
9D9F LD A,$15 A = row $15 (prompt line 1)
9DA1 LD HL,$9935 HL -> prompt string
9DA4 CALL print_string_at Print string at
9DA7 LD A,$17 A = row $17 (prompt line 2)
9DA9 LD HL,$9943 HL -> prompt string
9DAC CALL print_string_at Print string at
9DAF HALT HALT (sync)
enter_hiscore_name_1 9DB0 CALL proc_table Proc table
9DB3 LD A,($601C) get Signed input
9DB6 OR A
9DB7 JR NZ,enter_hiscore_name_1 horizontal input still held -> wait for release
enter_hiscore_name_2 9DB9 LD B,$06 B = 6 (debounce frames)
enter_hiscore_name_3 9DBB HALT
9DBC DJNZ enter_hiscore_name_3 loop 6 HALTs
9DBE CALL proc_table Proc table
9DC1 LD A,($601E) get Signed input
9DC4 LD HL,$601C
9DC7 OR (HL) fire or horizontal input? else keep waiting
9DC8 JR Z,enter_hiscore_name_2
9DCA LD A,($601E) get Signed input
9DCD OR A
9DCE JR Z,enter_hiscore_name_6 no vertical input -> confirm letter ($9DEB)
9DD0 LD IX,($9D7E) IX -> current letter slot
9DD4 ADD A,(IX+$04) A = input_y + current letter
9DD7 CP $40 wrapped below 'A'? -> wrap to 'Z'
9DD9 JR NZ,enter_hiscore_name_4
9DDB LD A,$5A A = 'Z'
9DDD JR enter_hiscore_name_5
enter_hiscore_name_4 9DDF CP $5B wrapped past 'Z'? -> wrap to 'A'
9DE1 JR NZ,enter_hiscore_name_5
9DE3 LD A,$41 A = 'A'
enter_hiscore_name_5 9DE5 LD (IX+$04),A store adjusted letter
9DE8 JP enter_hiscore_name_0 loop (redraw with new letter)
enter_hiscore_name_6 9DEB LD IX,($9D7E) IX -> current letter slot
9DEF INC IX IX += 1 (advance to next letter)
9DF1 LD ($9D7E),IX store advanced pointer
9DF5 LD A,($9D7D) A = letter-position counter
9DF8 INC A
9DF9 LD ($9D7D),A store advanced counter
9DFC CP $07 all 3 letters entered (counter==7)?
9DFE JR Z,enter_hiscore_name_7
9E00 LD A,(IX+$03) else: A = previous letter's final value
9E03 LD (IX+$04),A seed next letter with the same value
9E06 JP enter_hiscore_name_0 loop (redraw for next letter)
enter_hiscore_name_7 9E09 LD B,$19 B = $19 (pause frames)
enter_hiscore_name_8 9E0B HALT HALT
9E0C DJNZ enter_hiscore_name_8 loop pause frames
9E0E RET
9E0F: Print score entry
Used by the routine at draw_hiscore_screen.
Print one high-score row: cursor row A, colour C, entry record at HL (IX) formats the BCD score bytes via print_score_byte.
Clobbers: A,IX,...
print_score_entry 9E0F LD ($60C6),A
9E12 LD A,C
9E13 LD ($60C8),A set Current text colour/attribute
9E16 LD A,$09
9E18 LD ($60C7),A
9E1B PUSH HL
9E1C POP IX
9E1E LD A,(IX+$03)
9E21 CALL print_score_byte
9E24 LD A,(IX+$02)
9E27 CALL print_score_byte
9E2A LD A,(IX+$01)
9E2D CALL print_score_byte
9E30 LD A,(IX+$00)
9E33 CALL print_score_byte
9E36 LD A,$20
9E38 CALL jp_print_char
9E3B LD A,$20
9E3D CALL jp_print_char
9E40 LD A,$20
9E42 CALL jp_print_char
9E45 LD A,(IX+$04)
9E48 CALL jp_print_char
9E4B LD A,(IX+$05)
9E4E CALL jp_print_char
9E51 LD A,(IX+$06)
9E54 CALL jp_print_char
9E57 RET
9E58: Routine at 9E58
Used by the routine at print_score_entry.
print_score_byte 9E58 PUSH AF
9E59 SRL A
9E5B SRL A
9E5D SRL A
9E5F SRL A
9E61 CALL print_score_nibble
9E64 POP AF
print_score_nibble 9E65 AND $0F
9E67 ADD A,$30
9E69 JP jp_print_char
9E6C: Unused
9E6C DEFS $01
9E6D: Check hiscore
Used by the routine at enter_hiscore_name.
Compare the active player's 4-byte BCD score ($6043 / $6047 by lives_p1) against the high-score table and insert it in rank order if it qualifies.
Runs with IRQs off.
Clobbers: A,IX,IY,...
check_hiscore 9E6D DI DI
9E6E PUSH IY
9E70 LD A,($6040) get Lives
9E73 LD IX,$6043
9E77 OR A player index==0? select score buffer
9E78 JR Z,check_hiscore_0
9E7A LD IX,$6047 else IX -> P2 score ($6047)
check_hiscore_0 9E7E LD IY,$9D2F IY -> hiscore table (hiscore_entry_1)
9E82 LD B,$04 B = 4 (BCD score bytes)
check_hiscore_1 9E84 LD A,(IY+$00) A = table entry byte
9E87 SUB (IX+$00) subtract score byte (with carry chain)
9E8A LD A,(IY+$01)
9E8D SBC A,(IX+$01)
9E90 LD A,(IY+$02)
9E93 SBC A,(IX+$02)
9E96 LD A,(IY+$03)
9E99 SBC A,(IX+$03)
9E9C JR C,check_hiscore_2 table entry >= score? -> next entry
9E9E LD DE,$0007
9EA1 ADD IY,DE IY += 7 (next table row)
9EA3 DJNZ check_hiscore_1 loop 4 bytes; no qualifying row -> fall through
9EA5 POP IY restore IY
9EA7 EI EI
9EA8 OR A
9EA9 RET RET (no new high score)
check_hiscore_2 9EAA PUSH IY save insertion point (IY) into DE
9EAC POP DE
9EAD LD IY,$9D44 IY -> end of hiscore table (hiscore_entry_4)
check_hiscore_3 9EB1 PUSH IY
9EB3 POP HL
9EB4 LD A,L A = low byte of IY
9EB5 CP E
9EB6 JR NZ,check_hiscore_4 not yet at insertion point? keep shifting
9EB8 LD A,H
9EB9 CP D
9EBA JR Z,check_hiscore_6 reached insertion point -> store new score
check_hiscore_4 9EBC LD B,$07 B = 7 (bytes per table row)
check_hiscore_5 9EBE DEC IY shift row down: IY -= 1
9EC0 LD A,(IY+$00) A = byte to shift
9EC3 INC IY
9EC5 LD (IY+$06),A store into next row (+6)
9EC8 DEC IY
9ECA DJNZ check_hiscore_5 loop 7 bytes; then next row down
9ECC JP check_hiscore_3
check_hiscore_6 9ECF LD A,(IX+$00) A = new score byte (IX)
9ED2 LD (IY+$00),A store into table row (IY)
9ED5 LD A,(IX+$01)
9ED8 LD (IY+$01),A
9EDB LD A,(IX+$02)
9EDE LD (IY+$02),A
9EE1 LD A,(IX+$03)
9EE4 LD (IY+$03),A
9EE7 PUSH IY IY -> IX (restore active player's score ptr)
9EE9 POP IX
9EEB POP IY restore IY
9EED EI EI
9EEE SCF SCF (new high score inserted)
9EEF RET
9EF0: Unused
9EF0 DEFS $01
9EF1 DEFM "yvwxpxhx"
9EF9: Data block at 9EF9
9EF9 DEFB $88,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$68,$7C
9F01 DEFB $90,$90,$90
9F04 DEFM "xpphhyzyzy"
9F0E: Unused
9F0E DEFS $03
9F11: Init attract actors
Used by the routine at setup_level.
Set up the attract/demo actor records at 9EF0 (copy 5-byte template $9EFF->$9EFA, plant markers 9EF0=$FF, 9F0E=1).
Clobbers: A,BC,DE,HL.
init_attract_actors 9F11 LD HL,$9EFF
9F14 LD DE,$9EFA
9F17 LD BC,$0005
9F1A LDIR
9F1C LD A,$FF
9F1E LD ($9EF0),A
9F21 LD A,$01
9F23 LD ($9F0E),A
9F26 CALL render_play_frame Render play frame
9F29 LD B,$32
init_attract_actors_0 9F2B HALT
9F2C DJNZ init_attract_actors_0
9F2E XOR A
9F2F CALL attract_step Attract step
9F32 RET
9F33: Attract step
Used by the routines at init_attract_actors and level_shift_done.
Advance the attract/demo one frame: silence SFX, render_play_frame, then fire scripted sounds keyed off the demo phase 9F0E.
Phase 0: sweeps a tone counter down by 2/frame until $68, then advances to phase 1 (also plays sound 3). Phase 1: cycles the 4 actor Y-offsets down by 2/frame at $9EFB, resetting a sub-counter every 9th frame, plays sound 3 every wrap, advances to phase 2 at the end. Phase 2/4: replay scripted actor-position frames from tables 9F04/$9F09 indexed by a frame counter ($9F0F). Phase 3: sweeps $9EFA up/down by 2/frame between bounds, plays sound 3 at each end, toggling direction via $9F10. Phase else (5+): sweeps the same tone counter back up by 2/frame until $90, then RET (attract sequence done).
Clobbers: most.
attract_step 9F33 LD ($9F10),A stash tune id / phase-0 marker ($9F10)
9F36 CALL stop_sound Stop sound
attract_step_0 9F39 CALL render_play_frame Render play frame
9F3C LD A,($9F0E) A = demo phase (9F0E)
9F3F CP $01 phase == 1? -> $9F6A
9F41 JR NZ,attract_step_2
9F43 LD A,$0B A = sound id 11 (phase-0 tone)
9F45 CALL play_sound Play sound
9F48 LD HL,$9EFB HL -> actor Y-offsets ($9EFB)
9F4B LD B,$04 B = 4 (offset count)
attract_step_1 9F4D LD A,(HL) subtract 2 from this offset, next
9F4E SUB $02
9F50 LD (HL),A
9F51 INC HL
9F52 DJNZ attract_step_1 loop 4 offsets
9F54 CP $68 reached lower bound ($68)?
9F56 JP NZ,attract_step_0
9F59 LD HL,$9F0E HL -> demo phase
9F5C INC (HL) advance to phase 1
9F5D LD A,$00
9F5F LD ($9F0F),A reset frame sub-counter ($9F0F)=0
9F62 LD A,$03
9F64 CALL play_sound Play sound
9F67 JP attract_step_0
attract_step_2 9F6A CP $02 phase == 2? -> $9F97
9F6C JR NZ,attract_step_3
9F6E LD A,($9F0F) A = frame sub-counter
9F71 SRL A /2 -> table index
9F73 LD E,A
9F74 LD D,$00
9F76 LD HL,$9F04 HL -> position table 9F04
9F79 ADD HL,DE
9F7A LD A,(HL) fetch scripted X, store ($9EF6)
9F7B LD ($9EF6),A
9F7E LD HL,$9F09 HL -> position table $9F09
9F81 ADD HL,DE
9F82 LD A,(HL) fetch scripted Y, store (9EF1)
9F83 LD ($9EF1),A
9F86 LD HL,$9F0F HL -> frame sub-counter
9F89 INC (HL) advance sub-counter
9F8A LD A,(HL)
9F8B CP $09 reached frame $09? -> advance to phase 2
9F8D JP NZ,attract_step_0
9F90 LD HL,$9F0E
9F93 INC (HL)
9F94 JP attract_step_0
attract_step_3 9F97 CP $03 phase == 3? -> $9FE7
9F99 JR NZ,attract_step_5
9F9B LD A,($9F10) A = sweep direction flag ($9F10)
9F9E OR A nonzero? -> sweep up branch $9FC4
9F9F JR NZ,attract_step_4
9FA1 LD A,$00 zero flag: clear demo-actor marker (9EF0)
9FA3 LD ($9EF0),A
9FA6 LD A,($9EFA) A = sweep value ($9EFA)
9FA9 SUB $02 subtract 2 (sweep down)
9FAB LD ($9EFA),A
9FAE CP $54 reached lower bound ($54)?
9FB0 JP NZ,attract_step_0
9FB3 LD HL,$9F0E
9FB6 INC (HL)
9FB7 LD A,$08 A = 8, seed frame sub-counter
9FB9 LD ($9F0F),A
9FBC LD A,$03
9FBE CALL play_sound Play sound
9FC1 JP attract_step_0
attract_step_4 9FC4 LD A,($9EFA) A = sweep value ($9EFA)
9FC7 ADD A,$02 add 2 (sweep up)
9FC9 LD ($9EFA),A
9FCC CP $68 reached upper bound ($68)?
9FCE JP NZ,attract_step_0
9FD1 LD HL,$9F0E
9FD4 INC (HL)
9FD5 LD A,$FF A = $FF, mark demo-actor active
9FD7 LD ($9EF0),A
9FDA LD A,$08
9FDC LD ($9F0F),A
9FDF LD A,$03
9FE1 CALL play_sound Play sound
9FE4 JP attract_step_0
attract_step_5 9FE7 CP $04 phase == 4? -> $A013
9FE9 JR NZ,attract_step_6
9FEB LD A,($9F0F) A = frame sub-counter
9FEE SRL A
9FF0 LD E,A
9FF1 LD D,$00
9FF3 LD HL,$9F04
9FF6 ADD HL,DE
9FF7 LD A,(HL)
9FF8 LD ($9EF6),A
9FFB LD HL,$9F09
9FFE ADD HL,DE
9FFF LD A,(HL)
A000 LD ($9EF1),A
A003 LD HL,$9F0F HL -> frame sub-counter
A006 DEC (HL) decrement sub-counter
A007 BIT 7,(HL) underflowed (bit7 set)?
A009 JP Z,attract_step_0
A00C LD HL,$9F0E HL -> demo phase
A00F INC (HL) advance to next phase
A010 JP attract_step_0
attract_step_6 A013 LD A,$0B A = sound id 11 (final sweep tone)
A015 CALL play_sound Play sound
A018 LD HL,$9EFB HL -> actor Y-offsets ($9EFB)
A01B LD B,$04 B = 4 (offset count)
attract_step_7 A01D LD A,(HL) add 2 to this offset, next
A01E ADD A,$02
A020 LD (HL),A
A021 INC HL
A022 DJNZ attract_step_7 loop 4 offsets
A024 CP $90 reached upper bound ($90)? done
A026 JP NZ,attract_step_0
A029 RET
A02A: Render play frame
Used by the routines at init_attract_actors and attract_step.
One frame of the in-play tower screen: setup_tower_scroll, code_ad00, wait vsync (HALT), tick the sound engine (sound_tick), draw the tower body + column strip, refresh the strip params and the scroll-wrap edge, then process the 5 actor records at 9EF0.
Clobbers: most.
render_play_frame A02A CALL jp_setup_scroll setup_tower_scroll / code_ad00 trampolines
A02D CALL jp_code_ad00
A030 PUSH AF
A031 HALT
A032 CALL sound_tick Sound tick
A035 CALL jp_draw_tower draw tower body (jp_draw_tower)
A038 CALL jp_draw_strip draw column strip (jp_draw_strip)
A03B POP AF
A03C CALL jp_setup_strip refresh strip params (jp_setup_strip)
A03F CALL jp_blit_scroll_wrap scroll-wrap edge (jp_blit_scroll_wrap)
A042 LD IX,$9EF0 IX -> demo actor records (9EF0), B=5
A046 LD B,$05
render_play_frame_0 A048 PUSH IX save IX/BC for this actor
A04A PUSH BC
A04B LD A,(IX+$00) marker==$FF? inactive, skip
A04E CP $FF
A050 JP Z,render_play_frame_9
A053 LD A,(IX+$05) compute screen row from actor Y and row table
A056 SRL A
A058 SRL A
A05A SRL A
A05C LD L,A
A05D LD H,(IX+$0A)
A060 CALL jp_get_screen_addr
A063 PUSH HL
A064 LD A,(IX+$00) compute screen column
A067 CALL jp_get_sprite_def
A06A POP DE
A06B LD A,(IX+$0A) off-screen (Y>=$7C)? skip
A06E CP $7C
A070 JP NC,render_play_frame_9
A073 LD B,$14 clamp cell count near the bottom edge
A075 CP $6A
A077 JR C,render_play_frame_1
A079 LD B,A
A07A LD A,$7C
A07C SUB B
A07D LD B,A
render_play_frame_1 A07E DI DI
A07F LD ($6000),SP set Temporary place for SP
A083 LD SP,HL
A084 EX DE,HL
A085 LD A,C C selects row-width variant: 1/2/3-cell
A086 CP $04
A088 JP Z,render_play_frame_6
A08B CP $03
A08D JP Z,render_play_frame_4
render_play_frame_2 A090 POP DE --- 1-cell-wide: copy+merge 2 mask/data pairs ---
A091 LD A,(HL)
A092 AND E
A093 OR D
A094 LD (HL),A
A095 INC L
A096 POP DE
A097 LD A,(HL)
A098 AND E
A099 OR D
A09A LD (HL),A
A09B DEC L
A09C INC H
A09D LD A,H wrap to next screen row (char-cell step)
A09E AND $07
A0A0 JP NZ,render_play_frame_3
A0A3 LD A,L
A0A4 ADD A,$20
A0A6 LD L,A
A0A7 JR C,render_play_frame_3
A0A9 LD A,H
A0AA SUB $08
A0AC LD H,A
render_play_frame_3 A0AD DJNZ render_play_frame_2 loop B rows; done -> restore SP
A0AF JP render_play_frame_8
render_play_frame_4 A0B2 POP DE --- 2-cell-wide: copy+merge 3 mask/data pairs ---
A0B3 LD A,(HL)
A0B4 AND E
A0B5 OR D
A0B6 LD (HL),A
A0B7 INC L
A0B8 POP DE
A0B9 LD A,(HL)
A0BA AND E
A0BB OR D
A0BC LD (HL),A
A0BD INC L
A0BE POP DE
A0BF LD A,(HL)
A0C0 AND E
A0C1 OR D
A0C2 LD (HL),A
A0C3 DEC L
A0C4 DEC L
A0C5 INC H wrap to next screen row
A0C6 LD A,H
A0C7 AND $07
A0C9 JP NZ,render_play_frame_5
A0CC LD A,L
A0CD ADD A,$20
A0CF LD L,A
A0D0 JR C,render_play_frame_5
A0D2 LD A,H
A0D3 SUB $08
A0D5 LD H,A
render_play_frame_5 A0D6 DJNZ render_play_frame_4 loop B rows; done -> restore SP
A0D8 JP render_play_frame_8
render_play_frame_6 A0DB POP DE --- 3-cell-wide: copy+merge 3 mask/data pairs ---
A0DC LD C,L
A0DD LD A,(HL)
A0DE AND E
A0DF LD (HL),A
A0E0 INC L
A0E1 LD A,(HL)
A0E2 AND D
A0E3 POP DE
A0E4 OR E
A0E5 LD (HL),A
A0E6 INC L
A0E7 LD A,(HL)
A0E8 AND D
A0E9 POP DE
A0EA OR E
A0EB LD (HL),A
A0EC INC L
A0ED LD A,(HL)
A0EE AND D
A0EF LD (HL),A restore L, advance H
A0F0 LD L,C
A0F1 INC H
A0F2 LD A,H wrap to next screen row
A0F3 AND $07
A0F5 JP NZ,render_play_frame_7
A0F8 LD A,L
A0F9 ADD A,$20
A0FB LD L,A
A0FC JR C,render_play_frame_7
A0FE LD A,H
A0FF SUB $08
A101 LD H,A
render_play_frame_7 A102 DJNZ render_play_frame_6 loop B rows
render_play_frame_8 A104 LD SP,($6000) get Temporary place for SP
A108 EI
render_play_frame_9 A109 POP BC next actor, loop B=5, RET
A10A POP IX
A10C INC IX
A10E DEC B
A10F JP NZ,render_play_frame_0
A112 RET
A113: Level shift entry
Used by the routine at 879B.
Entry into pstate_9, taken from pstate_4's landing code ($87E9) when game_state==3 (level transition).
Sets pstate_phase=$FF (negative, selects pstate_9's shift-in-progress branch) and player_state=9, falls into pstate_9.
level_shift_entry A113 LD A,$FF
A115 LD ($60BF),A set Player state-machine phase/height window
A118 LD A,$09
A11A LD ($60C0),A set Player state-machine state
A11D: Pstate 9
State 9: level-transition tower-column shift (slides the next section of the tower map into place after landing).
While pstate_phase is negative: if the level-column property byte ($603A) has counted down to 6, jumps to $A15B to wait for scroll_pos to reach the wrap point (scroll_pos==$03E0), then INCs pstate_phase (0, still non-negative) to end the shift on the next call.
Otherwise: steps actor_obj column position back by 4, shifts 6 columns of the work_buffers tower map left by one row (byte-copy loop), decrements $603A, stops the current sound and plays sound $0C -- repeats every call until the column property hits 6.
Once pstate_phase is non-negative, falls through (next call) to level_shift_done to finish the transition.
pstate_9 A11D LD A,($60BF) get Player state-machine phase/height window
A120 OR A
A121 JP P,level_shift_done
A124 LD A,($603A)
A127 CP $06
A129 JR Z,pstate_9_2
A12B LD HL,($60BA)
A12E LD DE,$FFFC
A131 ADD HL,DE
A132 LD ($60BA),HL
A135 LD A,($603A)
A138 SUB $05
A13A LD L,A
A13B LD H,$F0
A13D LD C,$06
pstate_9_0 A13F PUSH HL
A140 LD B,$10
pstate_9_1 A142 LD A,(HL)
A143 DEC L
A144 LD (HL),A
A145 INC L
A146 INC H
A147 DJNZ pstate_9_1
A149 POP HL
A14A INC L
A14B DEC C
A14C JR NZ,pstate_9_0
A14E LD HL,$603A
A151 DEC (HL)
A152 CALL stop_sound Stop sound
A155 LD A,$0C
A157 CALL play_sound Play sound
A15A RET
pstate_9_2 A15B LD HL,($6030) get Vertical scroll position
A15E LD A,H
A15F CP $03
A161 RET NZ
A162 LD A,L
A163 CP $E0
A165 RET NZ
A166 LD HL,$60BF
A169 INC (HL)
A16A RET
A16B: Data block at A16B
5-byte attract-actor template, copied into the demo actor slot ($9EFA) by level_shift_done.
A16B DEFB $54,$7C,$90,$90,$90
A170: Level shift done
Used by the routine at pstate_9.
Finishes the level-transition shift (reached once pstate_9's pstate_phase goes non-negative).
Resets the attract/demo actor state at 9EF0 (copies the A16B template, clears the marker, sets phase 1), calls ATTRACT_STEP once, then sets game_state=4 (finished) -- the signal main_tick polls to end the transition and hand control back to main_loop's level-complete handling.
level_shift_done A170 LD HL,$A16B
A173 LD DE,$9EFA
A176 LD BC,$0005
A179 LDIR
A17B LD A,$00
A17D LD ($9EF0),A
A180 LD A,$01
A182 LD ($9F0E),A
A185 LD A,$01
A187 CALL attract_step Attract step
A18A LD A,$04
A18C LD ($60D9),A set Game state
A18F RET
A190: Data block at A190
Music sequencer's live state, not fixed data: stream pointer (A190/91), current-note pointer ($A192/93), note-groups-remaining counter ($A194), note timer ($A195), active tune id ($A196). The DEFB values shown are just the reset state baked into the snapshot.
A190 DEFB $59,$A2,$72,$A2,$09,$00,$00
A197: Start music
Used by the routines at show_level_intro, draw_info_screen_a, draw_timeup_screen and title_menu.
Begin music/jingle A: stash the id ($A196) then fall into init_music_seq to choose the data stream and arm the sequencer.
In: A=tune id.
start_music A197 LD ($A196),A
A19A: Init music sequence
Used by the routine at music_tick.
Picks one of 3 preset note-stream start addresses by tune id: 0 -> $A257 (9 note-groups), 1 -> $A269 (1 group), else -> $A26B (fallback/short stinger). Arms the stream pointer, group counter and note timer (=1, fires immediately).
init_music_seq A19A LD A,($A196)
A19D LD HL,$A257 id==0 -> stream $A257, 9 groups
A1A0 LD B,$09
A1A2 OR A
A1A3 JR Z,init_music_seq_0
A1A5 LD HL,$A269 id==1 -> stream $A269, 1 group
A1A8 LD B,$01
A1AA DEC A
A1AB JR Z,init_music_seq_0
A1AD LD HL,$A26B else -> stream $A26B (same group count)
init_music_seq_0 A1B0 LD ($A190),HL arm stream pointer, group counter, note timer
A1B3 LD A,B
A1B4 LD ($A194),A
A1B7 LD A,$01
A1B9 LD ($A195),A
A1BC RET
A1BD: Music tick
Used by the routines at show_level_intro, draw_info_screen_a, draw_timeup_screen and title_menu_draw.
Per-frame music sequencer step (DI around it).
When the note timer ($A195) elapses, fetch the next event from the stream (A190), update the current note ($A192), and play it.
A $FF note byte ends the current group: decrements the group counter ($A194) and loops to the next group if any remain, otherwise either holds the last note (tune id==0) or restarts via init_music_seq (id!=0, one-shot jingles loop back to their own start).
Otherwise the note byte indexes two parallel tables at A238 (or $A23A, selected by the Specialist-beeper flag $6056) for a period value and A249 (an 11-char string used as a duration/shape lookup) for a tone-loop shape, then bit-bangs OUT ($FE) via an inlined tone loop (same shape as sfx_tone).
The Specialist has a 1-bit beeper too -- retarget the port and retune the delay loops.
music_tick A1BD DI
A1BE LD A,($A195)
A1C1 OR A
A1C2 JR Z,music_tick_1
music_tick_0 A1C4 XOR A
A1C5 LD ($A195),A
A1C8 LD HL,($A190) HL -> stream pointer, fetch next 16-bit event
A1CB LD E,(HL)
A1CC INC HL
A1CD LD D,(HL)
A1CE INC HL
A1CF LD ($A190),HL
A1D2 LD ($A192),DE
music_tick_1 A1D6 LD HL,($A192) A = current note byte
A1D9 LD A,(HL)
A1DA CP $FF $FF = end of note-group
A1DC JR NZ,music_tick_2
A1DE LD HL,$A194
A1E1 DEC (HL) decrement group counter
A1E2 JR NZ,music_tick_0
A1E4 LD A,($A196) last group done: id==0 -> hold, else -> restart tune
A1E7 OR A
A1E8 SCF
A1E9 JR NZ,music_tick_7
A1EB CALL init_music_seq Init music sequence
A1EE JR music_tick_0
music_tick_2 A1F0 INC HL
A1F1 LD ($A192),HL
A1F4 LD HL,$A237 index period table $A237+note, select beeper variant via $6056
A1F7 LD E,A
A1F8 LD D,$00
A1FA ADD HL,DE
A1FB LD BC,$A238
A1FE LD A,($6056)
A201 OR A
A202 JR NZ,music_tick_3
A204 LD BC,$A23A
music_tick_3 A207 LD A,(BC)
A208 LD ($A22B),A
A20B INC BC
A20C LD A,(BC)
A20D LD ($A22C),A
A210 LD B,(HL)
A211 LD A,$01
A213 ADD A,B
A214 LD C,A
A215 LD E,$00
A217 LD D,$16
A219 LD H,B
A21A XOR A
music_tick_4 A21B DEC H inlined tone-loop bit-bang (OUT $FE), shaped by duration string byte
A21C JR NZ,music_tick_5
A21E OR $10
A220 LD H,B
music_tick_5 A221 DEC L
A222 JR NZ,music_tick_6
A224 OR $10
A226 LD L,C
music_tick_6 A227 ADD A,$08
A229 AND $10
A22B OUT ($FE),A
A22D SRL A
A22F DEC E
A230 JR NZ,music_tick_4
A232 DEC D
A233 JR NZ,music_tick_4
A235 OR A
music_tick_7 A236 EI
A237 RET
A238: Data block at A238
Two parallel 8-entry period/duration tables (ZX at A238, alternate "Specialist" beeper variant at $A240), indexed by note code and selected via $6056.
A238 DEFB $D3,$FE,$23,$2B,$00,$F2,$E7,$D8
A240 DEFB $CD,$C2,$B7,$AC,$A1,$99,$90,$88
A248 DEFB $80
11-char duration/shape string, one char per note code: each character's ASCII value supplies the H/L sub-loop counts for music_tick's inlined tone loop.
A249 DEFM "yslfa[VPLHD"
A254: Data block at A254
A second, shorter period/shape table pair (1 byte + 3 chars) -- sparse coverage, likely only used by a short jingle's reduced note set.
A254 DEFB $40
A255 DEFM "96m"
A258: Data block at A258
Real note-stream data starts one byte earlier, at $A257 (see init_music_seq): $A257 (9-group stream), $A269 (1-group stream) and $A26B (fallback stream) are literal entry addresses into this block, not a lookup table.
Byte values are note codes (indices into the A238/A249 tables) $FF marks the end of a note-group. Runs through $A367 the final 5 bytes ($A368-$A36C) are SFX-engine state (active effect id/pointer/priority), not music data.
A258 DEFB $A2,$6D,$A2,$8E,$A2,$8E,$A2,$AF
A260 DEFB $A2,$F0,$A2,$F0,$A2,$31,$A3,$31
A268 DEFB $A3,$52,$A3,$5B,$A3,$18,$16,$18
A270 DEFB $16,$18,$18,$16,$18,$18,$16,$18
A278 DEFB $16,$1B,$1A,$1B,$1B,$18,$16,$18
A280 DEFB $16,$18,$18,$13,$16,$16,$15,$16
A288 DEFB $16,$16,$16,$16,$16,$FF,$1A,$18
A290 DEFB $1A,$18,$1A,$1A,$18,$1A,$1A,$18
A298 DEFB $1A,$18,$1D,$1C,$1D,$1D,$1A,$18
A2A0 DEFB $1A,$18,$1A,$1A,$15,$18,$18,$17
A2A8 DEFB $18,$18,$18,$18,$18,$18,$FF,$16
A2B0 DEFB $15,$11,$0E,$15,$13,$10,$0C,$11
A2B8 DEFB $10,$0E,$10,$10,$11,$11,$11,$16
A2C0 DEFB $15,$11,$0E,$15,$13,$10,$0C,$18
A2C8 DEFB $18,$16,$15,$15,$11,$11,$11,$16
A2D0 DEFB $15,$11,$0E,$15,$13,$10,$0C,$11
A2D8 DEFB $10,$0E,$10,$10,$11,$11,$11,$16
A2E0 DEFB $15,$13,$15,$15,$16,$16,$16,$19
A2E8 DEFB $18,$16,$18,$18,$19,$19,$1C,$FF
A2F0 DEFB $11,$0A,$15,$0A,$11,$0A,$15,$11
A2F8 DEFB $0A,$15,$0A,$11,$16,$15,$11,$0E
A300 DEFB $10,$09,$13,$09,$10,$09,$13,$10
A308 DEFB $09,$13,$09,$10,$11,$10,$0C,$09
A310 DEFB $0E,$07,$11,$07,$0E,$07,$11,$0E
A318 DEFB $07,$11,$07,$0E,$10,$0E,$11,$0E
A320 DEFB $10,$09,$13,$09,$10,$09,$13,$10
A328 DEFB $09,$13,$09,$10,$11,$10,$0E,$10
A330 DEFB $FF,$18,$18,$16,$15,$15,$11,$0E
A338 DEFB $11,$13,$11,$13,$15,$18,$15,$18
A340 DEFB $1A,$18,$18,$15,$11,$11,$0E,$11
A348 DEFB $13,$15,$13,$15,$18,$1A,$18,$15
A350 DEFB $11,$FF,$07,$08,$0B,$0C,$0E,$0E
A358 DEFB $0E,$0E,$FF,$0E,$0E,$0C,$0C,$0B
A360 DEFB $0B,$08,$08,$07,$07,$07,$07,$FF
A368 DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$00
A36D: Stop sound
Used by the routines at level_retry, attract_step and pstate_9.
Silences the current SFX effect by marking $A368=$FF (sound_tick's "active marker" gate).
stop_sound A36D LD A,$FF
A36F LD ($A368),A
A372 RET
A373: Data block at A373
13 pointer words (ids 0..$0C, matching play_sound's "id<$0D" range), one per sound effect, resolved by lookup_sound.
snd_ptr_table A373 DEFB $8D,$A3,$97,$A3,$9A,$A3,$9D,$A3
A37B DEFB $A2,$A3,$AA,$A3,$AF,$A3,$C9,$A3
A383 DEFB $C9,$A3,$CD,$A3,$D5,$A3,$D8,$A3
A38B DEFB $DB,$A3
A38D: Data block at A38D
The 13 effects' byte streams, back-to-back, addressed via snd_ptr_table. Each stream starts with 2 header bytes (priority, then a repeat/duration count copied into $A36C) followed by command/data bytes consumed by sound_tick: values <$80 are raw tone/length data for sfx_tone values $80/$81/$C8/$C9/$CA are specific ops (noise burst, random tone, etc., handled at A48A/A470/A4BF/A52E/A4F2) values $82..$C7 go through the generic handler at A493 anything else ends the effect.
snd_effect_data A38D DEFB $14,$08,$20,$1C,$18,$14,$20,$18
A395 DEFB $20,$18,$05,$01,$81,$0A,$01,$C9
A39D DEFB $0A,$03,$01,$20,$01,$0A,$06,$04
A3A5 DEFB $08,$0C,$10,$14,$18,$1E,$03,$01
A3AD DEFB $80,$81,$1E,$18,$01,$80,$82,$01
A3B5 DEFB $80,$82,$80,$82,$20,$81,$1C,$81
A3BD DEFB $18,$81,$14,$81,$10,$82,$0C,$82
A3C5 DEFB $08,$82,$04,$82,$1E,$02,$81,$82
A3CD DEFB $1E,$06,$82,$83,$84,$85,$86,$87
A3D5 DEFB $0A,$01,$80,$28,$01,$C8,$28,$01
A3DD DEFB $CA
A3DE: Play sound
Used by the routines at main_tick, pstate_play, pstate_2, code_8656, pstate_1, pstate4_entry, pstate_4, pstate_4_land_tail, pstate_6, coll_apply, code_8a71, enemy_attack_reset, check_actor_collision_b, consume_cell, update_actor_pos, attract_step and pstate_9.
Trigger sound effect A (id < $0D).
Saves all regs, looks up the effect via lookup_sound, and (subject to a priority check against the currently playing effect at $A368/$A369) latches its data pointer at $A36A for sound_tick to play out.
In: A=effect id.
Preserves all registers.
Most-called audio API.
play_sound A3DE PUSH AF
A3DF PUSH BC
A3E0 PUSH DE
A3E1 PUSH HL
A3E2 CP $0D
A3E4 JR NC,play_sound_1
A3E6 CALL lookup_sound
A3E9 LD D,A
A3EA LD A,($A368)
A3ED CP $FF
A3EF JR Z,play_sound_0
A3F1 LD A,($A369)
A3F4 CP B
A3F5 JR C,play_sound_1
play_sound_0 A3F7 LD ($A36A),HL
A3FA LD A,C
A3FB LD ($A36C),A
A3FE LD A,D
A3FF LD ($A368),A
A402 LD A,B
A403 LD ($A369),A
play_sound_1 A406 POP HL
A407 POP DE
A408 POP BC
A409 POP AF
A40A RET
A40B: Routine at A40B
Used by the routine at play_sound.
lookup_sound A40B LD L,A
A40C LD H,$00
A40E LD DE,$A373
A411 ADD HL,HL
A412 ADD HL,DE
A413 LD E,(HL)
A414 INC HL
A415 LD D,(HL)
A416 EX DE,HL
A417 LD B,(HL)
A418 INC HL
A419 LD C,(HL)
A41A INC HL
A41B RET
A41C: Sound tick
Used by the routines at main_tick and render_play_frame.
Per-frame SFX engine step.
Gated by the sound-enable flag ($6056) and the active marker ($A368).
Reads the next byte of the current effect (pointer $A36A), interpreting commands (>=$80) vs tone/length data, and emits to the speaker.
The Specialist has a 1-bit beeper too -- retarget the port and retune the delay loops.
sound_tick A41C LD A,($6056)
A41F OR A
A420 JP Z,$A50E
A423 LD A,($A368)
A426 CP $FF
A428 JP Z,$A50E
A42B LD HL,($A36A)
A42E LD A,(HL)
A42F INC HL
A430 LD ($A36A),HL
A433 CP $80
A435 JR C,sound_tick_0
A437 CALL Z,$A48A
A43A JR Z,sound_tick_1
A43C CP $81
A43E CALL Z,$A470
A441 JR Z,sound_tick_1
A443 CP $C8
A445 CALL Z,$A4BF
A448 JR Z,sound_tick_1
A44A CP $C9
A44C CALL Z,$A52E
A44F JR Z,sound_tick_1
A451 CP $CA
A453 CALL Z,$A4F2
A456 JR Z,sound_tick_1
A458 CP $82
A45A CALL NC,$A493
A45D JR NC,sound_tick_1
A45F JP sound_tick_2
sound_tick_0 A462 CALL sfx_tone SFX tone
sound_tick_1 A465 LD HL,$A36C
A468 DEC (HL)
A469 RET NZ
sound_tick_2 A46A LD A,$FF
A46C LD ($A368),A
A46F RET
A470: Routine at A470
Used by the routine at sound_tick.
A470 PUSH AF
A471 LD E,$10
A473 LD C,$12
A475 LD D,$80
A477 LD A,E
A478 OUT ($FE),A
A47A XOR $10
A47C LD E,A
A47D LD B,D
A47E DJNZ $A47E
A480 LD A,D
A481 SUB $03
A483 LD D,A
A484 DEC C
A485 JP NZ,$A477
A488 POP AF
A489 RET
A48A: Routine at A48A
Used by the routine at sound_tick.
A48A PUSH AF
A48B LD DE,$0143
A48E CALL sfx_noise SFX noise
A491 POP AF
A492 RET
A493: Routine at A493
Used by the routine at sound_tick.
A493 PUSH AF
A494 LD B,$10
A496 ADD A,$A3
A498 LD H,A
A499 LD L,$6D
A49B LD DE,$0143
A49E LD A,(HL)
A49F AND B
A4A0 OUT ($FE),A
A4A2 INC HL
A4A3 DEC DE
A4A4 LD A,D
A4A5 OR E
A4A6 NOP
A4A7 NOP
A4A8 JP NZ,$A49E
A4AB POP AF
A4AC RET
A4AD: SFX noise
Used by the routine at A48A.
White-noise burst: XORs the refresh register R into the speaker bit and toggles OUT ($FE) for DE iterations.
The Specialist has a 1-bit beeper too -- retarget the port and retune the delay loops.
sfx_noise A4AD LD B,$10
sfx_noise_0 A4AF LD A,R
A4B1 XOR D
A4B2 ADD A,E
A4B3 AND B
A4B4 OUT ($FE),A
A4B6 DEC DE
A4B7 LD A,D
A4B8 OR E
A4B9 NOP
A4BA NOP
A4BB JP NZ,sfx_noise_0
A4BE RET
A4BF: Routine at A4BF
Used by the routine at sound_tick.
A4BF PUSH AF
A4C0 CALL jp_random
A4C3 LD E,A
A4C4 LD HL,$8000
A4C7 LD BC,$007A
A4CA LD A,(HL)
A4CB AND $10
A4CD OUT ($FE),A
A4CF LD A,L
A4D0 ADD A,E
A4D1 LD L,A
A4D2 LD A,(HL)
A4D3 AND $10
A4D5 OUT ($FE),A
A4D7 LD A,L
A4D8 ADD A,E
A4D9 LD L,A
A4DA LD A,(HL)
A4DB AND $10
A4DD OUT ($FE),A
A4DF LD A,L
A4E0 ADD A,E
A4E1 LD L,A
A4E2 LD A,(HL)
A4E3 AND $10
A4E5 OUT ($FE),A
A4E7 LD A,L
A4E8 ADD A,E
A4E9 LD L,A
A4EA DEC BC
A4EB LD A,B
A4EC OR C
A4ED JP NZ,$A4CA
A4F0 POP AF
A4F1 RET
A4F2: Routine at A4F2
Used by the routine at sound_tick.
A4F2 PUSH AF
A4F3 LD A,($603A)
A4F6 SRL A
A4F8 SRL A
A4FA CP $23
A4FC JR C,$A500
A4FE LD A,$22
A500 PUSH AF
A501 CALL jp_random
A504 AND $03
A506 LD B,A
A507 POP AF
A508 XOR B
A509 CALL sfx_tone SFX tone
A50C POP AF
A50D RET
A50E: Routine at A50E
Used by the routine at sound_tick.
A50E LD HL,$036B
A511 DEC HL
A512 LD A,H
A513 OR L
A514 JP NZ,$A511
A517 RET
A518: Unused
A518 DEFS $01
A519: Routine at A519
Used by the routine at update_timer_digits.
sfx_random_tone A519 PUSH AF
A51A CALL jp_random
A51D AND $07
A51F LD B,A
A520 LD A,($A518)
A523 SUB B
A524 AND $1F
A526 LD ($A518),A
A529 CALL sfx_tone SFX tone
A52C POP AF
A52D RET
A52E: Routine at A52E
Used by the routine at sound_tick.
A52E PUSH AF
A52F CALL jp_random
A532 AND $03
A534 CALL sfx_tone SFX tone
A537 POP AF
A538 RET
A539: SFX tone
Used by the routines at sound_tick, A4F2, sfx_random_tone and A52E.
Emit a square-wave tone: pitch index A selects a half-period from table A560 toggles the speaker for a fixed duration ($0148 outer).
In: A=pitch.
Clobbers: A,BC,DE,HL,AF'.
The Specialist has a 1-bit beeper too -- retarget the port and retune the delay loops.
sfx_tone A539 LD L,A
A53A LD H,$00
A53C LD DE,$A560
A53F ADD HL,DE
A540 LD DE,$0148
A543 LD C,(HL)
A544 LD A,$10
A546 EX AF,AF'
A547 LD B,$10
A549 LD L,C
sfx_tone_0 A54A DEC L
A54B JR NZ,sfx_tone_2
A54D EX AF,AF'
A54E XOR B
A54F OUT ($FE),A
A551 EX AF,AF'
A552 LD L,C
sfx_tone_1 A553 DEC DE
A554 LD A,D
A555 OR E
A556 JR NZ,sfx_tone_0
A558 RET
sfx_tone_2 A559 OR $00
A55B BIT 0,A
A55D JP sfx_tone_1
A560 DEFM "fa[VQMHD"
A568: Data block at A568
A568 DEFB $40
A569 DEFM "=9630-+(&$\" "
A575: Data block at A575
A575 DEFB $1E,$1C,$1B,$19,$18,$16,$15,$14
