A700: Proc table
Used by the routines at poll_controls, main_tick, title_menu_draw, wait_frames and enter_hiscore_name.
Jump/trampoline dispatch table, 53 entries, each 'JP nnnn', 3 bytes.
The game CALLs these fixed slots instead of the moving routine bodies a CALL <slot> costs one extra JP.
proc_table A700 JP read_input
jp_scan_keys A703 JP scan_control_keys
This entry point is used by the routines at poll_controls and title_menu_draw.
jp_test_key A706 JP test_key -> test_key: A=key id, returns Z if pressed
jp_poll_any_key A709 JP poll_any_key
jp_wait_no_key A70C JP wait_no_key
This entry point is used by the routine at poll_controls.
jp_test_break A70F JP test_break_key
jp_delay A712 JP delay_loop
This entry point is used by the routines at A4BF, A4F2, sfx_random_tone and A52E.
jp_random A715 JP next_random
jp_save_regs A718 JP save_regs_frame
jp_restore_regs A71B JP restore_regs_frame
This entry point is used by the routine at game_entry.
jp_setup_attrs A71E JP setup_attrs -> setup_attrs: paint playfield attributes + PLAYER labels
This entry point is used by the routine at level_retry.
jp_reset_score A721 JP reset_score_state
This entry point is used by the routines at level_retry, main_loop, main_tick and screen_time_bonus.
jp_draw_hud A724 JP draw_hud_counters
This entry point is used by the routines at tally_score_to, check_actor_collision_b, update_actor_pos and screen_time_bonus.
jp_add_score A727 JP add_bcd_score
This entry point is used by the routines at level_retry and main_loop.
jp_init_bonus A72A JP init_bonus
This entry point is used by the routine at coll_apply.
jp_bonus_sub A72D JP bonus_sub
This entry point is used by the routines at check_actor_collision_b and update_actor_pos.
jp_field_604f_add A730 JP field_604f_add
This entry point is used by the routine at level_retry.
jp_set_level_timer A733 JP set_level_timer
This entry point is used by the routine at main_tick.
jp_tick_timer A736 JP tick_timer
This entry point is used by the routines at level_retry and screen_time_bonus.
jp_draw_timer A739 JP draw_timer
jp_print_char_at A73C JP $AAC2
This entry point is used by the routines at print_score_entry and print_score_byte.
jp_print_char A73F JP print_char
jp_print_str_loop A742 JP print_string_loop
This entry point is used by the routines at print_player_label and print_string_at.
jp_print_centered A745 JP print_centered
jp_set_print_pos A748 JP set_print_pos
jp_clear_obj_table A74B JP clear_obj_table
jp_calc_col_addr A74E JP calc_tower_col_addr
This entry point is used by the routines at setup_level and main_tick.
jp_col_visible A751 JP col_visible_offset
This entry point is used by the routines at main_tick and render_play_frame.
jp_blit_scroll_wrap A754 JP blit_scroll_wrap
This entry point is used by the routines at main_tick and render_play_frame.
jp_code_ad00 A757 JP code_ad00
This entry point is used by the routine at main_tick.
jp_anim_bg A75A JP anim_bg_phase
This entry point is used by the routines at main_tick and render_play_frame.
jp_setup_scroll A75D JP setup_tower_scroll
This entry point is used by the routines at main_tick and render_play_frame.
jp_draw_tower A760 JP draw_tower_cols
This entry point is used by the routine at setup_level.
jp_load_level_col A763 JP load_level_col
This entry point is used by the routines at pstate_6 and check_collision.
jp_alloc_slot A766 JP alloc_object_slot
This entry point is used by the routine at main_tick.
jp_update_objects A769 JP update_object_slots
This entry point is used by the routine at pstate5_exit.
jp_free_object A76C JP free_object_slot
This entry point is used by the routine at main_tick.
jp_update_actors A76F JP update_actors
This entry point is used by the routine at game_entry.
jp_clear_screen A772 JP clear_screen -> clear_screen: wipe display + game RAM, build LUT
jp_build_hi_lut A775 JP build_screen_hi_lut
This entry point is used by the routines at level_retry, main_loop, title_menu_redraw_anim and title_anim_tick.
jp_clear_playfield A778 JP clear_playfield
jp_hook_noop A77B JP hook_noop
This entry point is used by the routine at setup_level.
jp_init_b5cf A77E JP init_b5cf
This entry point is used by the routines at title_menu_draw and render_play_frame.
jp_get_screen_addr A781 JP get_screen_addr
This entry point is used by the routines at calc_sprite_shift and render_play_frame.
jp_get_sprite_def A784 JP get_sprite_def
This entry point is used by the routine at main_tick.
jp_select_sprite A787 JP select_player_sprite
This entry point is used by the routine at draw_actors_sorted.
jp_draw_actor A78A JP draw_actor_sprite
jp_actor_screen_y A78D JP actor_screen_y
This entry point is used by the routine at main_tick.
jp_draw_pending A790 JP draw_pending_sprite
This entry point is used by the routines at main_tick and render_play_frame.
jp_setup_strip A793 JP setup_strip_draw
This entry point is used by the routines at main_tick and render_play_frame.
jp_draw_strip A796 JP draw_tower_strip
This entry point is used by the routine at setup_level.
jp_paint_attrs A799 JP paint_tower_attrs
This entry point is used by the routine at main_tick.
jp_scroll_attrs A79C JP scroll_tower_attrs
A79F: Read input
Used by the routine at proc_table.
Proc_table[0]: sample controls into the signed input_x/input_y/input_fire vars.
Only active when game_mode (game_mode) == 4 (in play) otherwise returns.
Reads the Kempston joystick and converts each axis to -1/0/+1 via bit tests + SBC/NEG.
The Specialist has no Kempston port -- replace read_joystick with the target's keyboard/joystick scan, keeping the -1/0/+1 result.
read_input A79F LD A,($6017) A = game_mode (game_mode)
A7A2 CP $04
A7A4 JR NZ,scan_control_keys only read controls while in play (mode 4)
A7A6 CALL read_joystick A = raw joystick bits
A7A9 LD B,A
A7AA AND $01
A7AC SRL B
A7AE SRL B
A7B0 SBC A,$00
A7B2 LD ($601D),A input_y = -1/0/+1
A7B5 LD A,B
A7B6 AND $01
A7B8 SRL B
A7BA SRL B
A7BC SBC A,$00
A7BE NEG
A7C0 LD ($601E),A input_fire = -1/0/+1 (negated)
A7C3 LD A,B
A7C4 LD ($601C),A input_x = -1/0/+1
A7C7 RET
A7C8: Routine at A7C8
Used by the routines at read_input and poll_any_key.
read_joystick A7C8 IN A,($1F) read Kempston joystick port $1F (Z80 IN
A7CA AND $1F
A7CC CP $1F all 5 bits = nothing pressed
A7CE RET NZ
A7CF XOR A
A7D0 RET
A7D1: Data block at A7D1
A7D1 DEFB $1D,$15,$19,$22,$0F,$12,$1A,$1E
A7D9 DEFB $25,$08,$24,$1C,$14,$0C,$04,$04
A7E1 DEFB $13,$03,$0B,$23,$D1,$A7,$D6,$A7
A7E9 DEFB $DB,$A7,$E0,$A7
A7ED: Scan control keys
Used by the routines at proc_table and read_input.
Read one 5-key control set into the flag array at $6018.
A selects the set: index a word-pointer table at $A7E5 (A*2) to a 5-byte key-id list, then test_key each one, storing 0/1 into $6018..input_x.
Used to read the redefinable keys.
In: A=set index.
Out: $6018[0..4] flags.
Clobbers: A,B,DE,HL.
scan_control_keys A7ED ADD A,A
A7EE LD L,A
A7EF LD H,$00
A7F1 LD DE,$A7E5
A7F4 ADD HL,DE
A7F5 LD E,(HL)
A7F6 INC HL
A7F7 LD D,(HL)
A7F8 LD HL,$6018
A7FB LD B,$05
scan_control_keys_0 A7FD LD A,(DE)
A7FE CALL test_key Test key
A801 LD (HL),$00
A803 JR Z,scan_control_keys_1
A805 INC (HL)
scan_control_keys_1 A806 INC HL
A807 INC DE
A808 DJNZ scan_control_keys_0
A80A LD HL,$6018
A80D LD A,(HL)
A80E INC HL
A80F SUB (HL)
A810 INC HL
A811 NEG
A813 LD ($601D),A set Signed input
A816 LD A,(HL)
A817 INC HL
A818 SUB (HL)
A819 NEG
A81B LD ($601E),A set Signed input
A81E RET
A81F: Test key
Used by the routines at proc_table and scan_control_keys.
Is a specific key down?
(proc_table slot $A706.) In: A = key id, encoded (row<<3 | column) into the ZX keyboard matrix.
Out: Z set (A=0) if the key is pressed.
Builds the half-row select on the address bus (A=$FE rotated <row> times so one of A8..A15 is low) and reads ULA port $FE rotates out <column> bits and tests the active-low key bit.
test_key A81F PUSH BC
A820 LD C,A C = key id
A821 AND $07 low 3 bits = column -> bit count
A823 INC A
A824 LD B,A
A825 SRL C C >>= 3 -> row index
A827 SRL C
A829 SRL C
A82B LD A,$05
A82D SUB C 5 - row -> number of rotates for the row select
A82E LD C,A
A82F LD A,$FE A = $FE: one address line low selects a keyboard half-row
test_key_0 A831 RRCA rotate the low bit into the row position (DJNZ)
A832 DJNZ test_key_0
A834 IN A,($FE) read ULA keyboard port $FE (Z80 IN
test_key_1 A836 RRA shift out columns
A837 DEC C
A838 JR NZ,test_key_1
A83A LD A,C
A83B JR C,test_key_2 carry set = not pressed -> A=1
A83D INC A
test_key_2 A83E POP BC
A83F RET
A840: Poll any key
Used by the routine at proc_table.
Return NZ if ANY input is active: scans the five keyboard half-rows via IN ($FE) (CPL+AND $1F), and if none, tests the Kempston fire bit ($10).
Out: A/flags NZ = pressed.
poll_any_key A840 XOR A
A841 IN A,($FE)
A843 CPL
A844 AND $1F
A846 RET NZ
A847 CALL read_joystick
A84A AND $10
A84C RET
This entry point is used by the routine at proc_table.
wait_no_key A84D CALL poll_any_key Poll any key
A850 JR NZ,wait_no_key
A852 RET
A853: Test break key
Used by the routine at proc_table.
Test the BREAK combination (CAPS SHIFT + SPACE) via two IN ($FE) row reads.
Returns with CARRY CLEAR only when both are held (used to abort).
Clobbers: A.
test_break_key A853 LD A,$7F
A855 IN A,($FE)
A857 RRA
A858 RET C
A859 LD A,$FE
A85B IN A,($FE)
A85D RRA
A85E RET
A85F: Delay loop
Used by the routine at proc_table.
Busy-wait: B outer iterations of a ~$0086 inner countdown.
In: B=outer count.
Clobbers: A,B,HL (HL preserved).
delay_loop A85F PUSH HL
delay_loop_0 A860 LD HL,$0086
delay_loop_1 A863 DEC HL
A864 LD A,H
A865 OR L
A866 JR NZ,delay_loop_1
A868 DJNZ delay_loop_0
A86A POP HL
A86B RET
A86C: Next random
Used by the routine at proc_table.
Pseudo-random generator.
Reads the 16-bit seed (random_seed), applies a multiply/shift + add $0029 LCG step, writes the new seed back, and returns A = high byte.
Out: A=random byte, (random_seed) advanced.
Clobbers: A,DE,HL.
next_random A86C PUSH DE
A86D PUSH HL
A86E LD DE,($6002) get 16-bit LCG seed
A872 LD H,E
A873 LD L,$00
A875 ADD HL,DE
A876 EX DE,HL
A877 ADD HL,HL
A878 ADD HL,HL
A879 ADD HL,HL
A87A EX DE,HL
A87B OR A
A87C SBC HL,DE
A87E LD DE,$0029
A881 ADD HL,DE
A882 LD ($6002),HL set 16-bit LCG seed
A885 LD A,H
A886 POP HL
A887 POP DE
A888 RET
A889: Save regs frame
Used by the routine at proc_table.
Paired full CPU-context save and restore.
save_regs_frame pushes main+alternate register sets, IX, IY (EX AF,AF'/EXX to reach the shadow bank), then reads a 16-bit value out of the saved frame ($SP+$17) into HL before returning.
save_regs_frame A889 EX (SP),HL
A88A PUSH DE
A88B PUSH BC
A88C PUSH AF
A88D EX AF,AF'
A88E EXX
A88F PUSH HL
A890 PUSH DE
A891 PUSH BC
A892 PUSH AF
A893 EX AF,AF'
A894 EXX
A895 PUSH IX
A897 PUSH IY
A899 PUSH HL
A89A PUSH AF
A89B LD HL,$0017
A89E ADD HL,SP
A89F LD A,(HL)
A8A0 DEC HL
A8A1 LD L,(HL)
A8A2 LD H,A
A8A3 POP AF
A8A4 RET
A8A5: Restore regs frame
Used by the routine at proc_table.
Pops all registers back.
restore_regs_frame A8A5 POP HL
A8A6 POP IY
A8A8 POP IX
A8AA EX AF,AF'
A8AB EXX
A8AC POP AF
A8AD POP BC
A8AE POP DE
A8AF POP HL
A8B0 EX AF,AF'
A8B1 EXX
A8B2 POP AF
A8B3 POP BC
A8B4 POP DE
A8B5 EX (SP),HL
A8B6 RET
Initial values for play_timer
tbl_play_timer_init A8B7 DEFW $0500,$0600,$0700,$0800
A8BF DEFW $0900,$1000,$1100,$1200
str_player1 A8C7 DEFM "PLAYER 1"
A8CF DEFB $00
str_player2 A8D0 DEFM "PLAYER 2"
A8D8 DEFB $00
A8D9: Data block at A8D9
attr_rle A8D9 DEFB $08,$04,$02,$00,$0C,$06,$02,$00
A8E1 DEFB $08,$04,$08,$07,$10,$06,$08,$07
A8E9 DEFB $20,$00
A8EB: Setup attrs
Used by the routine at proc_table.
Paint the static playfield attribute layout and the two PLAYER score labels.
Run once at start (via jp_setup_attrs).
Reads attr_rle (9 run-length [count,attr] pairs) and floods the attribute area 5800+ with attr|$40 (BRIGHT set on every cell).
Then prints the PLAYER 1 / PLAYER 2 strings and seeds two score/counter widgets via helper calls.
In: nothing.
Clobbers: A,BC,DE,HL.
setup_attrs A8EB LD A,$FF A = $FF
A8ED LD ($60C8),A store $FF to a flag at cur_color
A8F0 LD HL,$A8D9 HL -> attr_rle (run-length attribute table)
A8F3 LD DE,$5800 DE -> 5800 = attribute area base
A8F6 LD C,$09 C = 9 outer groups
setup_attrs_0 A8F8 LD B,(HL) B = run length for this group
A8F9 INC HL
A8FA LD A,(HL) A = attribute byte for this group
A8FB OR $40 force BRIGHT bit ($40) on
A8FD INC HL
setup_attrs_1 A8FE LD (DE),A write attr to cell
A8FF INC DE
A900 DJNZ setup_attrs_1
A902 DEC C
A903 JR NZ,setup_attrs_0 next group until C groups done
A905 LD HL,$A8C7 HL -> 'PLAYER 1' string
A908 LD BC,$0000 BC = 0x0000 = attr/screen position arg
A90B CALL print_string Print string
A90E LD BC,$1800 BC = 0x1800 = position for 'PLAYER 2'
A911 CALL print_string Print string
A914 LD BC,$0900
A917 LD A,$A8
A919 CALL $AAC2
A91C LD BC,$1600
A91F LD A,$A7
A921 CALL $AAC2
A924 LD BC,$0002
A927 CALL draw_hud_bar Draw HUD bar
A92A LD BC,$1802
A92D CALL draw_hud_bar Draw HUD bar
A930 CALL reset_score_state Reset score state
A933 JP draw_hud_counters tail-jump into the next setup step
A936: Draw HUD bar
Used by the routine at setup_attrs.
At the print position in BC, print 8 copies of glyph $81 (a solid block) -- the score-panel divider bar.
In: BC=print pos.
Clobbers: A,B.
draw_hud_bar A936 CALL set_print_pos
A939 LD A,$81
A93B LD B,$08
draw_hud_bar_0 A93D CALL print_char Print char
A940 DJNZ draw_hud_bar_0
A942 RET
A943: Reset score state
Used by the routines at proc_table and setup_attrs.
Init the score/lives HUD state for a new game: lives_p2 (lives_p2)=3, $6042=3, $60C2=0, and zero the 8-byte block $6043..$604A (the two 4-byte BCD score buffers).
Reached from proc_table slot $A721 (JP) and from $A930 (CALL, then falls through to draw_hud_counters).
reset_score_state A943 LD A,$03
A945 LD ($6041),A set Lives
A948 LD ($6042),A
A94B XOR A
A94C LD ($60C2),A
A94F LD HL,$6043
A952 LD B,$08
reset_score_state_0 A954 LD (HL),A
A955 INC HL
A956 DJNZ reset_score_state_0
A958 RET
A959: Draw HUD counters
Used by the routines at proc_table and setup_attrs.
Paint the score/lives panel.
Sets the '1UP'/'2UP' marker attribute cells (toggled by lives_p1), then prints lives_p2/$6042 as 2-digit decimals and the two BCD score buffers via set_print_pos + print_bcd_byte.
Clobbers: A,BC,DE,HL.
draw_hud_counters A959 LD A,$FF
A95B LD ($60C8),A set Current text colour/attribute
A95E LD HL,$0046
A961 LD A,($6040) get Lives
A964 OR A
A965 JR Z,draw_hud_counters_0
A967 LD HL,$4600
draw_hud_counters_0 A96A LD A,L
A96B LD ($5809),A
A96E LD A,H
A96F LD ($5816),A
A972 LD HL,$5840
A975 LD A,($6041) get Lives
A978 CALL draw_gauge8
A97B LD HL,$5858
A97E LD A,($6042)
A981 CALL draw_gauge8
A984 LD BC,$0001
A987 LD HL,$6046
A98A CALL print_score_field Print score field
A98D LD BC,$1801
A990 LD HL,$604A
A993: Print score field
Used by the routine at draw_hud_counters.
Position the cursor (BC) then print a 2-byte (4-digit) BCD value at HL via print_bcd_word.
In: BC=pos, HL=MSB.
print_score_field A993 CALL set_print_pos
A996 CALL print_bcd_word
print_bcd_word A999 CALL print_bcd_byte
print_bcd_byte A99C LD A,(HL)
A99D RRA
A99E RRA
A99F RRA
A9A0 RRA
A9A1 CALL print_nibble
A9A4 LD A,(HL)
A9A5 DEC HL
print_nibble A9A6 AND $0F
A9A8 ADD A,$30
A9AA JP print_char
A9AD: Routine at A9AD
Used by the routine at draw_hud_counters.
draw_gauge8 A9AD LD B,$08
A9AF LD C,A
draw_gauge8_0 A9B0 LD A,$00
A9B2 DEC C
A9B3 JP M,draw_gauge8_1
A9B6 LD A,$42
draw_gauge8_1 A9B8 LD (HL),A
A9B9 INC HL
A9BA DJNZ draw_gauge8_0
A9BC RET
A9BD: Add BCD score
Used by the routine at proc_table.
Add HL (16-bit BCD delta) to the active player's 4-byte BCD score (lives_p1 selects $6043 or $6047), with DAA carry propagation across all 4 bytes.
In: HL=BCD delta, lives_p1=index.
Clobbers: A,B,C,DE.
add_bcd_score A9BD LD DE,$6043
A9C0 LD A,($6040) get Lives
A9C3 OR A
A9C4 JR Z,add_bcd_score_0
A9C6 LD DE,$6047
add_bcd_score_0 A9C9 LD A,(DE)
A9CA ADD A,L
A9CB DAA
A9CC LD (DE),A
A9CD INC DE
A9CE LD A,(DE)
A9CF LD B,A
A9D0 ADC A,H
A9D1 DAA
A9D2 LD (DE),A
A9D3 LD C,A
A9D4 INC DE
A9D5 LD A,(DE)
A9D6 ADC A,$00
A9D8 LD (DE),A
A9D9 INC DE
A9DA LD A,(DE)
A9DB ADC A,$00
A9DD LD (DE),A
A9DE LD A,B
A9DF AND $F0
A9E1 CP $40
A9E3 JR NZ,add_bcd_score_1
A9E5 LD A,C
A9E6 AND $F0
A9E8 CP $50
A9EA JR Z,add_bcd_score_2
add_bcd_score_1 A9EC LD A,B
A9ED AND $F0
A9EF CP $90
A9F1 RET NZ
A9F2 LD A,C
A9F3 AND $F0
A9F5 RET NZ
add_bcd_score_2 A9F6 LD HL,$6041
A9F9 LD A,($6040) get Lives
A9FC OR A
A9FD JR Z,add_bcd_score_3
A9FF INC HL
add_bcd_score_3 AA00 INC (HL)
AA01 LD A,(HL)
AA02 CP $08
AA04 RET C
AA05 LD (HL),$08
AA07 RET
AA08: Init bonus
Used by the routine at proc_table.
Seed the active player's (lives_p1 index) bonus/ accumulator block at $604B: bonus = BCD 0100, $604F-side cleared.
Clobbers: A,HL.
init_bonus AA08 LD HL,$604B
AA0B LD A,($6040) get Lives
AA0E OR A
AA0F JR Z,init_bonus_0
AA11 INC HL
AA12 INC HL
init_bonus_0 AA13 LD (HL),$00
AA15 INC HL
AA16 LD (HL),$01
AA18 INC HL
AA19 INC HL
AA1A INC HL
AA1B LD (HL),$00
AA1D INC HL
AA1E LD (HL),$00
AA20 RET
AA21: Bonus sub
Used by the routine at proc_table.
BCD-subtract B from the active player's bonus value ($604B, 2 bytes), clamping at 0 on underflow (RET NC else zero it).
In: A/B=BCD amount.
Clobbers: A,B,HL.
bonus_sub AA21 LD B,A
AA22 LD HL,$604B
AA25 LD A,($6040) get Lives
AA28 OR A
AA29 JR Z,bonus_sub_0
AA2B INC HL
AA2C INC HL
bonus_sub_0 AA2D LD A,(HL)
AA2E SUB B
AA2F DAA
AA30 LD (HL),A
AA31 INC HL
AA32 LD A,(HL)
AA33 SBC A,$00
AA35 DAA
AA36 LD (HL),A
AA37 RET NC
AA38 XOR A
AA39 LD (HL),A
AA3A DEC HL
AA3B LD (HL),A
AA3C RET
AA3D: Add to bonus field 604F
Used by the routine at proc_table.
BCD-add B to the active player's 2-byte field at $604F, saturating: high byte hitting 6 resets to 05/low=00.
In: A/B=BCD amount.
Clobbers: A,B,HL.
field_604f_add AA3D LD B,A
AA3E LD HL,$604F
AA41 LD A,($6040) get Lives
AA44 OR A
AA45 JR Z,field_604f_add_0
AA47 INC HL
AA48 INC HL
field_604f_add_0 AA49 LD A,(HL)
AA4A ADD A,B
AA4B DAA
AA4C LD (HL),A
AA4D INC HL
AA4E LD A,(HL)
AA4F ADC A,$00
AA51 LD (HL),A
AA52 CP $06
AA54 RET C
AA55 LD (HL),$05
AA57 DEC HL
AA58 LD (HL),$00
AA5A RET
AA5B: Set level timer
Used by the routine at proc_table.
Load play_timer from the word table at tbl_play_timer_init indexed by (6039)*2 (per-level start time), then fall into draw_timer to show it.
Clobbers: A,DE,HL.
set_level_timer AA5B LD A,($6039)
AA5E ADD A,A
AA5F LD E,A
AA60 LD D,$00
AA62 LD HL,$A8B7
AA65 ADD HL,DE
AA66 LD E,(HL)
AA67 INC HL
AA68 LD D,(HL)
AA69 LD ($6053),DE set Level countdown timer
AA6D JP draw_timer
AA70: Tick timer
Used by the routine at proc_table.
Prescaled level-timer countdown.
6055 counts 0..4 only every 5th call does it BCD-decrement play_timer (2 bytes, clamped at 0) and redraw via draw_timer.
RET M while prescaler is negative (timer disabled).
Clobbers: A,HL.
tick_timer AA70 LD HL,$6055
AA73 LD A,(HL)
AA74 OR A
AA75 RET M
AA76 INC A
AA77 LD (HL),A
AA78 CP $05
AA7A RET C
AA7B LD (HL),$00
AA7D LD HL,$6053
AA80 LD A,(HL)
AA81 SUB $01
AA83 DAA
AA84 LD (HL),A
AA85 INC HL
AA86 LD A,(HL)
AA87 SBC A,$00
AA89 DAA
AA8A LD (HL),A
AA8B JP P,draw_timer
AA8E XOR A
AA8F LD (HL),A
AA90 DEC HL
AA91 LD (HL),A
AA92 DEC A
AA93 LD ($6055),A set Level timer prescaler
AA96: Draw timer
Used by the routines at proc_table, set_level_timer and tick_timer.
Print the 2-byte BCD play_timer ($6054) as decimal digits at print row $0E (white).
Uses print_2digits/print_digit.
Clobbers: A,BC,HL.
draw_timer AA96 LD A,$FF
AA98 LD ($60C8),A set Current text colour/attribute
AA9B LD BC,$0E00
AA9E CALL set_print_pos Set print pos
AAA1 LD HL,$6054
AAA4 CALL print_2digits
AAA7 DEC HL
print_2digits AAA8 LD A,(HL)
AAA9 RRA
AAAA RRA
AAAB RRA
AAAC RRA
AAAD CALL print_digit
AAB0 LD A,(HL)
print_digit AAB1 AND $0F
AAB3 ADD A,$30
AAB5 JP print_char
AAB8: Data block at AAB8
punct_map AAB8 DEFB $20,$00
AABA DEFM "<(>'"
AABE: Data block at AABE
AABE DEFB $40,$26,$2A,$19,$CD,$7A,$AB
AAC5: Print char
Used by the routines at proc_table, draw_hud_bar, print_score_field, draw_timer and print_string.
Render one character at the current text cursor.
In: A = character code.
>=$80 : direct glyph index (graphics char), A&$7F.
'A'..'Z' : glyph = (A-'A')+2.
'0'..'9' : glyph = (A-'0')+$1C.
<$10 : colour/attribute control code -> cur_color.
else : looked up in punct_map.
cursor in print_cursor (print_cursor lo=col) cur_color (cur_color).
Out: glyph blitted to screen column cursor ($60C7) advanced.
print_char AAC5 PUSH AF save all regs (caller-transparent)
AAC6 PUSH BC
AAC7 PUSH DE
AAC8 PUSH HL
AAC9 BIT 7,A bit7 set? -> direct graphics glyph index
AACB JR Z,print_char_0
AACD AND $7F strip bit7, use as glyph index
AACF JR glyph_addr
print_char_0 AAD1 CP $41 'A'($41)..'Z'($5A)?
AAD3 JR C,print_char_1
AAD5 CP $5B
AAD7 JR NC,print_char_1
AAD9 SUB $41 glyph = (A-'A') + 2 (letters start at glyph 2)
AADB ADD A,$02
AADD JR glyph_addr
print_char_1 AADF CP $30 '0'($30)..'9'($39)?
AAE1 JR C,print_char_2
AAE3 CP $3A
AAE5 JR NC,print_char_2
AAE7 SUB $30 glyph = (A-'0') + $1C (digits start at glyph $1C)
AAE9 ADD A,$1C
AAEB JR glyph_addr
print_char_2 AAED CP $10 code <$10? -> colour control code
AAEF JR NC,print_char_4
AAF1 LD B,A
AAF2 AND $07 ink = A & 7
AAF4 BIT 3,B bit3 of code = BRIGHT -> OR $40
AAF6 JR Z,print_char_3
AAF8 OR $40
print_char_3 AAFA LD ($60C8),A store into cur_color (cur_color)
AAFD JR print_char_9
print_char_4 AAFF LD HL,$AAB8 HL -> punct_map (5 entries)
AB02 LD B,$05
print_char_5 AB04 CP (HL) compare code against each map char
AB05 JR Z,print_char_6
AB07 INC HL
AB08 INC HL
AB09 DJNZ print_char_5
AB0B JR print_char_9
print_char_6 AB0D INC HL
AB0E LD A,(HL) matched: A = glyph index from map
glyph_addr AB0F LD L,A L = glyph index
AB10 ADD A,A A = index*6 via index + index*2, then *2 (6 bytes/glyph)
AB11 ADD A,L
AB12 ADD A,A
AB13 LD L,A HL = index*6
AB14 LD H,$00
AB16 LD DE,$C000 DE = font_base = font base
AB19 ADD HL,DE HL -> glyph bitmap (6 bytes)
AB1A PUSH HL
AB1B LD BC,($60C6) BC = print_cursor (C=column 0..31)
glyph_blit AB1F LD A,C A = column
AB20 AND $18 H = (col & $18) | $40 : screen-addr high byte (third+page)
AB22 LD H,A
AB23 SET 6,H
AB25 RRCA
AB26 RRCA
AB27 RRCA
AB28 OR $58 OR $58 -> D = matching attribute-area high byte ($58xx)
AB2A LD D,A
AB2B LD A,C
AB2C AND $07
AB2E RRCA
AB2F RRCA
AB30 RRCA
AB31 ADD A,B
AB32 LD L,A
AB33 LD E,A
AB34 LD A,($60C8) A = cur_color
AB37 CP $FF $FF = 'transparent' -> skip attribute write
AB39 JR Z,print_char_7
AB3B LD (DE),A write colour to attribute cell
print_char_7 AB3C POP DE
AB3D LD (HL),$00 blank top scanline of the cell
AB3F INC H INC H = step down one pixel row (Z80 ZX adjacency)
AB40 LD B,$06 B = 6 font rows
print_char_8 AB42 LD A,(DE) copy one font row to screen
AB43 LD (HL),A
AB44 INC DE
AB45 INC H
AB46 DJNZ print_char_8
AB48 LD (HL),$00 blank bottom scanline
AB4A LD HL,$60C7
AB4D INC (HL) advance column cursor ($60C7)
print_char_9 AB4E POP HL
AB4F POP DE
AB50 POP BC
AB51 POP AF
AB52 RET
AB53: Print string
Used by the routine at setup_attrs.
Print an ASCIIZ string at position BC.
In: HL=string (00-terminated), BC=cursor pos.
Out: HL past terminator.
print_string AB53 CALL set_print_pos set cursor = BC
This entry point is used by the routines at proc_table and print_centered.
print_string_loop AB56 LD A,(HL) A = next char
AB57 INC HL
AB58 OR A 00 terminator?
AB59 RET Z done (ASCIIZ) -- RET on Z
AB5A CALL print_char Print char
AB5D JR print_string_loop next char
AB5F: Print centered
Used by the routine at proc_table.
print_centered AB5F PUSH HL
AB60 LD C,$00 C = visible-char count
print_centered_0 AB62 LD A,(HL)
AB63 OR A
AB64 JR Z,print_centered_2
AB66 CP $10 ignore codes <$10 (colour controls) in the count
AB68 JR C,print_centered_1
AB6A INC C
print_centered_1 AB6B INC HL
AB6C JR print_centered_0
print_centered_2 AB6E LD A,$20 A = 32 - count
AB70 SUB C
AB71 RRA RRA = /2 = left margin
AB72 LD ($60C7),A store as start column ($60C7)
AB75 POP HL
AB76 CALL print_string_loop
AB79 RET
AB7A: Set print pos
Used by the routines at proc_table, draw_hud_bar, print_score_field, draw_timer and print_string.
set_print_pos AB7A LD ($60C6),BC
AB7E RET
AB7F: Clear obj table
Used by the routines at proc_table and setup_tower_scroll.
clear_obj_table AB7F LD HL,$6460
AB82 LD DE,$000D
AB85 LD B,$14
AB87 XOR A
clear_obj_table_0 AB88 LD (HL),A
AB89 INC HL
AB8A LD (HL),A
AB8B INC HL
AB8C LD (HL),A
AB8D ADD HL,DE
AB8E LD (HL),A
AB8F INC HL
AB90 LD (HL),A
AB91 INC HL
AB92 LD (HL),A
AB93 INC HL
AB94 DJNZ clear_obj_table_0
AB96 RET
AB97: Calc tower col addr
Used by the routines at proc_table and setup_tower_scroll.
Compute a byte address inside the $6400 tower/column buffer from disp_height (&7, *24) and scroll_pos, returning the slot pointer(s) for column rendering.
Clobbers: A,BC,DE,HL.
calc_tower_col_addr AB97 LD A,($6032) row offset = (disp_height&7)*24 into $6400
AB9A AND $07
AB9C LD B,A
AB9D ADD A,A
AB9E ADD A,B
AB9F ADD A,A
ABA0 ADD A,A
ABA1 LD C,A
ABA2 LD B,$00
ABA4 LD HL,$6400
ABA7 ADD HL,BC
ABA8 EX DE,HL
ABA9 ADD A,$30 column offset, wrapped mod $60 (96)
ABAB CP $60
ABAD JR C,calc_tower_col_addr_0
ABAF SUB $60
calc_tower_col_addr_0 ABB1 LD C,A
ABB2 LD HL,$6400 HL -> $6400+offset (primary column pointer)
ABB5 ADD HL,BC
ABB6 LD A,($6030) get Vertical scroll position
ABB9 AND $04 scroll_pos bit2 selects HL/DE swap
ABBB JR NZ,calc_tower_col_addr_1
ABBD EX DE,HL
calc_tower_col_addr_1 ABBE PUSH DE copy 2x12-byte snapshot from HL/DE into $6463/$6475 scratch
ABBF LD DE,$6463
ABC2 LDI
ABC4 LDI
ABC6 LDI
ABC8 LDI
ABCA LDI
ABCC LDI
ABCE LDI
ABD0 LDI
ABD2 LDI
ABD4 LDI
ABD6 LDI
ABD8 LDI
ABDA LD DE,$6475
ABDD POP HL
ABDE LDI
ABE0 LDI
ABE2 LDI
ABE4 LDI
ABE6 LDI
ABE8 LDI
ABEA LDI
ABEC LDI
ABEE LDI
ABF0 LDI
ABF2 LDI
ABF4 LDI
ABF6 LD HL,$6463 HL/DE -> $6463/$6487, A=$12 repeat count
ABF9 LD DE,$6487
ABFC LD A,$12
calc_tower_col_addr_2 ABFE LDI duplicate the 24-byte snapshot forward (stride 12) to extend the strip buffer
AC00 LDI
AC02 LDI
AC04 LDI
AC06 LDI
AC08 LDI
AC0A LDI
AC0C LDI
AC0E LDI
AC10 LDI
AC12 LDI
AC14 LDI
AC16 LD BC,$0006
AC19 ADD HL,BC
AC1A EX DE,HL
AC1B ADD HL,BC
AC1C EX DE,HL
AC1D DEC A
AC1E JP NZ,calc_tower_col_addr_2
AC21 RET
AC22: Col visible offset
Used by the routine at proc_table.
Compute the on-screen column offset of the object at $60BA relative to scroll_pos, folded into the 4-quadrant (mod $03) tower wrap.
Returns A=offset (RET Z if off-screen).
Clobbers: A,B,DE,HL.
col_visible_offset AC22 LD HL,($60BA)
AC25 LD A,L
AC26 SUB $28
AC28 LD L,A
AC29 LD A,H
AC2A SBC A,$00
AC2C AND $03
AC2E LD H,A
AC2F LD B,$00
AC31 LD DE,($6030) get Vertical scroll position
AC35 LD A,L
AC36 SUB E
AC37 LD L,A
AC38 LD A,H
AC39 SBC A,D
AC3A AND $03
AC3C CP $02
AC3E LD A,L
AC3F JR C,col_visible_offset_0
AC41 DEC B
AC42 NEG
col_visible_offset_0 AC44 OR A
AC45 RET Z
AC46 ADD A,$03
AC48 SRL A
AC4A SRL A
AC4C BIT 7,B
AC4E JR Z,col_visible_offset_1
AC50 NEG
col_visible_offset_1 AC52 LD HL,$6030
AC55 ADD A,(HL)
AC56 LD (HL),A
AC57 LD A,B
AC58 INC HL
AC59 ADC A,(HL)
AC5A AND $03
AC5C LD (HL),A
AC5D RET
AC5E: Blit scroll wrap
Used by the routine at proc_table.
When scroll_pos has wrapped past the top (H==$03 && L>=$D8), fast-fill the newly exposed strip using the LD SP,screen / stack-write trick (saves real SP to temp_sp, DI, reads mask_acb0).
RET early when no wrap is pending.
blit_scroll_wrap AC5E LD HL,($6030)
AC61 LD A,H
AC62 CP $03
AC64 RET NZ
AC65 LD A,L
AC66 CP $D8
AC68 RET C
AC69 XOR A
AC6A SUB L
AC6B SUB L
AC6C PUSH AF
AC6D LD B,A
AC6E LD A,$B8
AC70 SUB B
AC71 LD H,A
AC72 LD L,$1E
AC74 CALL get_screen_addr Get screen addr
AC77 POP AF
AC78 LD ($6000),SP set Temporary place for SP
AC7C DI
AC7D LD DE,$ACB0
blit_scroll_wrap_0 AC80 EX AF,AF'
AC81 LD SP,HL
AC82 EX DE,HL
AC83 LD C,(HL)
AC84 INC HL
AC85 LD B,C
AC86 PUSH BC
AC87 PUSH BC
AC88 PUSH BC
AC89 PUSH BC
AC8A PUSH BC
AC8B PUSH BC
AC8C PUSH BC
AC8D PUSH BC
AC8E PUSH BC
AC8F PUSH BC
AC90 PUSH BC
AC91 PUSH BC
AC92 PUSH BC
AC93 PUSH BC
AC94 EX DE,HL
AC95 INC H
AC96 LD A,H
AC97 AND $07
AC99 JR NZ,blit_scroll_wrap_1
AC9B LD A,L
AC9C ADD A,$20
AC9E LD L,A
AC9F JR C,blit_scroll_wrap_1
ACA1 LD A,H
ACA2 SUB $08
ACA4 LD H,A
blit_scroll_wrap_1 ACA5 EX AF,AF'
ACA6 DEC A
ACA7 JP NZ,blit_scroll_wrap_0
ACAA LD SP,($6000) get Temporary place for SP
ACAE EI
ACAF RET
ACB0: Data block at ACB0
mask_acb0 ACB0 DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$20,$00
ACB8 DEFB $08,$00,$40,$00,$02,$20,$00,$01
ACC0 DEFB $80,$04,$20,$08,$40,$02,$10,$01
ACC8 DEFB $88,$22,$44,$11,$88,$22,$44,$11
ACD0 DEFB $92,$25,$92,$45,$8A,$51,$A2,$51
ACD8 DEFB $55,$AA,$55,$AA,$55,$AA,$55,$AA
ACE0 DEFB $6D,$DA,$6D,$BA,$75,$AE,$5D,$AE
ACE8 DEFB $77,$DD,$BB,$EE,$77,$DD,$BB,$EE
ACF0 DEFB $7F,$FB,$DF,$F7,$BF,$FD,$EF,$FE
ACF8 DEFB $FF,$FF,$FF,$FF,$FF,$FF,$FF,$FF
AD00: Clear object list near scroll seam
Used by the routine at proc_table.
Called every frame from render_play_frame, before setup_tower_scroll.
Reads scroll_pos and checks whether it is near the tower's top or bottom wrap seam (two symmetric branches on the high byte, biased by +$15/+$13 and compared against the $603A level-column threshold). If near an edge, computes an overshoot count C and zero-fills C*9 bytes at $6460+ -- clearing stale object/sprite-list entries that would otherwise linger once the visible column range shrinks near the seam. If not near an edge (C==0), returns immediately.
Clobbers: A,BC,DE,HL.
code_ad00 AD00 LD C,$00
AD02 LD HL,($6030) get Vertical scroll position
AD05 LD A,H
AD06 CP $03
AD08 JR NZ,code_ad00_0
AD0A LD A,L
AD0B CP $D8
AD0D JR C,code_ad00_0
AD0F LD A,L
AD10 SRL H
AD12 RRA
AD13 SRL H
AD15 RRA
AD16 ADD A,$15
AD18 LD HL,$603A
AD1B CP (HL)
AD1C JR C,code_ad00_2
AD1E DEC A
AD1F SUB (HL)
AD20 JP code_ad00_1
code_ad00_0 AD23 LD A,L
AD24 SRL H
AD26 RRA
AD27 SRL H
AD29 RRA
AD2A ADD A,$13
AD2C LD HL,$603A
AD2F CP (HL)
AD30 JR C,code_ad00_2
AD32 INC A
AD33 SUB (HL)
code_ad00_1 AD34 LD C,A
code_ad00_2 AD35 LD A,C
AD36 OR A
AD37 RET Z
AD38 PUSH AF
AD39 ADD A,A
AD3A ADD A,A
AD3B ADD A,A
AD3C ADD A,C
AD3D ADD A,A
AD3E LD C,A
AD3F LD B,$00
AD41 DEC BC
AD42 LD HL,$6460
AD45 LD DE,$6461
AD48 LD (HL),$00
AD4A LDIR
AD4C POP AF
AD4D RET
AD4E: Anim bg phase
Used by the routine at proc_table.
Advance the background animation phase counter ($603D) on the wrap point reload $603C with $08 (animation restart).
Clobbers: A,HL.
anim_bg_phase AD4E LD HL,$603D
AD51 INC (HL)
AD52 LD A,(HL)
AD53 ADD A,A
AD54 AND $18
AD56 CP $18
AD58 JR NZ,anim_bg_phase_0
AD5A LD A,$08
anim_bg_phase_0 AD5C LD ($603C),A
AD5F RET
AD60: Data block at AD60
Self-modified scratch, not pure data: AD60/$AD61 are 1-byte accumulators seeded by setup_tower_scroll ($ADA1/$ADAF) with a row-start bias and a fine row offset, then incremented every pass of setup_tower_scroll's row loop ($AE6E-$AE81) by table bytes stored at $AD62..$AD80 (16 rows x 2-byte deltas) -- i.e. a per-row coarse/fine screen-row stepping table, not just static data.
AD60 DEFB $00,$00,$FF,$F8,$FF,$F8,$FF,$F8
AD68 DEFB $04,$20,$01,$08,$01,$08,$01,$08
AD70 DEFB $F8,$C0,$FF,$F8,$FF,$F8,$FF,$F8
AD78 DEFB $FC,$E0,$01,$08,$01,$08,$01,$08
AD80 DEFB $00,$00
AD82: Setup tower scroll
Used by the routine at proc_table.
Per-frame scroll/render setup.
Clears the object table, derives the coarse column ($ADC6) and sub-column fraction ($6021) from scroll_pos, and the row base ($AD61) from disp_height.
SELF-MODIFIES the render code ($ADC6/$AD61) and latches $6021 for draw_tower_cols.
Runs a 16-row loop ($ADB8..$AE85): for each row it reads two per-column-cell nibbles from map data at 6200+DE / $6280+DE, classifies the cell (bit-pattern tests at $AE21-$AE67) and jumps to one of the masked-blit handlers below (render_col_partial/AF2A/render_col_cap_b/render_col_fill/render_anim_texture) or straight to the row-accumulator advance at $AE6D. Each handler returns to the common continuation $AE18 (advance HL by -$12, DJNZ loop) before the next row.
Clobbers: A,C,HL.
setup_tower_scroll AD82 CALL clear_obj_table clear object table (calc_tower_col_addr's prep)
AD85 LD HL,($6030) get Vertical scroll position
AD88 LD A,L
AD89 SRL H shift scroll_pos right by 3 (coarse column)
AD8B RRA
AD8C SRL H
AD8E RRA
AD8F LD ($ADC6),A self-mod store: coarse column ($ADC6)
AD92 LD A,L
AD93 AND $03 isolate sub-column fraction bits
AD95 ADD A,A
AD96 LD ($6021),A latch sub-column phase ($6021)
AD99 LD A,($6032) get Display height
AD9C LD C,A
AD9D AND $07 isolate row-in-cell bits (disp_height&7)
AD9F ADD A,$58 bias by $58 (attribute-row base)
ADA1 LD ($AD61),A self-mod store: row base ($AD61)
ADA4 SRL C C = disp_height >> 3 (coarse row)
ADA6 SRL C
ADA8 SRL C
ADAA LD A,$0B A = $0B - coarse row
ADAC SUB C
ADAD AND $0F mask to 4 bits
ADAF LD ($AD60),A self-mod store: row accumulator (AD60)
ADB2 EXX
ADB3 LD B,$00 B = 0 (row counter)
ADB5 LD HL,$AD62 HL -> per-row delta table ($AD62)
setup_tower_scroll_0 ADB8 LD A,B A = row counter
ADB9 CP $08 row==8? recompute column addr mid-loop
ADBB JP NZ,setup_tower_scroll_1
ADBE PUSH BC
ADBF PUSH HL
ADC0 CALL calc_tower_col_addr Calc tower col addr
ADC3 POP HL
ADC4 POP BC
setup_tower_scroll_1 ADC5 LD E,$00 E = 0 (cell index low)
ADC7 LD A,($AD60) D = row accumulator + $F0 (map page)
ADCA ADD A,$F0
ADCC LD D,A
ADCD EXX
ADCE LD A,($AD61) E = column fraction (map offset low)
ADD1 LD E,A
ADD2 LD D,$00
ADD4 LD HL,$6200 HL -> 6200 + column (cell type table 1)
ADD7 ADD HL,DE
ADD8 LD A,(HL)
ADD9 OR A negative? -> $AE6D (empty cell, skip)
ADDA JP M,setup_tower_scroll_11
ADDD SRL A shift cell nibble right 2
ADDF SRL A
ADE1 LD ($AE35),A self-mod: patch two render-handler operands
ADE4 LD ($AE4B),A
ADE7 LD A,(HL) A = cell byte again, mask low 2 bits
ADE8 AND $03
ADEA LD ($AF2D),A self-mod: patch two more render-handler operands
ADED LD ($AFD3),A
ADF0 LD HL,$6280 HL -> $6280 + column (cell type table 2)
ADF3 ADD HL,DE
ADF4 LD A,(HL)
ADF5 OR A
ADF6 JP M,setup_tower_scroll_2 negative? skip the shift
ADF9 SRL A shift cell nibble right 2
ADFB SRL A
setup_tower_scroll_2 ADFD LD ($AE40),A self-mod: patch two render-handler operands
AE00 LD ($AE58),A
AE03 LD A,(HL) A = 2nd cell byte, mask low 2 bits
AE04 AND $03
AE06 LD ($AF89),A self-mod: patch two more render-handler operands
AE09 LD ($B044),A
AE0C LD B,$14 B = $14 (20 sub-cells to classify)
AE0E LD HL,$65B6 HL -> classification bitmap ($65B6)
setup_tower_scroll_3 AE11 EXX fetch next classification bit
AE12 LD A,(DE)
AE13 INC E
AE14 EXX
AE15 ADD A,A shift bit into carry
AE16 JR C,setup_tower_scroll_5
This entry point is used by the routines at render_col_partial, AF2A, render_col_cap_b, render_col_fill and render_anim_texture.
setup_tower_scroll_4 AE18 LD DE,$FFEE DE = -$12 (back up dest ptr)
AE1B ADD HL,DE
AE1C DJNZ setup_tower_scroll_3 loop 20 sub-cells
AE1E JP setup_tower_scroll_11
setup_tower_scroll_5 AE21 RRA shift in the next bit
AE22 LD C,A C = classification byte so far
AE23 AND $0C test bits $0C (cell sub-type)
AE25 JR Z,setup_tower_scroll_6
AE27 BIT 0,C test bit 0 of C
AE29 JR NZ,setup_tower_scroll_8
setup_tower_scroll_6 AE2B LD A,C
AE2C AND $03 mask to bits 0-1
AE2E SRL A shift right (test bit 0)
AE30 JR NZ,setup_tower_scroll_9 nonzero -> $AE55 (anim-texture case)
AE32 JR NC,setup_tower_scroll_7 no carry -> $AE3F (cap-b case)
AE34 LD DE,$0000 self-mod operand for the handler call
AE37 BIT 7,E test bit 7 of E
AE39 JP Z,$AF2A dispatch -> render_col_cap_a
AE3C JP setup_tower_scroll_4
setup_tower_scroll_7 AE3F LD DE,$0000
AE42 BIT 7,E
AE44 JP Z,render_col_cap_b
AE47 JP setup_tower_scroll_4
setup_tower_scroll_8 AE4A LD DE,$0000
AE4D BIT 7,E
AE4F JP Z,render_col_fill
AE52 JP setup_tower_scroll_4
setup_tower_scroll_9 AE55 JR C,setup_tower_scroll_10 carry set -> $AE62 (partial-column case)
AE57 LD DE,$0000
AE5A BIT 7,E
AE5C JP Z,render_anim_texture
AE5F JP setup_tower_scroll_4
setup_tower_scroll_10 AE62 LD A,($AD61) A = row base
AE65 CP $40 compare vs $40 (screen-half boundary)
AE67 JP C,render_col_partial dispatch -> render_col_partial
AE6A JP setup_tower_scroll_4
setup_tower_scroll_11 AE6D EXX EXX back to main register set
AE6E LD A,($AD60) A = row accumulator
AE71 ADD A,(HL) add per-row delta, wrap to 4 bits
AE72 AND $0F
AE74 LD ($AD60),A store updated row accumulator
AE77 INC HL
AE78 LD A,($AD61) A = fine row offset
AE7B ADD A,(HL) add per-row delta, wrap to 7 bits
AE7C AND $7F
AE7E LD ($AD61),A store updated fine row offset
AE81 INC HL
AE82 INC B B++ (row counter)
AE83 BIT 4,B done all 16 rows?
AE85 JP Z,setup_tower_scroll_0
AE88 RET
AE89: Data block at AE89
24-entry (2 bytes each) AND/OR mask-pair table: "AND clear old bits, OR in new bits" merge masks for column pixel bytes that don't align to a whole screen byte (edge-clipped column tops/bottoms). Read by the handler at render_col_partial.
col_blit_masks AE89 DEFB $FF,$00,$FC,$02,$F3,$08,$F0,$0A
AE91 DEFB $CF,$20,$CC,$00,$C3,$28,$C0,$22
AE99 DEFB $3F,$80,$3C,$00,$33,$00,$30,$00
AEA1 DEFB $0F,$A0,$0C,$00,$03,$88,$00,$82
AEA9 DEFB $00,$80,$C0,$20,$F0,$08,$FC,$02
AEB1 DEFB $3F,$80,$0F,$20,$03,$08,$00,$02
AEB9 DEFB $00,$00
AEBB: Render col partial
Used by the routine at setup_tower_scroll.
One of setup_tower_scroll's five per-cell render handlers -- the "partial/short column" case.
Looks up a length adjustment (table $6300) and an edge-type value (table $6340) indexed by the column (DE), computes and self-mod-stores a destination pointer ($AEB9), then masked-merges the destination byte(s) using mask pairs picked from col_blit_masks (col_blit_masks, 16-entry branch for tall/negative case) or from a second 4-entry table at $AEA9 (short/positive case), plus a run of up to 3 plain-store bytes and a final 2-entry table ($AEB1) merge.
Ends by jumping back to setup_tower_scroll's row loop continuation ($AE18).
Clobbers: A,DE,HL.
render_col_partial AEBB PUSH BC DE=A; HL -> length table $6300+DE
AEBC PUSH HL
AEBD LD E,A
AEBE LD D,$00
AEC0 LD HL,$6300
AEC3 ADD HL,DE
AEC4 LD A,(HL) length+3 -> destination byte offset
AEC5 ADD A,$03
AEC7 LD C,A
AEC8 LD B,$00
AECA POP HL
AECB PUSH HL
AECC ADD HL,BC
AECD LD ($AEB9),HL self-mod store: destination pointer ($AEB9)
AED0 LD HL,$6340 HL -> edge-type table $6340+DE
AED3 ADD HL,DE
AED4 LD A,(HL)
AED5 OR A
AED6 JP P,render_col_partial_0 sign bit set? -> short/positive branch
AED9 AND $0F tall branch: index col_blit_masks (col_blit_masks) by (type&$0F)*2
AEDB ADD A,A
AEDC LD E,A
AEDD LD HL,$AE89
AEE0 ADD HL,DE
AEE1 LD DE,($AEB9)
AEE5 LD A,(DE) AND/OR merge one byte, restore regs, return
AEE6 AND (HL)
AEE7 INC HL
AEE8 OR (HL)
AEE9 LD (DE),A
AEEA POP HL
AEEB POP BC
AEEC JP setup_tower_scroll_4
render_col_partial_0 AEEF LD C,A short branch: index 2nd mask table $AEA9 by (type&3)*2
AEF0 AND $03
AEF2 ADD A,A
AEF3 LD E,A
AEF4 LD HL,$AEA9
AEF7 ADD HL,DE
AEF8 LD DE,($AEB9)
AEFC LD A,(DE) AND/OR merge first byte, advance DE
AEFD AND (HL)
AEFE INC HL
AEFF OR (HL)
AF00 LD (DE),A
AF01 INC DE
AF02 LD A,C C = type; test bits $30 (extra-byte count)
AF03 AND $30
AF05 JR Z,render_col_partial_3
AF07 CP $20 write 0/1/2 plain bytes depending on $30 bits
AF09 LD A,$00
AF0B JR C,render_col_partial_2
AF0D JR Z,render_col_partial_1
AF0F LD (DE),A
AF10 INC DE
render_col_partial_1 AF11 LD (DE),A
AF12 INC DE
render_col_partial_2 AF13 LD (DE),A
AF14 INC DE
render_col_partial_3 AF15 LD A,C C = (type>>1)&$06 -> index final 2-entry table $AEB1
AF16 RRA
AF17 AND $06
AF19 LD C,A
AF1A LD B,$00
AF1C LD HL,$AEB1
AF1F ADD HL,BC
AF20 LD A,(DE) AND/OR merge last byte, restore regs, return
AF21 AND (HL)
AF22 INC HL
AF23 OR (HL)
AF24 LD (DE),A
AF25 POP HL
AF26 POP BC
AF27 JP setup_tower_scroll_4
AF2A: Render col cap a
Used by the routine at setup_tower_scroll.
Another per-cell render handler, selected for a specific column-edge sub-case (DE==0 test in setup_tower_scroll's classification).
4-way branch on a self-mod test byte ($AF2C/$AF2E, patched by setup_tower_scroll at $ADEA/$ADED). Each branch writes 3 destination bytes with mostly-fixed bit patterns (pre-rendered tower-cap/edge tile graphics) merged via AND/OR masks at the first/last byte to blend with the adjacent column.
Ends `POP HL / JP $AE18` in all 4 branches.
AF2A PUSH HL save dest ptr
AF2B ADD HL,DE HL += DE (apply column offset)
AF2C LD A,$00 A = self-mod test byte
AF2E SRL A shift test byte -> Z/C flags
AF30 JP NZ,$AF57 nonzero -> branch 3/4 ($AF57)
AF33 JP C,$AF42 carry set -> branch 2 ($AF42)
AF36 LD (HL),$1B branch 1: fixed byte 1 ($1B)
AF38 INC HL
AF39 LD (HL),$FB fixed byte 2 ($FB)
AF3B INC HL
AF3C LD (HL),$A4 fixed byte 3 ($A4)
AF3E POP HL restore dest ptr
AF3F JP setup_tower_scroll_4 done -> setup_tower_scroll continuation
AF42 LD A,(HL) branch 2: A = dest byte 1
AF43 AND $C0 keep high 2 bits (blend mask)
AF45 OR $06 OR in fixed low bits
AF47 LD (HL),A
AF48 INC HL
AF49 LD (HL),$FE fixed byte 2 ($FE)
AF4B INC HL
AF4C LD (HL),$E9 fixed byte 3 ($E9)
AF4E INC HL
AF4F LD A,(HL) A = dest byte 3
AF50 AND $3F keep low 6 bits (blend mask)
AF52 LD (HL),A
AF53 POP HL
AF54 JP setup_tower_scroll_4
AF57 JP C,$AF71 branch 3/4: carry set -> branch 4 ($AF71)
AF5A LD A,(HL) branch 3: A = dest byte 1
AF5B AND $F0 keep high nibble (blend mask)
AF5D OR $01 OR in fixed low bit
AF5F LD (HL),A
AF60 INC HL
AF61 LD (HL),$BF fixed byte 2 ($BF)
AF63 INC HL
AF64 LD (HL),$BA fixed byte 3 ($BA)
AF66 INC HL
AF67 LD A,(HL) A = dest byte 3
AF68 AND $0F keep low nibble (blend mask)
AF6A OR $40 OR in fixed high bits
AF6C LD (HL),A
AF6D POP HL
AF6E JP setup_tower_scroll_4
AF71 LD A,(HL) branch 4: A = dest byte 1
AF72 AND $FC keep high 6 bits (blend mask)
AF74 LD (HL),A
AF75 INC HL
AF76 LD (HL),$6F fixed byte 2 ($6F)
AF78 INC HL
AF79 LD (HL),$EE fixed byte 3 ($EE)
AF7B INC HL
AF7C LD A,(HL) A = dest byte 3
AF7D AND $03 keep low 2 bits (blend mask)
AF7F OR $90 OR in fixed high bits
AF81 LD (HL),A
AF82 POP HL
AF83 JP setup_tower_scroll_4
AF86: Render col cap b
Used by the routine at setup_tower_scroll.
Structurally identical 4-way dispatch to render_col_cap_a (self-mod test byte at $AF89, patched by $AE06/$AE09), but writes only 2 destination bytes per branch instead of 3 -- a narrower cap/edge graphic for a different column class.
Ends `POP HL / JP $AE18` in all branches.
render_col_cap_b AF86 PUSH HL
AF87 ADD HL,DE
AF88 LD A,$00
AF8A SRL A
AF8C JP NZ,render_col_cap_b_1
AF8F JP C,render_col_cap_b_0
AF92 LD (HL),$2F
AF94 INC HL
AF95 LD A,(HL)
AF96 AND $0F
AF98 OR $80
AF9A LD (HL),A
AF9B POP HL
AF9C JP setup_tower_scroll_4
render_col_cap_b_0 AF9F LD A,(HL)
AFA0 AND $C0
AFA2 OR $0B
AFA4 LD (HL),A
AFA5 INC HL
AFA6 LD A,(HL)
AFA7 AND $03
AFA9 OR $E0
AFAB LD (HL),A
AFAC POP HL
AFAD JP setup_tower_scroll_4
render_col_cap_b_1 AFB0 JP C,render_col_cap_b_2
AFB3 LD A,(HL)
AFB4 AND $F0
AFB6 OR $02
AFB8 LD (HL),A
AFB9 INC HL
AFBA LD (HL),$F8
AFBC POP HL
AFBD JP setup_tower_scroll_4
render_col_cap_b_2 AFC0 LD A,(HL)
AFC1 AND $FC
AFC3 LD (HL),A
AFC4 INC HL
AFC5 LD (HL),$BE
AFC7 INC HL
AFC8 LD A,(HL)
AFC9 AND $3F
AFCB LD (HL),A
AFCC POP HL
AFCD JP setup_tower_scroll_4
AFD0: Render col fill
Used by the routine at setup_tower_scroll.
Third 4-way dispatch variant (test byte self-mod at $AFD3, sharing the $ADEA/$ADED patch point with render_col_partial's mask selection), writing up to 3 bytes with mostly solid-$FF fill patterns -- a plain/bright tower-segment fill rather than a textured cap. One branch ($B000) has a nested sub-branch (JP C,$B016), making 5 cases total.
Ends `POP HL / JP $AE18` in all branches.
render_col_fill AFD0 PUSH HL save dest ptr
AFD1 ADD HL,DE HL += DE (apply column offset)
AFD2 LD A,$00 A = self-mod test byte
AFD4 SRL A shift test byte -> Z/C flags
AFD6 JP NZ,render_col_fill_1 nonzero -> branch 3/4 ($B000)
AFD9 JP C,render_col_fill_0 carry set -> branch 2 ($AFF0)
AFDC LD A,(HL) branch 1: A = dest byte 1
AFDD AND $C0 keep high 2 bits (blend mask)
AFDF OR $0F OR in fixed low bits
AFE1 LD (HL),A
AFE2 INC HL
AFE3 LD (HL),$FF solid fill byte 2 ($FF)
AFE5 INC HL
AFE6 LD A,(HL) A = dest byte 3
AFE7 AND $03 keep low 2 bits (blend mask)
AFE9 OR $F0
AFEB LD (HL),A
AFEC POP HL
AFED JP setup_tower_scroll_4
render_col_fill_0 AFF0 LD A,(HL) branch 2: A = dest byte 1
AFF1 AND $F0 keep high nibble (blend mask)
AFF3 OR $03
AFF5 LD (HL),A
AFF6 INC HL
AFF7 LD (HL),$FF solid fill byte 2 ($FF)
AFF9 INC HL
AFFA LD (HL),$FC solid fill byte 3 ($FC)
AFFC POP HL
AFFD JP setup_tower_scroll_4
render_col_fill_1 B000 JP C,render_col_fill_2 branch 3/4: carry set -> branch 4 ($B016)
B003 LD A,(HL) branch 3: A = dest byte 1
B004 AND $FC keep high 6 bits (blend mask)
B006 LD (HL),A
B007 INC HL
B008 LD (HL),$FF solid fill byte 2 ($FF)
B00A INC HL
B00B LD (HL),$FF solid fill byte 3 ($FF)
B00D INC HL
B00E LD A,(HL) A = dest byte 3
B00F AND $3F keep low 6 bits (blend mask)
B011 LD (HL),A
B012 POP HL
B013 JP setup_tower_scroll_4
render_col_fill_2 B016 INC HL branch 4: skip byte 1 (no write)
B017 LD (HL),$3F solid fill byte 2 ($3F)
B019 INC HL
B01A LD (HL),$FF solid fill byte 3 ($FF)
B01C INC HL
B01D LD A,(HL) A = dest byte 3
B01E AND $0F keep low nibble (blend mask)
B020 OR $C0 OR in fixed high bits
B022 LD (HL),A
B023 POP HL
B024 JP setup_tower_scroll_4
B027: Data block at B027
3 groups of 8 bytes (mask/value pairs), indexed by the $603C animation phase counter (anim_bg_phase) via render_anim_texture (render_anim_texture) -- an animated dithered/striped tower texture that cycles as $603C advances.
anim_texture_masks B027 DEFB $3F,$C0,$0F,$F0,$03,$FC,$FF,$00
B02F DEFB $2A,$80,$0A,$A0,$02,$A8,$AA,$00
B037 DEFB $15,$40,$05,$50,$01,$54,$55,$00
B03F: Render anim texture
Used by the routine at setup_tower_scroll.
Final per-cell render handler, and the only one whose graphic depends on animation phase rather than static column geometry: reads $603C into BC as an index into anim_texture_masks (anim_texture_masks), giving an animated tower stripe/shade effect.
Same self-mod 4-way branch-and-mask-merge shape as the other handlers (test byte at $B045), each branch copying/merging bytes from a different anim_texture_masks offset ($B029/$B02B/$B02D).
Ends `POP BC / POP HL / JP $AE18` in all branches.
render_anim_texture B03F PUSH HL save HL (destination byte ptr)
B040 ADD HL,DE DE += HL (advance to next cell)
B041 EX DE,HL swap: HL=dest ptr, DE=advance amount
B042 PUSH BC save BC
B043 LD A,$00 A = self-mod test byte (patched by setup_tower_scroll)
B045 SRL A shift test byte -> Z/C flags
B047 LD A,($603C) A = anim_bg_phase ($603C)
B04A LD C,A C = phase (BC = table index)
B04B LD B,$00
B04D JP NZ,render_anim_texture_1 test byte nonzero -> $B078
B050 JP C,render_anim_texture_0 test byte carry set -> $B063
B053 LD HL,$B027 HL -> anim_texture_masks + phase
B056 ADD HL,BC
B057 LDI copy one animated byte via LDI
B059 LD A,(DE) A = current dest byte
B05A AND $0F mask low nibble
B05C OR (HL) OR in the animated bits
B05D LD (DE),A
B05E POP BC
B05F POP HL
B060 JP setup_tower_scroll_4
render_anim_texture_0 B063 LD HL,$B029 HL -> $B029 + phase (2nd branch base)
B066 ADD HL,BC
B067 LD A,(DE) A = current dest byte
B068 AND $C0 mask high 2 bits ($C0)
B06A OR (HL)
B06B LD (DE),A
B06C INC HL
B06D INC DE
B06E LD A,(DE) A = second dest byte
B06F AND $03 mask low 2 bits ($03)
B071 OR (HL)
B072 LD (DE),A
B073 POP BC
B074 POP HL
B075 JP setup_tower_scroll_4
render_anim_texture_1 B078 JP C,render_anim_texture_2 test byte carry set -> $B08D
B07B LD HL,$B02B HL -> $B02B + phase (3rd branch base)
B07E ADD HL,BC
B07F LD A,(DE) A = current dest byte
B080 AND $F0 mask high nibble ($F0)
B082 OR (HL)
B083 LD (DE),A
B084 INC HL
B085 INC DE
B086 LD A,(HL)
B087 LD (DE),A
B088 POP BC
B089 POP HL
B08A JP setup_tower_scroll_4
render_anim_texture_2 B08D LD HL,$B02D HL -> $B02D + phase (4th branch base)
B090 ADD HL,BC
B091 LD A,(DE) A = current dest byte
B092 AND $FC mask high 6 bits ($FC)
B094 LD (DE),A
B095 INC DE
B096 LDI copy one animated byte via LDI
B098 LD A,(DE) A = second dest byte
B099 AND $3F mask low 6 bits ($3F)
B09B LD (DE),A
B09C POP BC
B09D POP HL
B09E JP setup_tower_scroll_4
B0A1: Unused
Self-mod scratch byte, not unused: draw_tower_cols patches this as the immediate operand of its column-count loop (see draw_tower_cols's own note) -- the live loop-count constant, reduced near the scroll wrap edge.
B0A1 DEFS $01
B0A2: Draw tower cols
Used by the routine at proc_table.
Render the visible tower column strip.
Patches the column count at B0A1 ($14, reduced near the wrap edge from scroll_pos) and dispatches on the $6021 sub-column phase.
SELF-MOD: B0A1 loop count.
Clobbers: A,B,HL (+ render regs).
draw_tower_cols B0A2 LD A,$14
B0A4 LD ($B0A1),A
B0A7 LD HL,($6030) get Vertical scroll position
B0AA LD A,H
B0AB CP $03
B0AD JR NZ,draw_tower_cols_0
B0AF LD A,L
B0B0 NEG
B0B2 DEC A
B0B3 RRA
B0B4 RRA
B0B5 AND $0F
B0B7 LD B,A
B0B8 LD A,$13
B0BA SUB B
B0BB LD ($B0A1),A
draw_tower_cols_0 B0BE LD A,($6021)
B0C1 OR A
B0C2 JR Z,draw_tower_cols_1
B0C4 CP $02
B0C6 JP Z,draw_tower_cols_4
B0C9 CP $04
B0CB JP Z,draw_tower_cols_7
B0CE JP draw_tower_cols_10
draw_tower_cols_1 B0D1 DI
B0D2 LD ($6000),SP set Temporary place for SP
B0D6 LD SP,$B22E

B0DF EXX

B0E3 LD C,A

B0E9 EXX
B0EA POP HL
B0EB EXX

B22E DEFB $67,$40,$87,$40,$A7,$40,$C7,$40

B336 DEFB $67,$56,$87,$50,$87,$56,$A7,$50
B33E DEFB $A7,$56,$C7,$50,$C7,$56,$E7,$50
B346: Load level col
Used by the routine at proc_table.
Fetch column descriptor 6039 from the column-major table at 7A00 (stride 8): low byte (+0) and high byte (+8) form a pointer (offset by 6A00 into level data), and the property byte (+16) is stashed in $603A.
Cell byte format for the map this fills: docs/Level-Format.md.
Returns IX=pointer.
Clobbers: A,BC,DE,HL,IX.
load_level_col B346 LD A,($6039)
B349 LD C,A
B34A ADD A,A
B34B ADD A,$6A
B34D LD D,A
B34E LD E,$00
B350 LD B,$00
B352 LD IX,$7A00
B356 ADD IX,BC
B358 LD L,(IX+$00)
B35B LD H,(IX+$08)
B35E LD BC,$6A00
B361 ADD HL,BC
B362 LD A,(IX+$10)
B365 LD ($603A),A
B368 PUSH HL
B369 POP IX
B36B LD C,A
B36C LD HL,$F000
load_level_col_0 B36F CALL blit_masked_column Blit masked column
B372 CALL blit_masked_column Blit masked column
B375 LD H,$F0
B377 INC L
B378 DEC C
B379 JR NZ,load_level_col_0
B37B LD HL,$F000
B37E LD ($6015),HL set Unknown word
B381 RET
B382: Blit masked column
Used by the routine at load_level_col.
Draw an 8-pixel-tall column at HL: for each set bit of the mask byte at (DE), store the next source byte (IX++) and step down one pixel row (INC H) skip rows whose mask bit is 0.
In: DE=mask ptr, IX=pixels, HL=screen.
Clobbers: A,B,HL,IX.
blit_masked_column B382 LD A,(DE)
B383 INC DE
B384 LD B,$08
blit_masked_column_0 B386 RRA
B387 JR NC,blit_masked_column_1
B389 PUSH AF
B38A LD A,(IX+$00)
B38D INC IX
B38F LD (HL),A
B390 POP AF
blit_masked_column_1 B391 INC H
B392 DJNZ blit_masked_column_0
B394 RET
B395: Alloc object slot
Used by the routine at proc_table.
Find a free entry (marker $FF at +0) in the 4-slot active-object table at $60A9.
On success store its type ($7D), source pointer (HL) and a copied data byte, set CY, RET.
In: HL=source.
Out: CY=allocated.
Clobbers: A,B,IX.
alloc_object_slot B395 LD IX,$60A9
B399 LD B,$04
alloc_object_slot_0 B39B LD A,(IX+$00)
B39E CP $FF
B3A0 JR NZ,alloc_object_slot_1
B3A2 LD A,(HL)
B3A3 AND $0C
B3A5 CP $04
B3A7 JR Z,alloc_object_slot_2
B3A9 LD A,(HL)
B3AA LD (IX+$0C),A
B3AD LD (IX+$04),L
B3B0 LD (IX+$08),H
B3B3 LD (IX+$00),$7D
B3B7 SCF
B3B8 RET
alloc_object_slot_1 B3B9 INC IX
B3BB DJNZ alloc_object_slot_0
alloc_object_slot_2 B3BD OR A
B3BE RET
B3BF: Update object slots
Used by the routine at proc_table.
Tick the 4-entry active-object table at $60A9 (counterpart to alloc_object_slot).
For each slot: $FF=free (skip), >1 = decrement its countdown, and on expiry write the stored byte back through its screen pointer (erase the object).
Clobbers: A,B,HL,IX.
update_object_slots B3BF LD IX,$60A9
B3C3 LD B,$04
update_object_slots_0 B3C5 LD A,(IX+$00)
B3C8 OR A
B3C9 JR Z,update_object_slots_1
B3CB CP $FF
B3CD JR Z,update_object_slots_3
B3CF DEC (IX+$00)
B3D2 JR update_object_slots_3
update_object_slots_1 B3D4 LD L,(IX+$04)
B3D7 LD H,(IX+$08)
B3DA LD A,(IX+$0C)
B3DD AND $0C
B3DF LD (HL),A
B3E0 DEC L
B3E1 DEC (IX+$04)
B3E4 LD A,(HL)
B3E5 LD (IX+$0C),A
B3E8 AND $0C
B3EA CP $04
B3EC JR Z,update_object_slots_2
B3EE LD (HL),$8D
B3F0 JP update_object_slots_3
update_object_slots_2 B3F3 LD (HL),$85
B3F5 LD (IX+$00),$FF
update_object_slots_3 B3F9 INC IX
B3FB DJNZ update_object_slots_0
B3FD RET
B3FE: Free object slot
Used by the routine at proc_table.
Mark the $60A9 slot whose stored pointer equals HL as free (set +0 = $FF).
In: HL=pointer.
Clobbers: A,B,IX.
free_object_slot B3FE LD IX,$60A9
B402 LD B,$04
free_object_slot_0 B404 LD A,(IX+$00)
B407 CP $FF
B409 JR Z,free_object_slot_1
B40B LD A,L
B40C CP (IX+$04)
B40F JR NZ,free_object_slot_1
B411 LD A,H
B412 CP (IX+$08)
B415 JR NZ,free_object_slot_1
B417 LD (IX+$00),$FF
B41B RET
free_object_slot_1 B41C INC IX
B41E DJNZ free_object_slot_0
B420 RET
B421: Update actors
Used by the routine at proc_table.
Per-frame actor pass (proc_table slot A76F).
Skips when player_state is 8/9 (level-end).
Derives the camera row 6036 from $6059, then walks the 4 object_table actors, acting on each live one (state field IX+48 != 0).
Clobbers: A,B,HL,IX.
update_actors B421 LD A,($60C0) skip if player_state 8 or 9 (level-end)
B424 CP $08
B426 RET Z
B427 CP $09
B429 RET Z
B42A LD HL,($6059) camera row = ($6059)>>2 (RR chain)
B42D LD A,L
B42E RR H
B430 RRA
B431 RR H
B433 RRA
B434 LD ($6036),A set Camera row
B437 LD IX,$605B find first active actor (state!=0) in object_table
B43B LD B,$04
update_actors_0 B43D LD A,(IX+$30)
B440 CP $00
B442 JR Z,update_actors_1
B444 INC IX
B446 DJNZ update_actors_0
B448 RET
update_actors_1 B449 LD HL,($6015) get Unknown word
B44C LD A,($603A) already aligned to camera row? -> spawn dispatch ($B512)
B44F CP L
B450 JP Z,update_actors_14
B453 LD A,($6036) get Camera row
B456 CP L
B457 JP Z,update_actors_14
B45A JP C,update_actors_14
update_actors_2 B45D LD A,(HL) test cell flags at HL: special/type check
B45E OR A
B45F JP M,update_actors_3
B462 AND $70
B464 JR NZ,update_actors_4
update_actors_3 B466 LD A,H step 6015 high nibble toward $F0..$FF ring, wrap
B467 SUB $F0
B469 INC A
B46A AND $0F
B46C ADD A,$F0
B46E LD H,A
B46F CP $F0
B471 JR NZ,update_actors_2
B473 INC L
B474 LD ($6015),HL set Unknown word
B477 RET
update_actors_4 B478 LD ($6015),HL set Unknown word
B47B LD A,(HL)
B47C PUSH AF
B47D PUSH HL
B47E LD A,H
B47F SUB $F0
B481 INC A
B482 AND $0F
B484 ADD A,$F0
B486 LD H,A
B487 CP $F0
B489 JR NZ,update_actors_5
B48B INC L
update_actors_5 B48C LD ($6015),HL set Unknown word
B48F POP HL
B490 POP AF
B491 AND $70 classify flag bits ($70 mask)
B493 CP $10
B495 JR Z,update_actors_12
B497 CP $60 bit pattern $60? branch
B499 JR NC,update_actors_10
B49B CP $40 bit pattern $40? branch
B49D JR NC,update_actors_8
B49F CP $20 bit $20 exact? set speed=1 else 0
B4A1 JR Z,update_actors_6
B4A3 XOR A
B4A4 JR update_actors_7
update_actors_6 B4A6 LD A,$01
update_actors_7 B4A8 LD (IX+$36),A speed/phase/pattern = 0/1/0 (idle-ish spawn)
B4AB LD (IX+$42),$01
B4AF LD (IX+$3C),$00
B4B3 JP update_actors_13
update_actors_8 B4B6 AND $10 speed=1or2, phase=8, pattern=0 (variant B)
B4B8 JR Z,update_actors_9
B4BA LD A,$01
update_actors_9 B4BC INC A
B4BD LD (IX+$36),A
B4C0 LD (IX+$42),$08
B4C4 LD (IX+$3C),$00
B4C8 JP update_actors_13
update_actors_10 B4CB AND $10 speed=1or2, phase=9, pattern=0 (variant C)
B4CD JR Z,update_actors_11
B4CF LD A,$01
update_actors_11 B4D1 INC A
B4D2 LD (IX+$36),A
B4D5 LD (IX+$42),$09
B4D9 LD (IX+$3C),$00
B4DD JP update_actors_13
update_actors_12 B4E0 LD A,$01 speed=1, phase=2, pattern=0 (variant D)
B4E2 LD (IX+$36),A
B4E5 LD (IX+$42),$02
B4E9 LD (IX+$3C),$00
update_actors_13 B4ED LD A,H compute angular pos from row (H-$F0)*8+4
B4EE SUB $F0
B4F0 ADD A,A
B4F1 ADD A,A
B4F2 ADD A,A
B4F3 ADD A,$04
B4F5 AND $7F
B4F7 LD (IX+$00),A
B4FA XOR A compute position (IX+6/+12) via rotate-left pack
B4FB RL L
B4FD RL A
B4FF RL L
B501 RL A
B503 LD (IX+$06),L
B506 LD (IX+$0C),A
B509 LD (IX+$30),$06 activate: state=6, phase=0
B50D LD (IX+$2A),$00
B511 RET
update_actors_14 B512 LD A,($60A3) $60A3 flag: pending single-shot spawn?
B515 CP $FF
B517 RET Z
B518 DEC A
B519 LD ($60A3),A
B51C CP $FF
B51E RET NZ
B51F LD (IX+$30),$07 activate as state 7 (wall marker), reset phase
B523 LD (IX+$2A),$00
B527 LD A,($60A4) get Player's current column
B52A NEG
B52C LD ($60A4),A set Player's current column
B52F LD (IX+$36),A
B532 ADD A,A derive angular pos 0/$78 from sign
B533 LD A,$00
B535 JR NC,update_actors_15
B537 LD A,$78
update_actors_15 B539 LD (IX+$00),A store position (IX+0/+6/+12) from player position
B53C LD HL,($60BA)
B53F LD (IX+$06),L
B542 LD (IX+$0C),H
B545 RET
B546: Clear screen
Used by the routine at proc_table.
Cold wipe before the title/play screen draws.
1) zero game RAM temp_sp..$61FF (512 bytes, 2 pages).
2) zero the entire display 4000..$5AFF (pixels+attrs) via LDIR.
3) CALL build_screen_hi_lut (build a lookup table in the reclaimed printer buffer 5B00) and build_row_addr_table.
Clobbers: A,BC,DE,HL.
clear_screen B546 LD HL,$6000 HL -> game RAM base temp_sp
B549 LD B,$02 B = 2 pages to clear
clear_screen_0 B54B LD (HL),$00 store 0
B54D INC L
B54E JR NZ,clear_screen_0 loop 256 bytes of this page
B550 INC H next page
B551 DJNZ clear_screen_0
B553 LD HL,$4000 HL -> screen base 4000
B556 LD DE,$4001 DE -> $4001 (LDIR src=HL, dst=DE, overlap fill)
B559 LD BC,$1AFF BC = $1AFF = 6911 bytes = whole display minus 1
B55C LD (HL),$00 seed first byte = 0
B55E LDIR LDIR: propagate 0 across 4000..$5AFF
B560 CALL build_screen_hi_lut Build screen hi LUT
B563 CALL build_row_addr_table Build row addr table
B566 RET
B567: Build screen hi LUT
Used by the routines at proc_table and clear_screen.
Fill a 256-byte table at 5B00 mapping a byte value to its bit-reordered form used in ZX screen-address math.
Processes the index in 2-bit groups groups 00 and 11 pass through, 01/10 get +$40 -- the Y2<->Y0 swap that makes the ZX pixel rows non-linear.
Result accumulates in H.
The porter can DELETE this and the 5B00 lookups, replac
build_screen_hi_lut B567 LD DE,$5B00 DE -> 5B00 LUT base (reclaimed printer buffer)
build_screen_hi_lut_0 B56A LD C,E C = current index (low byte of DE)
B56B LD B,$04 B = 4 groups of 2 bits
build_screen_hi_lut_1 B56D LD A,C
B56E AND $C0 isolate the top 2-bit group
B570 JR Z,build_screen_hi_lut_2 00 -> pass through
B572 CP $C0
B574 JR Z,build_screen_hi_lut_2 11 -> pass through
B576 ADD A,$40 01/10 -> +$40 (the scan-order bit swap)
build_screen_hi_lut_2 B578 LD L,A
B579 ADD HL,HL HL <<= 2 (accumulate this group)
B57A ADD HL,HL
B57B SLA C shift C left 2 to expose next group (Z80 SLA)
B57D SLA C
B57F DJNZ build_screen_hi_lut_1
B581 LD A,H
B582 LD (DE),A store H as LUT[index]
B583 INC E
B584 JR NZ,build_screen_hi_lut_0 loop all 256 entries (E wraps to 0)
B586 RET
B587: Routine at B587
Used by the routine at proc_table.
clear_playfield B587 LD C,$20
clear_playfield_0 B589 LD A,C compute screen-addr high byte from row index
B58A RRA
B58B RRA
B58C RRA
B58D AND $18
B58F OR $40
B591 LD H,A
B592 LD A,C
B593 AND $07
B595 ADD A,H
B596 LD H,A
B597 LD A,C
B598 RLA
B599 RLA
B59A AND $E0
B59C LD L,A
B59D LD B,$20
clear_playfield_1 B59F LD (HL),$00 zero 32 pixel bytes of this row
B5A1 INC HL
B5A2 DJNZ clear_playfield_1
B5A4 INC C
B5A5 LD A,C
B5A6 CP $B8 stop at row $B8
B5A8 JR NZ,clear_playfield_0
B5AA LD HL,$5860
B5AD LD DE,$5861
B5B0 LD BC,$0020
B5B3 LD (HL),$00
B5B5 LDIR LDIR: clear an attribute strip
B5B7 LD BC,$025F
B5BA CALL lookup_col_attr
B5BD LD (HL),A
B5BE LDIR
B5C0 LD BC,$0020
B5C3 LD (HL),$00
B5C5 LDIR
B5C7 RET
B5C8: Routine at B5C8
Used by the routine at proc_table.
hook_noop B5C8 RET
B5C9 NOP
B5CA NOP
flag_b5cb B5CB DEFB $00
B5CC DEFB $00,$00
B5CE DEFB $00
B5CF: Routine at B5CF
Used by the routine at proc_table.
init_b5cf B5CF LD A,$FF
B5D1 LD ($6038),A set Flag?? $00 $FE $FF
B5D4 XOR A
B5D5 LD ($B5CB),A zero flag_b5cb at flag_b5cb (write into code padding)
B5D8 RET
B5D9: Get screen addr
Used by the routines at proc_table, blit_scroll_wrap, select_player_sprite, draw_actor_sprite, draw_pending_sprite, setup_strip_draw and draw_tower_strip.
Pixel address for (row=H, col-in-row=L).
Indexes scr_row_addr_tbl (scr_row_addr_tbl, 2 bytes/row) at 2*H to fetch the row base, then adds L to get the final byte address in HL.
In: H=row 0..191, L=byte offset within row.
Out: HL=screen addr.
Clobbers A preserves DE.
get_screen_addr B5D9 PUSH DE
B5DA LD D,L
B5DB LD A,H
B5DC ADD A,A L = 2*H = table index
B5DD LD L,A
B5DE LD A,$5D H = $5D + carry -> HL -> scr_row_addr_tbl[H]
B5E0 ADC A,$00
B5E2 LD H,A
B5E3 LD A,D
B5E4 ADD A,(HL) A = row base low + col offset
B5E5 INC L
B5E6 LD H,(HL) H = row base high
B5E7 LD L,A
B5E8 POP DE
B5E9 RET
B5EA: Build row addr table
Used by the routine at clear_screen.
Precompute 192 screen-row addresses into scr_row_addr_tbl (scr_row_addr_tbl, little-endian words).
For each row y in 0..191 it builds the ZX pixel address:
high = $40 | (y&7) | ((y>>3)&$18)
low = (y&$38)<<2 i.e.
the famous ZX scan-order layout (Y bits split across the address).
get_screen_addr then just indexes this table.
build_row_addr_table B5EA LD DE,$5D00 DE -> scr_row_addr_tbl table base
B5ED LD HL,$0000 HL: H = row counter y, builds address in HL
build_row_addr_table_0 B5F0 PUSH HL
B5F1 PUSH DE
B5F2 LD A,H (y>>3)&$18 -> D (the y bits 3..5 region)
B5F3 RRCA
B5F4 RRCA
B5F5 RRCA
B5F6 AND $18
B5F8 LD D,A
B5F9 LD A,H
B5FA AND $07
B5FC ADD A,D + (y&7) + $40 -> high byte
B5FD ADD A,$40
B5FF LD D,A
B600 LD A,H
B601 RLC A (y<<2)&$E0 -> low byte (E)
B603 RLC A
B605 AND $E0
B607 LD E,A
B608 LD H,$00
B60A ADD HL,DE HL = full row-0 screen address
B60B POP DE
B60C LD A,L
B60D LD (DE),A store LE word into table[y]
B60E INC DE
B60F LD A,H
B610 LD (DE),A
B611 INC DE
B612 POP HL
B613 INC H
B614 LD A,H
B615 CP $C0 stop after row $BF (192 rows)
B617 JP NZ,build_row_addr_table_0
B61A RET
B61B: Get sprite def
Used by the routines at proc_table, select_player_sprite and draw_actor_sprite.
Look up sprite/graphic descriptor A in the gfx_c100 table (A*2): unpack the frame count (top 2 bits, 0->4) into C and the bitmap pointer (low 6 bits + $C1xx base) into HL.
In: A=sprite id.
Out: HL=bitmap, C=frames.
Clobbers: A,HL.
get_sprite_def B61B ADD A,A Sprite ID to word index
B61C LD L,A
B61D LD H,$00
B61F LD DE,$C101 Point to sprite offsets table (gfx_c100)
B622 ADD HL,DE
B623 LD A,(HL) Descriptor: top 2 bits = frame count, low 6 bits = offset
B624 RLCA
B625 RLCA
B626 AND $03 Extract frame count into C (0-3)
B628 LD C,A
B629 JR NZ,get_sprite_def_0
B62B LD C,$04 Default to 4 frames if count is zero
get_sprite_def_0 B62D LD A,(HL)
B62E AND $3F Keep low 6 bits
B630 ADD A,$C1 Compute bitmap address high byte: $C1 + offset
B632 DEC HL
B633 LD L,(HL) Low byte from preceding word entry
B634 LD H,A
B635 RET
B636: Select player sprite
Used by the routine at proc_table.
Pick the player's current sprite frame from $60C1 plus facing ($60BE bit7) and player_state, applying frame-base offsets ($1F walking-left / $44 walking-right).
RET early if $60C2==0 (nothing to draw).
Clobbers: A,H,L.
select_player_sprite B636 LD A,($60C2)
B639 OR A
B63A RET Z
B63B LD A,($60C0) get Player state-machine state
B63E CP $08
B640 LD A,($60C1)
B643 JR NZ,select_player_sprite_1
B645 LD HL,$60BE
B648 BIT 7,(HL)
B64A JR Z,select_player_sprite_0
B64C ADD A,$1F
B64E JR select_player_sprite_3
select_player_sprite_0 B650 ADD A,$44
B652 JR select_player_sprite_3
select_player_sprite_1 B654 LD H,A
B655 AND $7F
B657 BIT 7,H
B659 JR NZ,select_player_sprite_2
B65B ADD A,$25
B65D JR select_player_sprite_3
select_player_sprite_2 B65F ADD A,$00
select_player_sprite_3 B661 CALL get_sprite_def Get sprite def
B664 PUSH HL
B665 PUSH BC
B666 LD A,($60BA)
B669 LD HL,$6030
B66C SUB (HL)
B66D ADD A,A
B66E LD C,A
B66F LD A,$A4
B671 SUB C
B672 LD H,A
B673 LD L,$0E
B675 LD ($B5C9),HL
B678 CALL get_screen_addr Get screen addr
B67B EX DE,HL
B67C POP BC
B67D POP HL
B67E LD ($6000),SP set Temporary place for SP
B682 DI
B683 LD SP,HL
B684 EX DE,HL
B685 LD B,$0A
B687 BIT 2,C
B689 JP NZ,select_player_sprite_6
B68C INC L
select_player_sprite_4 B68D POP DE
B68E LD A,(HL)
B68F AND E
B690 OR D
B691 LD (HL),A
B692 INC L
B693 POP DE
B694 LD A,(HL)
B695 AND E
B696 OR D
B697 LD (HL),A
B698 DEC L
B699 INC H
B69A POP DE
B69B LD A,(HL)
B69C AND E
B69D OR D
B69E LD (HL),A
B69F INC L
B6A0 POP DE
B6A1 LD A,(HL)
B6A2 AND E
B6A3 OR D
B6A4 LD (HL),A
B6A5 DEC L
B6A6 INC H
B6A7 LD A,H
B6A8 AND $07
B6AA JP NZ,select_player_sprite_5
B6AD LD A,L
B6AE ADD A,$20
B6B0 LD L,A
B6B1 JR C,select_player_sprite_5
B6B3 LD A,H
B6B4 SUB $08
B6B6 LD H,A
select_player_sprite_5 B6B7 DJNZ select_player_sprite_4
B6B9 JP select_player_sprite_8
select_player_sprite_6 B6BC POP DE
B6BD LD C,L
B6BE LD A,(HL)
B6BF AND E
B6C0 LD (HL),A
B6C1 INC L
B6C2 LD A,(HL)
B6C3 AND D
B6C4 POP DE
B6C5 OR E
B6C6 LD (HL),A
B6C7 INC L
B6C8 LD A,(HL)
B6C9 AND D
B6CA POP DE
B6CB OR E
B6CC LD (HL),A
B6CD INC L
B6CE LD A,(HL)
B6CF AND D
B6D0 LD (HL),A
B6D1 LD L,C
B6D2 INC H
B6D3 POP DE
B6D4 LD A,(HL)
B6D5 AND E
B6D6 LD (HL),A
B6D7 INC L
B6D8 LD A,(HL)
B6D9 AND D
B6DA POP DE
B6DB OR E
B6DC LD (HL),A
B6DD INC L
B6DE LD A,(HL)
B6DF AND D
B6E0 POP DE
B6E1 OR E
B6E2 LD (HL),A
B6E3 INC L
B6E4 LD A,(HL)
B6E5 AND D
B6E6 LD (HL),A
B6E7 LD L,C
B6E8 INC H
B6E9 LD A,H
B6EA AND $07
B6EC JP NZ,select_player_sprite_7
B6EF LD A,L
B6F0 ADD A,$20
B6F2 LD L,A
B6F3 JR C,select_player_sprite_7
B6F5 LD A,H
B6F6 SUB $08
B6F8 LD H,A
select_player_sprite_7 B6F9 DJNZ select_player_sprite_6
select_player_sprite_8 B6FB LD SP,($6000) get Temporary place for SP
B6FF EI
B700 LD A,($6038) get Flag?? $00 $FE $FF
B703 CP $FF
B705 RET Z
B706 LD HL,($B5C9)
B709 LD A,H
B70A ADD A,$14
B70C LD H,A
B70D CALL get_screen_addr Get screen addr
B710 LD DE,$FC3F
B713 LD B,$00
B715 CALL plot_masked_row Plot masked row
B718 DEC B
B719 CALL plot_masked_row Plot masked row
B71C CALL plot_row_wrapped Plot row wrapped
B71F CALL plot_masked_row Plot masked row
B722 CALL plot_row_wrapped Plot row wrapped
B725 CALL plot_masked_row Plot masked row
B728 CALL plot_row_wrapped Plot row wrapped
B72B CALL plot_masked_row Plot masked row
B72E INC B
B72F: Plot row wrapped
Used by the routine at select_player_sprite.
Advance the screen address HL down one pixel row (ZX scan-order arithmetic: char-cell wrap via +$20 / -$08) then fall into plot_masked_row.
Clobbers: A,HL (+ plot regs).
plot_row_wrapped B72F LD A,H
B730 AND $07
B732 JR NZ,plot_masked_row
B734 LD A,L
B735 ADD A,$20
B737 LD L,A
B738 JR C,plot_masked_row
B73A LD A,H
B73B SUB $08
B73D LD H,A
B73E: Plot masked row
Used by the routines at select_player_sprite and plot_row_wrapped.
Plot a 3-cell sprite slice at HL: AND the left edge cell with mask D, write the two middle cells = B, AND the right edge with mask E restores L and bumps H.
In: HL,B,D,E.
Clobbers: A,C,HL.
plot_masked_row B73E LD C,L
B73F LD A,(HL)
B740 AND D
B741 LD (HL),A
B742 INC L
B743 LD (HL),B
B744 INC L
B745 LD (HL),B
B746 INC L
B747 LD A,(HL)
B748 AND E
B749 LD (HL),A
B74A LD L,C
B74B INC H
B74C RET
B74D: Draw actor sprite
Used by the routine at proc_table.
Render one object_table actor (IX = entry).
Skips if the sprite id (IX+24)==$FF (inactive).
Computes screen-Y via actor_screen_y (also leaves an X-related byte in $60A6). State 7 (IX+48, the "static wall marker" convention used by collision_test) doubles $60A6 into a fresh angular-position copy rather than drawing normally.
Bounds-checks the screen row ($60A8) to [$18,$B0) (RET if off-screen), derives a sub-column fraction from $60A6, and for state-7 entries queues a one-shot deferred sprite via flag_b5cb/B5CC/B5CE (consumed later by draw_pending_sprite) instead of blitting immediately.
Otherwise computes the screen address (GET_SCREEN_ADDR), then picks the bitmap: sprite id >=$7C indexes the runtime table at $BC80 directly (fixed 3-cell-wide, 8-row blit) sprite id <$7C goes through GET_SPRITE_DEF (adds a facing offset derived from $60A6) to fetch the normal gfx_c100 sprite definition. Falls through into blit_sprite_fast to draw.
Clobbers: A,HL,IX,...
draw_actor_sprite B74D LD A,(IX+$18)
B750 CP $FF sprite id == $FF -> RET (inactive)
B752 RET Z
B753 CALL actor_screen_y Actor screen y
B756 LD A,(IX+$30) state 7 (wall marker)?
B759 CP $07
B75B JR NZ,draw_actor_sprite_0
B75D LD A,(IX+$00) double angular pos -> $60A6
B760 ADD A,A
B761 LD ($60A6),A
draw_actor_sprite_0 B764 LD A,($60A8) A = screen row ($60A8)
B767 CP $18 bounds check row to [$18,$B0), else RET
B769 RET C
B76A CP $B0
B76C RET NC
B76D LD H,A
B76E LD A,($60A6) sub-column fraction: ($60A6)>>3 &$1F -> L
B771 RRA
B772 RRA
B773 RRA
B774 AND $1F
B776 LD L,A
B777 LD A,(IX+$30) state 7 again -> defer draw
B77A CP $07
B77C JR NZ,draw_actor_sprite_1
B77E LD ($B5CC),HL queue deferred sprite: pos -> B5CC
B781 LD A,($60A6) facing quadrant -> B5CE
B784 SRL A
B786 AND $03
B788 LD ($B5CE),A
B78B LD A,$01 flag_b5cb = 1 (pending)
B78D LD ($B5CB),A
draw_actor_sprite_1 B790 CALL get_screen_addr Get screen addr
B793 PUSH HL
B794 LD A,(IX+$18) sprite id >= $7C? (fixed runtime-table graphic)
B797 CP $7C
B799 JR C,draw_actor_sprite_3
B79B SUB $7C offset*24 into $BC80 table
B79D ADD A,A
B79E ADD A,A
B79F ADD A,A
B7A0 ADD A,A
B7A1 ADD A,A
B7A2 LD H,$00
B7A4 LD L,A
B7A5 ADD A,A
B7A6 ADD A,L
B7A7 LD L,A
B7A8 JR NC,draw_actor_sprite_2
B7AA INC H
draw_actor_sprite_2 B7AB LD DE,$BC80 HL -> $BC80 + offset
B7AE ADD HL,DE
B7AF LD C,$03 C=3,B=8: fixed 3-cell/8-row blit
B7B1 LD B,$08
B7B3 JR blit_sprite_fast_0
draw_actor_sprite_3 B7B5 LD C,A else: C = raw sprite id
B7B6 LD A,($60A6) facing quadrant + sprite id
B7B9 SRL A
B7BB AND $03
B7BD ADD A,C
B7BE PUSH AF stash variant byte for blit_sprite_fast
B7BF CALL get_sprite_def Get sprite def
B7C2: Blit sprite fast
Fast masked sprite blit using the LD SP,screen / PUSH stack-write trick (saves the real SP to temp_sp, DI, treats the screen as a "stack" so each POP+merge writes one masked byte pair).
Entered by fallthrough from draw_actor_sprite's GET_SPRITE_DEF/RET (POP AF recovers the sprite-variant byte pushed before that call): height B is 8 rows normally, or 4 rows when that byte is >=$4E (used for the fixed $BC80 runtime-table sprites, which pre-set C=3/B=8 explicitly and skip this test).
C selects the row width variant: 1-cell-wide ($B7DD, 2 mask/data pairs per row), 2-cell-wide ($B7FD, 4 pairs), or 3-cell-wide ($B82B, 6 pairs) each variant does the same AND/OR merge-and-advance idiom (INC H each row, wrapping L by +$20/-8 on the char-cell boundary every 8 rows -- the standard ZX scan-order step), looping B times via DJNZ.
Data is consumed via POP off the (redirected) stack: each row pops a mask/value pair per cell, ANDs the mask into the destination screen byte, ORs in the value.
Restores the real SP from temp_sp and EI before returning.
Clobbers: A,BC,DE,HL.
blit_sprite_fast B7C2 POP AF recover sprite-variant byte pushed before GET_SPRITE_DEF
B7C3 LD B,$08 height = 4 rows if variant >= $4E, else 8
B7C5 CP $4E
B7C7 JR NC,blit_sprite_fast_0
B7C9 LD B,$04
This entry point is used by the routine at draw_actor_sprite.
blit_sprite_fast_0 B7CB POP DE DE = mask/value source pointer
B7CC LD ($6000),SP set Temporary place for SP
B7D0 DI
B7D1 LD SP,HL SP=HL (write-via-POP trick); HL=mask/value ptr
B7D2 EX DE,HL
B7D3 DEC C C selects row-width variant: 1/2/3-cell
B7D4 JR Z,blit_sprite_fast_1
B7D6 DEC C
B7D7 JP Z,blit_sprite_fast_3
B7DA JP blit_sprite_fast_5
blit_sprite_fast_1 B7DD POP DE --- 1-cell-wide variant ---
B7DE LD A,(HL)
B7DF AND E
B7E0 OR D
B7E1 LD (HL),A
B7E2 INC H
B7E3 POP DE
B7E4 LD A,(HL)
B7E5 AND E
B7E6 OR D
B7E7 LD (HL),A
B7E8 INC H
B7E9 LD A,H wrap to next screen row (char-cell boundary step)
B7EA AND $07
B7EC JR NZ,blit_sprite_fast_2
B7EE LD A,L
B7EF ADD A,$20
B7F1 LD L,A
B7F2 JR C,blit_sprite_fast_2
B7F4 LD A,H
B7F5 SUB $08
B7F7 LD H,A
blit_sprite_fast_2 B7F8 DJNZ blit_sprite_fast_1 loop B rows
B7FA JP blit_sprite_fast_7 done -> restore SP
blit_sprite_fast_3 B7FD POP DE --- 2-cell-wide variant ---
B7FE LD A,(HL)
B7FF AND E
B800 OR D
B801 LD (HL),A
B802 INC L
B803 POP DE
B804 LD A,(HL)
B805 AND E
B806 OR D
B807 LD (HL),A
B808 DEC L
B809 INC H
B80A POP DE
B80B LD A,(HL)
B80C AND E
B80D OR D
B80E LD (HL),A
B80F INC L
B810 POP DE
B811 LD A,(HL)
B812 AND E
B813 OR D
B814 LD (HL),A
B815 DEC L
B816 INC H
B817 LD A,H wrap to next screen row
B818 AND $07
B81A JR NZ,blit_sprite_fast_4
B81C LD A,L
B81D ADD A,$20
B81F LD L,A
B820 JR C,blit_sprite_fast_4
B822 LD A,H
B823 SUB $08
B825 LD H,A
blit_sprite_fast_4 B826 DJNZ blit_sprite_fast_3 loop B rows
B828 JP blit_sprite_fast_7
blit_sprite_fast_5 B82B POP DE --- 3-cell-wide variant ---
B82C LD C,L
B82D LD A,(HL)
B82E AND E
B82F OR D
B830 LD (HL),A
B831 INC L
B832 POP DE
B833 LD A,(HL)
B834 AND E
B835 OR D
B836 LD (HL),A
B837 INC L
B838 POP DE
B839 LD A,(HL)
B83A AND E
B83B OR D
B83C LD (HL),A
B83D LD L,C
B83E INC H
B83F POP DE
B840 LD A,(HL)
B841 AND E
B842 OR D
B843 LD (HL),A
B844 INC L
B845 POP DE
B846 LD A,(HL)
B847 AND E
B848 OR D
B849 LD (HL),A
B84A INC L
B84B POP DE
B84C LD A,(HL)
B84D AND E
B84E OR D
B84F LD (HL),A
B850 LD L,C
B851 INC H
B852 LD A,H wrap to next screen row
B853 AND $07
B855 JR NZ,blit_sprite_fast_6
B857 LD A,L
B858 ADD A,$20
B85A LD L,A
B85B JR C,blit_sprite_fast_6
B85D LD A,H
B85E SUB $08
B860 LD H,A
blit_sprite_fast_6 B861 DJNZ blit_sprite_fast_5 loop B rows
blit_sprite_fast_7 B863 LD SP,($6000) get Temporary place for SP
B867 EI EI; RET
B868 RET
B869: Actor screen y
Used by the routines at proc_table and draw_actor_sprite.
Object-table counterpart of col_screen_y: map an actor's angular column (IX+0)+$20, minus tower_height rotation, wrapped (AND $7F), through col_y_table to a screen row also derives the object index ((IX low)-$5B).
Clobbers: A,DE,HL.
actor_screen_y B869 LD A,(IX+$00)
B86C ADD A,$20
B86E LD HL,$60B9
B871 SUB (HL)
B872 AND $7F
B874 LD E,A
B875 LD D,$00
B877 LD HL,$6380
B87A ADD HL,DE
B87B PUSH IX
B87D POP DE
B87E LD A,E
B87F SUB $5B
B881 LD E,A
B882 CP $04
B884 LD A,(HL)
B885 JR NZ,actor_screen_y_0
B887 ADD A,$02
actor_screen_y_0 B889 ADD A,A
B88A ADD A,$78
B88C LD ($60A6),A
B88F LD A,(IX+$06)
B892 LD HL,$6030
B895 SUB (HL)
B896 ADD A,A
B897 LD C,A
B898 LD A,$A8
B89A SUB C
B89B LD C,A
B89C LD A,E
B89D CP $04
B89F LD A,C
B8A0 JR NZ,actor_screen_y_1
B8A2 ADD A,$08
actor_screen_y_1 B8A4 LD ($60A8),A
B8A7 RET
B8A8: Draw pending sprite
Used by the routine at proc_table.
If flag_b5cb is set, consume it and draw the one-shot sprite queued at B5CC (pos) / B5CE (id), with on-screen bounds checks.
RET immediately when nothing queued.
Clobbers: A,BC,HL.
draw_pending_sprite B8A8 LD A,($B5CB) flag_b5cb set? consume it, else RET
B8AB OR A
B8AC RET Z
B8AD XOR A
B8AE LD ($B5CB),A
B8B1 LD HL,($B5CC) HL = queued pos, B = queued sprite id
B8B4 LD A,($B5CE)
B8B7 LD B,A
B8B8 LD A,L bounds check: column edge cases (row 7..$17 skip)
B8B9 CP $07
B8BB JR C,draw_pending_sprite_0
B8BD CP $17
B8BF RET C
draw_pending_sprite_0 B8C0 CP $1E bounds check: row < $1E
B8C2 RET NC
B8C3 LD C,$02 C=2 (default cell count); row<5 -> $B8DE
B8C5 CP $05
B8C7 JR C,draw_pending_sprite_2
B8C9 JR Z,draw_pending_sprite_3 row==6 or edge cases -> adjust C (1 or 3 cells)
B8CB DEC C
B8CC CP $06
B8CE JR Z,draw_pending_sprite_3
B8D0 CP $17
B8D2 JR NZ,draw_pending_sprite_1
B8D4 LD A,B
B8D5 OR A
B8D6 RET Z
draw_pending_sprite_1 B8D7 INC C adjust cell count near right/bottom edge
B8D8 CP $19
B8DA JR NC,draw_pending_sprite_2
B8DC INC L
B8DD DEC C
draw_pending_sprite_2 B8DE LD A,B sprite id (B)!=0? bump C for extra cell
B8DF OR A
B8E0 JR Z,draw_pending_sprite_3
B8E2 INC C
draw_pending_sprite_3 B8E3 CALL get_screen_addr Get screen addr
B8E6 LD B,$08 B=8 rows, D=0 (erase byte)
B8E8 LD D,$00
B8EA DEC C C selects erase width: 1/2/3-cell
B8EB JR Z,draw_pending_sprite_4
B8ED DEC C
B8EE JR Z,draw_pending_sprite_6
B8F0 JP draw_pending_sprite_8
draw_pending_sprite_4 B8F3 LD (HL),D --- 1-cell erase: write+wrap to next row ---
B8F4 INC H
B8F5 LD (HL),D
B8F6 INC H
B8F7 LD A,H
B8F8 AND $07
B8FA JR NZ,draw_pending_sprite_5
B8FC LD A,L
B8FD ADD A,$20
B8FF LD L,A
B900 JR C,draw_pending_sprite_5
B902 LD A,H
B903 SUB $08
B905 LD H,A
draw_pending_sprite_5 B906 DJNZ draw_pending_sprite_4
B908 RET
draw_pending_sprite_6 B909 LD (HL),D --- 2-cell erase: write+wrap to next row ---
B90A INC L
B90B LD (HL),D
B90C INC H
B90D LD (HL),D
B90E DEC L
B90F LD (HL),D
B910 INC H
B911 LD A,H
B912 AND $07
B914 JR NZ,draw_pending_sprite_7
B916 LD A,L
B917 ADD A,$20
B919 LD L,A
B91A JR C,draw_pending_sprite_7
B91C LD A,H
B91D SUB $08
B91F LD H,A
draw_pending_sprite_7 B920 DJNZ draw_pending_sprite_6
B922 RET
draw_pending_sprite_8 B923 LD (HL),D --- 3-cell erase: write+wrap to next row ---
B924 INC L
B925 LD (HL),D
B926 INC L
B927 LD (HL),D
B928 INC H
B929 LD (HL),D
B92A DEC L
B92B LD (HL),D
B92C DEC L
B92D LD (HL),D
B92E INC H
B92F LD A,H
B930 AND $07
B932 JR NZ,draw_pending_sprite_9
B934 LD A,L
B935 ADD A,$20
B937 LD L,A
B938 JR C,draw_pending_sprite_9
B93A LD A,H
B93B SUB $08
B93D LD H,A
draw_pending_sprite_9 B93E DJNZ draw_pending_sprite_8
B940 RET

strip_params B941 DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$0F,$17,$2F
B949 DEFB $09
