B965: Setup strip draw
Used by the routine at proc_table.
Patch the tower column-strip blitter's params (strip_params/$B9A1 row, $B942 column base) from disp_height and scroll_pos (bit2 selects the $24/$2C column phase).
SELF-MOD.
Clobbers: A,B,C.
setup_strip_draw B965 LD B,A
B966 LD A,($6032) get Display height
B969 LD ($B941),A
B96C LD ($B9A1),A
B96F LD C,$24
B971 LD A,($6030) get Vertical scroll position
B974 BIT 2,A
B976 JR Z,setup_strip_draw_0
B978 LD C,$2C
B97A DEC B
setup_strip_draw_0 B97B AND $03
B97D ADD A,A
B97E ADD A,C
B97F LD C,A
B980 LD ($B942),A
B983 LD A,($6030) get Vertical scroll position
B986 RRA
B987 RRA
B988 RRA
B989 XOR $0F
B98B AND $0F
B98D LD E,A
B98E LD D,$00
B990 LD HL,$B945
B993 ADD HL,DE
B994 LD ($B944),HL
B997 EX DE,HL
B998 LD H,C
B999 LD A,B
B99A EXX
B99B LD B,A
B99C EXX
B99D LD B,$0A
setup_strip_draw_1 B99F LD A,(DE)
B9A0 SUB $00
B9A2 AND $3F
B9A4 CP $1C
B9A6 JR NC,setup_strip_draw_4
B9A8 EXX
B9A9 BIT 7,B
B9AB EXX
B9AC JR Z,setup_strip_draw_2
B9AE CP $08
B9B0 JR C,setup_strip_draw_2
B9B2 CP $14
B9B4 JR C,setup_strip_draw_4
setup_strip_draw_2 B9B6 ADD A,$02
B9B8 LD L,A
B9B9 LD C,H
B9BA CALL get_screen_addr Get screen addr
B9BD LD A,(HL)
B9BE OR A
B9BF JR NZ,setup_strip_draw_3
B9C1 LD (HL),$18
B9C3 INC H
B9C4 LD (HL),$18
setup_strip_draw_3 B9C6 LD H,C
setup_strip_draw_4 B9C7 INC DE
B9C8 LD A,H
B9C9 ADD A,$10
B9CB LD H,A
B9CC CP $B8
B9CE RET NC
B9CF EXX
B9D0 DEC B
B9D1 DEC B
B9D2 EXX
B9D3 DJNZ setup_strip_draw_1
B9D5 RET
B9D6: Draw tower strip
Used by the routine at proc_table.
Blit one vertical column strip of up to 10 tower elements using the params patched by setup_strip_draw resolves each cell's screen address via get_screen_addr.
Clobbers: A,B,DE,HL.
draw_tower_strip B9D6 LD DE,($B944)
B9DA LD HL,($B941)
B9DD LD B,$0A
B9DF LD A,($B941)
B9E2 LD ($B9E7),A
draw_tower_strip_0 B9E5 LD A,(DE)
B9E6 SUB $00
B9E8 AND $3F
B9EA CP $1C
B9EC JR NC,draw_tower_strip_2
B9EE ADD A,$02
B9F0 LD L,A
B9F1 LD C,H
B9F2 CALL get_screen_addr Get screen addr
B9F5 LD A,$18
B9F7 CP (HL)
B9F8 JR NZ,draw_tower_strip_1
B9FA INC H
B9FB CP (HL)
B9FC JR NZ,draw_tower_strip_1
B9FE XOR A
B9FF LD (HL),A
BA00 DEC H
BA01 LD (HL),A
draw_tower_strip_1 BA02 LD H,C
draw_tower_strip_2 BA03 INC DE
BA04 LD A,H
BA05 ADD A,$10
BA07 LD H,A
BA08 CP $B8
BA0A RET NC
BA0B DJNZ draw_tower_strip_0
BA0D RET
BA0E DEFM "GFEDGFED"
BA16: Routine at BA16
Used by the routines at clear_playfield, paint_tower_attrs and scroll_tower_attrs.
lookup_col_attr BA16 PUSH DE
BA17 PUSH HL
BA18 LD A,($6039) get Level number 0
BA1B LD L,A
BA1C LD H,$00
BA1E LD DE,$BA0E
BA21 ADD HL,DE
BA22 LD A,(HL)
BA23 POP HL
BA24 POP DE
BA25 RET
BA26: Paint tower attrs
Used by the routine at proc_table.
Repaint the tower's attribute (colour) band in the $58xx attribute file from $5860, using lookup_col_attr (lookup_col_attr) for per-column colours and fill_run ($BA61) to lay rows.
SELF-MOD: BA66 row count.
Clobbers: A,BC,D,HL.
paint_tower_attrs BA26 LD A,$0E
BA28 LD ($BA66),A
BA2B LD HL,$5860
BA2E LD B,$20
BA30 XOR A
BA31 LD D,A
BA32 CALL fill_run
BA35 LD C,$0B
paint_tower_attrs_0 BA37 LD (HL),D
BA38 INC HL
BA39 LD (HL),D
BA3A INC HL
BA3B LD B,$1C
BA3D CALL lookup_col_attr
BA40 CALL fill_run
BA43 LD (HL),D
BA44 INC HL
BA45 LD (HL),D
BA46 INC HL
BA47 DEC C
BA48 JR NZ,paint_tower_attrs_0
BA4A LD C,$08
paint_tower_attrs_1 BA4C LD (HL),D
BA4D INC HL
BA4E LD (HL),D
BA4F INC HL
BA50 LD B,$1C
BA52 LD A,$41
BA54 CALL fill_run
BA57 LD (HL),D
BA58 INC HL
BA59 LD (HL),D
BA5A INC HL
BA5B DEC C
BA5C JR NZ,paint_tower_attrs_1
BA5E LD B,$20
BA60 XOR A
fill_run BA61 LD (HL),A
BA62 INC HL
BA63 DJNZ fill_run
BA65 RET
BA66: Unused
BA66 DEFS $01
BA67: Scroll tower attrs
Used by the routine at proc_table.
Adjust the painted attribute band as scroll_pos advances (compares against the BA66 latch set by paint_tower_attrs) so colours track the scrolling tower.
Clobbers: A,B,HL.
scroll_tower_attrs BA67 LD HL,($6030) HL = scroll_pos
BA6A LD A,H top-of-tower page (H==3)?
BA6B CP $03
BA6D LD A,$16 A = $16 (default row index)
BA6F JR NZ,scroll_tower_attrs_0
BA71 LD A,L A = scroll_pos low byte
BA72 NEG negate (count down from top)
BA74 RRA /4, mask to row range
BA75 RRA
BA76 AND $0F
BA78 SUB $16 bias by -$16
BA7A NEG negate back
scroll_tower_attrs_0 BA7C LD B,A B = target row index
BA7D LD A,($BA66) A = previous row index (BA66 latch)
BA80 SUB B delta = new - previous
BA81 JR Z,scroll_tower_attrs_1 unchanged? -> just update latch, RET
BA83 CP $FF delta == -1 (scrolled up one row)?
BA85 JR Z,scroll_tower_attrs_2
BA87 CP $01 delta == +1 (scrolled down one row)?
BA89 JR NZ,scroll_tower_attrs_1
BA8B LD A,B store new row index in latch
BA8C LD ($BA66),A
BA8F INC B B++ (paint the row below)
BA90 LD C,$41 C = $41 (default attribute)
BA92 JR scroll_tower_attrs_3
scroll_tower_attrs_1 BA94 LD A,B
BA95 LD ($BA66),A
BA98 RET
scroll_tower_attrs_2 BA99 LD A,B store new row index in latch
BA9A LD ($BA66),A
BA9D CALL lookup_col_attr recompute the attribute for this column via lookup_col_attr
BAA0 LD C,A
scroll_tower_attrs_3 BAA1 LD L,B HL = B * 32 (attribute row address)
BAA2 LD H,$00
BAA4 ADD HL,HL
BAA5 ADD HL,HL
BAA6 ADD HL,HL
BAA7 ADD HL,HL
BAA8 ADD HL,HL
BAA9 LD DE,$5802 DE = $5802 (attribute area base + offset)
BAAC ADD HL,DE
BAAD LD B,$1C B = $1C (28 columns)
scroll_tower_attrs_4 BAAF LD (HL),C fill one attribute row with C, loop
BAB0 INC HL
BAB1 DJNZ scroll_tower_attrs_4
BAB3 RET
BAB4: Unused
BAB4 DEFS $06
BABA: Routine at BABA
im2_dispatch BABA JP irq_handler

BC00: Runtime tables
runtime_tables..$BFFF, zero in the snapshot.
Game-state tables populated after init (enemy slots, column state, score work).
Referenced bases below are filled/read at runtime.

Graphics, animation frame 1 of 4 -- masks for Pogo (frames 2-4 are gfx_c26e/gfx_c2e6/gfx_c35e, each an independent block).
NOT a raw 1bpp raster -- these bytes only become real pixels after calc_sprite_shift/expand_sprite_b's self-modifying decode (see patch_expand_masks, $8E48). Reading this block directly as a bitmap produces garbage; a UDG array macro cannot render it.
Verified by emulation (decode_sprite.py, real Z80 core, self-mod masks forced to identity OR $00/AND $FF): decodes to a fixed 16 rows x 3 cells (24x16px), 96 source bytes consumed, invariant across all 4 shift values.

FC8A: Tape read byte
Used by the routine at tape_load_header.
Read one byte from tape with the custom turbo edge-timing loop (ULA EAR bit via IN ($FE), border feedback via OUT ($FE)).
Accumulates 8 bits CY=success, NC=load error/abort.

FC8B EX AF,AF'
FC8C DEC D
FC8D DI
FC8E EXX

FD20: Tape get edge
Used by the routine at tape_read_byte.
Measure one tape signal edge on the EAR line (IN A,($FE) with row $7F), toggling the reference in C times out via B.
Out: CY=edge seen.
Clobbers: A,B,C.
tape_get_edge FD20 JP $FD3B

FD24 RET Z
FD25 LD A,$7F

FD29 RRA
